 the moment be too long ; the power begin to fail , the sens grow more faint and confused at every heavy throb . little image and little question take the place of large one . in vain I look for even a cobweb , or the skeleton of a fly , where the dull light flicker through the pane of glass . in vain I listen for a mouse . even a rat   much as I hate he   would have be welcome then . the repeater be purposely make so low of tick , that I get no comfort thence . all be deep , unfathomable silence , except the sound of my rebel heart . 
 as a forlorn hope , I begin to reckon sixty slowly , as a child keep with a tick clock . 
 it would not do . my heart be beat louder than ever , and my hand be tremble ; even my tooth rattle like dice in a box as the time approach . 
 the nerve will not be hoodwink ; the mind can not swindle the body . I once slap the cheek of my governess . I can not treat nature so . try the sweet influence , and the honest coin of reason . it will not do . all tremble , I strike the repeater . five minute more , and the trial must come . my heart be flutter like a pigeon 's throat . the long suspense have be too much . oh ! why be I submit to this cruel ordeal ? the wall be thick . I can hear no movement in the secret room . 
 there come a creep , fingering , sound , as of one whose candle be out , grope for the door . it pass along the pane of glass , and a shadow be throw on the rafter . who can it be ? what stealthy hand but that of my father 's murderer ? 
 the word - the thought be enough . what resolution , reason , justice , all in turn , have fail to do , passion have do at once - passion at myself , as well as at my enemy . be it Clara Vaughan , who , for eight long year of demon 's reign , have breathe but for this moment - be Clara Vaughan to shake like the wooden - legged blackbird now her chance be come ? 
 a rush of triumph burn , like vitriol , through my vein . every nerve be brace , every sense alert and eager . against the light of that window , dull as it be , I could have thread the fine needle that ever be make . 
 I strike my repeater again . it be the hour , the minute , when my father die . with the mere spring of my instep I leap upon the stool . I could see it clearly now . I dip the broad camel's - hair brush in the flat phial , hold it carefully at arm 's length , and then draw it lightly along the pane , quite at the bottom , from corner to corner . one more dip , one more stripe above , a steam hover on the glass , and there be a gazing - place , clear as crystal , and wide enough to show most of the narrow room . of the room itself I take no heed ; the occupant be my study . 
 only four in all . one man at a high desk write rapidly ; three man sit round a small table , talk earnestly , and with much gesticulation , but the tone too low for I even to guess their language . from the appearance , manner , and action of the speaker , I feel sure that it be not English , and I think that it be not french . why , I can not say ; but my attention fix itself upon the man who be write at the top of the room . perhaps it be because I could see he well , for he stand with his face full towards I . 
 he be a man of middle age and stature , strongly frame , closely knit , and light of limb , with a handsome , keenly oval face , broad forehead , black eye , glance quickly and scornfully at his three comrade , long hair of an iron grey , fall on his shoulder , and toss back often with a jerk of the head . his hand be white and restless , quick as light in their motion . on the left thumb flash a large red jewel . though I could not see the paper , I know by the course of the quill that the writing be very small . but one minute I watch he , for the film be return upon the glass , and I must scan the other ; yet in that time he have write several line , half of they without look at the paper , but with his eye upon the other three . 
 I know he now he be in clear light , I could swear to he anywhere again . the last glance I could spare he send a shudder through I , for in his impatience he shift one foot from the shade of the desk . it be small , pointed , and elegant . 
 the film be thickening , like frost upon the pane , when I begin to observe the other . but I see enough to print their face on my memory , or those at least of two . the third I could not see so well . he seem old than the rest . all the man wear loose grey tunic , with a red sash over the left shoulder . I judge that the three be debate hotly , as to some measure , upon which the fourth have resolve . every now and then , they glance at he uneasily . 
 at he I gaze again , with deadly hatred , cold as ice , upon my heart . I feel my dagger handle . oh for one moment with he ! in my fury I forget the Inspector 's warning . the film be close over . I touch the glass with my lash . a flash of agony shoot through my eye . with a jerk I draw back , the stool rock under I , one foot of it strike the wall . I clutch the window sill , and throw my weight inward . down come the foot of the stool , loud as the bang of a door . 
 I think it be all over . how I stifle a scream I know not ; have it escape I , I should never have tell this story . I have the presence of mind to stand still , and watch , though my eye be madden I , what the cut - throat would do . through the agony , and the dimness , I could just see they all start , and rush to the door at the side of the room . the writer stand first , with his paper thrust anyhow into his bosom , a pistol in one hand , a poniard in the other . do I know the shape of it ? the other three be arm , but I could not see with what . they crouch behind a heavy screen , present   I suppose   their pistol muzzle at the door . find no attack ensue , they begin to search . now be the real danger to I . if they search that window before the size return , my life end there . fear be past . desperation seize I . if I be doom to blindness , just as well to death . but I clutch my dagger . 
 my left ear be against the wall . I hear a hand graze the partition inside , then a chair place under the embrasure , and a step upon it . I be still upon the stool , stoop close beneath the window frame . suddenly the light streak vanish , the size fly over it , as the breath fly over glass in the hard frost . the hand feel along the window frame , the dull shadow of a head flit upon the beam . it be within a foot of mine . the searcher pass on , without suspicion . 
 strange it be , but now the deadly peril be over , triple fear fall upon I . the heat fly back to my heart , just now so stanch and rigid ; my hair seem to creep with terror . dear life , like true love scorn , would have its way within I . quietly I slide down from the stool , and cower upon it , in a storm of trembling . my eyelid drop in agony , I could not lift they again , but blue and red light seem to dance within they . I have make up my mind to blindness ; but not , oh not just yet , to death . 
 how long I remain in this abject state , scorn myself , yet none the braver , be more than I can tell , or even care to ask . may it never be the lot of any , not even the base murderer ! wear out at last , in a lull of pain and terror , I fall into deep sleep , from which I be awaken by a hand upon my shoulder . 
 I try to look up , but could not . Sight be flee , and as I think for ever . but I feel that it be a friend . 
 " ah , I see how it be " - the voice be Inspector Cutting 's - " my poor child , there be now no danger . give I your hand : " he try to lift I , but I fall against the wall . 
 " take a sip of this , we must restore circulation . it be the cold as much as anything ; another sip , Miss Vaughan . " he use my true name on purpose ; it help to restore I . he be most humane and kind ; he do not even remind I of dutch courage . 
 CLARA VAUGHAN 
 BOOK III . 
 CHAPTER I. 
 in the morning I dream of Isola . across a broad black river , I see her lovely smile . Thick fog rise from the water , in which two swan be beat a dog , and by snatch only could I see my darling . she wave she little hand to I , and beg I , with that coax smile which bent cast iron and even gold , to come across to Isola . in vain I look for a boat , even in my dream I know that I could not swim , and if I could , the lead upon my eyelid would have sink I . so I call to she to come to I , and with that cry awake . 
 it be strike ten - my own little clock which my father give I . I count every stroke . what be Mrs. Shelfer do , that she have not call I yet ? what be I do , that I lay there so late ; for I always get up early ? and what be the sun about , that no light come into the room ? I know it be ten in the morning . 
 I feel all round . I be in my little bed , the splinter at the side of the head - board run into my finger as usual . there I be , and nowhere else . be it a tremendous fog ? if it be , they should have tell I , for they know that I like fog . at least they think so , from the interest I feel . 
 I grope for the little bell - pull , a sleezy worsted cord , which mean to break every time , but be not strong enough to do it . I jerk with all my strength , which seem very little somehow . what a pleasure ! the bell ring like a fire - peal . I fall back on the pillow , exhaust , but determine to have it out with Mrs. Shelfer . I put my hand up to arrange my hair , to look a little more like Clara Vaughan , when the light should enter , and to frighten Mrs. Shelfer . 
 there be something on my head . I never wear a night - cap ; my long black hair would scorn it . be I in a madhouse , be this put to keep I cool ? cold it be , and my brain so hot . all Wenham lake on dive , and he will only hiss . while I be pull at it , and find it stream wet , in come - I know her step - Mrs. Shelfer . but there be no light from the passage ! 
 " Mrs. Shelfer , what do you mean by this ? " 
 " by what , my dear good soul ? I have do all the bless thing I be tell to do for you . you might have put a ostrich feather or a marabout to my mouth , Miss Valence , and tuck I up , and a headstone , and none the wiser , when Uncle John bring you home last night . " 
 " I suppose I be dream . but I be sure I ring the bell . " 
 " Miss Valence , you do so , and no mistake . bless I ! I start in my shoe . a good job , Shelfer be not   home , he be so nervous . he would have go for gin straightway . now get up , that be a dear good soul , and when you have have some breakfast , we will talk over it , Miss Valence . let I see how your eye be . Uncle John say they be bad , and I be to keep they cover . I expect he here every minute . now turn they up to the light . what large eye you have , to be sure . bless I ! where be your long black lash ? " 
 " Mrs. Shelfer , there be some strange mistake . let the light into the room . " 
 I have rise in the bed , and her breath be on my forehead . 
 " Light , dear child , I can not   let more . the sun be on your face . " 
 I fall back upon my pillow , and could rise no more . the truth have be tingle through I , all the time she talk . I be stone - blind . I fling the bandage from I , and wish my heart would break . Mrs. Shelfer try some comfort . she seem to grieve for my eyelash , more than for my eye ; and address her comfort more to my look than sight . of course , I do not listen . when would the creature be go , and let I try to think ? 
 poor little thing ! I be very sorry ; what fault be it of her ? who and what be I , blind I , to find fault with any one who mean I well ? I drop my eyelid , I can feel they fall ; I lift they , I can feel they rise ; a full gaze , a side gaze , a half gaze ; with both eye , with one ; it be all the same ; gaze there may be , but no sight . henceforth I want no eyelid . 
 the sun be on my face . I can feel his winter ray , though my cheek be wet . what use be he to I ? 
 I have the dagger somewhere by which my father die . let I find it , if I can . 
 I could have swear that the box be in that corner carefully conceal . I strike against a washing - stand . ah , now I have it ; the box be lock , my key be in the top - drawer . I bear the box to the bed , and go grope for the chest of drawer . already I can tell by the sun - warmth on my face , which way I be go . surely , if I wait , I shall have the instinct of the blind . 
 what care I for that ? the coward love of life suggest that poor solace . now I have the key . quick unlock the box . 
 at length I throw the cover back . the weapon handle be to the right . I stoop to seize it . I grasp a square of colour . pretty instinct this ! I have get my large drawing box . 
 oh paint , my paint , so loved but yesterday , that ape the colour I shall never see , my hot tear make you water - colour indeed ! if God have rob my eye of sight , he have not dry my tear . 
 the gush flood relieve I . what right have I to die ? even without ask if my case be hopeless ! who know but what these lovely tint may glow for I again ? may I not once more intone the carmine damask of the rose , the gauzy green of April 's scarf ? soften scene before I rise . I lie my box of colour by , and creep into my bed for warmth . 
 presently the doctor come . Inspector Cutting have choose he , and choose well . from his voice I know that he be a gentleman , from his word and touch instinctively I feel that he understand the case . 
 when he have finish the examination he see I tremble for the answer which I dare not seek . 
 " young lady , I have hope , strong hope . it be quite impossible to say what course the inflammation may pursue . all depend on that . at present there be a film over the membrane , but the cornea be uninjured . perfect quiet , composure , so far as in such a case be possible , cold application , and the exclusion of light , be the simple remedy . all the rest must be leave to nature . avoid excitement of any kind . Diet as low as possible . do not admit your dear friend , unless they will keep perfect silence . even so , they be well away , unless you pine at loneliness . " 
 " oh no . I be quite accustomed to that . " 
 " that be well . I shall make a point of call daily , but shall not examine your eye every time . the excitement and the effort would strain the optic nerve . our object be to keep the inflammation from strike inward . I should not tell you all this , but I see that you have much self - command . on that and your constitution , under Providence , the cure depend . one question . I be not a profess ophthalmist , would you prefer to have one ? " 
 " oblige I with your opinion . " 
 " it be a delicate point for I . there be no operation to perform . it be a medical , not a surgical case . I have deal with such before . be you my own child I would call in no ophthalmist , but as you be a stranger to I , I wish you to decide for yourself . " 
 " then , I will have none . I have perfect confidence in you . " 
 he seem gratify , and take his leave . " please God , Miss Valence , you shall look I in the face ere long . " 
 CHAPTER II . 
 " composure be my only chance . " what chance have I of composure until I know the meaning of what I see last night ? blind though I be , one face be ever before I . no thickening of the membrane can exclude that face . Inspector Cutting be still below ; I will send for he at once . 
 Mrs. Shelfer remonstrate . " it will excite you so , my good friend . the doctor say perfect quiet . " 
 " just so . I can have none , until I have speak to your Uncle John . let he stay in my sitting - room , open the folding - door a little , and then , Mrs. Shelfer , please to go down stair . " 
 I hear the Inspector 's step , not so heavy this time . he ask how I be , and express his sorrow . I feel obliged to he for not remind I that the fault be all my own . then I implore he , if he wish I ever to see again , to tell I all he know about the man I see last night . 
 thus entreat , he can not refuse I , but first look up and down the stair , as I know by the sound of his step ; then he shut the door of the sitting - room . all he know be not very much . they be refugee , italian refugee ; two political and two criminal exile , leader now of a conspiracy to revolutionize their country . 
 " but why do he not arrest they ? ' 
 " simply because he have no right . as for the political refugee , of course , we never meddle with they ; as for the two criminal , they have not be demand by their government . wonderful now , be not   it ? the two fellow who have commit murder their government would not give sixpence for they ; but the two man who have only spout a little , it would give a thousand pound for either of they . he can not   understand such a system . " 
 and Inspector Cutting suck his lip - I know it by the sound - he always do it when he be in a puzzle . be a true Englishman , he know no more of serfdom , than of the dark half of the moon . I mean , of course , political serfdom . of social slavery we have enough to last ten generation more . 
 " would he be afraid to arrest they ? he say they be desperate man . " 
 " he should rather hope he would not . they have get their knife , and pistol , and all that humbug . but it be more show than fight . they be desperate man in a private quarrel , particular when they could come round a corner , and when woman be concern ; but as for show honest fight , he would soon come across three of they , than one good irish murderer . " 
 " what be his proof against my enemy ? I need not ask he which it be . " 
 the excitement of this question send needle through my eye . and I could not see he , to probe his pupil . 
 " well , his proof be very little . in fact it be no proof at all as yet . but he be not like a juryman . he be quite convince ; and his eye should never be off that man , until he have he under warrant , and the whole case clear . would that satisfy I ? " 
 he speak with such hearty professional pride , that I could not help believe he . but as for be satisfied - why should his evidence be a mystery to I ? " catch he at once , " be my idea ; but a hot and foolish one . " get up the evidence first , " be Inspector Cutting 's , " I can catch he at any time . " that be the whole gist of it . could he always catch he ? 
 he scorn the idea of there be any difficulty about it . the man could leave for no part of the continent ; he be a political refugee . America be his only bourne beyond the Inspector 's jurisdiction . and thither he could not try to go without the Police be down upon he at once . 
 by this time I be wear out , though my reason be not exhaust . in a word , I be only half satisfied , but I could not help myself . if , in my helpless blindness , I offend Inspector Cutting , the whole chance disappear . only one question remain . " why do he take I thither ? " 
 " for excellent reason . as to the one , it be most important that I should always know he again . moreover , it save my energy from waste . as to the other three , he have his own reason for require an intelligent witness about their proceeding . " 
 I think of the thousand pound , and say no more . Inspector Cutting be an Englishman , and proud , in his way , of english freedom . but , like nine - tenth of we , he think that we alone understand what freedom be . what good be it to such fellow as those ? they would only be free of one another 's throat . and like all of we , with most rare exception , next to freedom , he value money . for our love of this , many foreigner jeer we . all we can say be , that with we it be second , with they it be first . but we be of such staple , our second be strong than their first . 
 when the Inspector be go , I form a very sensible resolve . since there be nothing more to be do or learn at present , my only care should be the recovery of my sight . if I be to be blind till death , the purpose of my life be lose , and I might as well die at once . but now the first blind agony , the sudden shock , be over ; and I have too much of what the Inspector denominate " pluck , " to knock under so . 
 in the afternoon , when all be quiet , lovely Isola come . strict order have be give that no one should be admit . but Mrs. Shelfer be not proof against the wile of Isola . 
 " she smile so bootiful , when I open the door , Miss , it fetch out all my hair pin ; and when I tell she you be ill in bed , and strike stone blind along of some chemical stuff , two big tear come out of her long blue eye , same as the wet out of a pennorth of violet , Miss ; and as for stop she , she throw her muff at I , and tell I to stop that if I like , and to run and tell you that she be come , quick , quick ! 
 " to be sure , and here I be ! " cry the cheery voice I love so well . " oh , Clara dear , dear Clara ! " the little darling fling her soft warm arm around I , utterly forgetful of her dress , forgetful of all the world , but that little bit of it she hold . her delicious breath come over my fevere cheek , her cool satin flesh be on my burn eyelid . what lotion could be compare to this ? how long she stay , I can not tell ; I only know that while I hear her voice , and feel her touch , blindness seem no loss to I . she pronounce herself head nurse ; and as for doctor , what be they , compare to her own father ? if she could coax he , he should come next day , and deliver his opinion , and then the doctor might betake himself to thing he understand , if indeed he understand anything , which she do not believe he do , because he have say she be not to come . my drawing too she admire , much more than they deserve , and her brother Conrad must come and see they , he be so fond of drawing , and there be nothing he could not do . she be so sorry she must go now , but old Cora must be tired of patroling , and she herself have a lecture to attend upon the chemical affinity of body . what it mean she have no idea , but that would not matter the least ; some of the clever girl say they do , but she would not believe they ; it take a man , she be sure , to understand such subject . she would bring her work the next day , such as it be , and the nice bit of sponge that be ever see , it could not be buy in London ; and she would answer for it I should be able to paint her likeness in a week ; and she would not go till it be dark ; and then the Professor should come for she when his lecture be over , and examine I ; he know all about optic , and retina , and pencil of light , and refraction and aberration , and she could not remember any more name ; but she feel quite certain this be a case of optical delusion , and nothing else . 
 how I wish I could have see she , when she pronounce this opinion , with no little solemnity . she must have look such a sage ! the thought of that make I laugh , as well as the absurdity of the idea . but I only ask how the Professor be to examine my eye , if he do not come till dark . 
 to be sure ! she never think of that . what a little goose she be ! but she would make he come in the morning , before his work begin ; and then old Cora would fetch her home to tea . and she have very great hope , that if she could only persuade her papa to deliver a lecture in my room , it would have such an effect on my optic nerve , that they would come all right directly , at any rate I should know how to treat they . 
 delight with this idea , she kiss I , and hug I , and off she run , after tell I to be sure to keep my spirit up , and the bandage not too tight . 
 the latter injunction be much easy to obey than the former . she have enliven I wonderfully , as well as nurse I most delicately ; but now that she be go , the usual reaction commence . moreover , although as the saying be , the sight of she would have be good for sore eye , the effort at see she , which I could not control , when she be present , be , I already feel , anything but good for they . and the loss , when she be go , be like a second loss of light . 
 light ! what million thought flash through I at that little word ! swift thing the mind have meet , too like itself to understand . be it stee or wing of mind ? Nay , not swift enough for that . be it then the food of life , prepare betime ere life appear , the food the blind receive but can not taste ? if so , far well to be blind from birth . well I know the taste from memory ; shall I never taste it else ? have beauty lose its way to I ? the many golden fold of air , the lustrous dance of sunny morn , the soft reclining of the moon , the grand perspective of the star   long avenue to God 's own home , be these all blank to I , and night make one with day ? 
 oh God , whose first approach be light , replenisher of sun and star , whence dart anew thy gushing flood   solid or liquid we know not , whose subtle volume have no bourne or track ; light , the dayside half of life , leaping , flash , beaming ; glistening , twinkle , steal ; light ! oh God , if live I must , grudge I not a ray ! 
 CHAPTER III . 
 low fever follow the long prostration to which the fear of outer darkness have reduce my jade nerve . this fever probably redeem my sight , by generalize the local inflammation , to which object the doctor 's effort have be direct . toss on my weary bed , without a glimpse of anything , how I long for the soft caress and cool lip of Isola ! but since that one visit , she have be sternly exclude . the Professor have no chance of deliver his therapeutic lecture . in fact he do not come . " once for all , " say Dr. Franks , when he hear of that proposal , " choose , Miss Valence , between my service , and the maundering of some pansophist . if you prefer the former , I will do my utmost , and can almost promise you success ; but I must and will be obey . none shall enter your room , except Mrs. Shelfer and myself . as to your lovely friend , of whom Mrs. Shelfer be so full , if she truly love you , she will keep away . she have do you already more harm than I can undo in a week . I be deeply interested in this case , and feel for you sincerely ; but unless you promise I to see - I mean to receive - no one without my permission , I will come no more . " 
 it sound very hard , but I feel that he be right . 
 " no crying , my dear child , no crying ! dear I , I have hear so much of your courage . too much inflammation already . whatever you do , you must not cry . that be one reason why I will not have your friend here . when two young lady get together in trouble , I know by my own daughter what they do . you may laugh as much as you like , in a quiet way ; and I be sure Mrs. Shelfer can make any one laugh , under almost any circumstance . can not   you now ? " 
 " to be sure , my good friend , I have see such a many rogue . that is , when I know Charley 's a - come home . " 
 " now good bye , Miss Valence . but I would recommend you not to play with your paint so . there be an effluvium from they . " 
 " oh , what can I do , what be I to do to pass the endless night ? I be only try to build a house in the dark . " 
 " sleep as much as you can . I be give you gentle opiate . when you can sleep no long , let Mrs. Shelfer talk or read to you , and have a little music . I will lend you my musical box , which play twenty - four tune : have it in the next room , not to be too loud . and then play on the musical glass , not too long at a time : you will soon find out how to do that in the dark . " 
 he most kindly send we both the box that very day ; and many a weary hour they lighten of its load . poor Isola come every day to inquire , and several time she have her brother with she . she make an entire conquest of Mrs. Shelfer , who even give she a choice canary bird . I be never tired of hear the little woman 's description of her beauty , and her visit to the kitchen form the chief event of the day . Mrs. Shelfer   who have irish blood in her vein   use to declare that the ground be not good enough for they to walk on . 
 " such a pair , Miss ! to see she so light , and soft , and loving , trip along , and such eye and such fur ; and he walk so straight , and brave , and noble . I be sure you would go a mile , Miss , to see he walk . " 
 " you forget , Mrs. Shelfer , I may never enjoy that pleasure . " 
 " no , no . quite true , my good friend . but then we may , all the same . " 
 exactly so . there lie all the difference to I , but none to any other . this set I moralise in my shallow way , a thing by no mean natural to I , who be so concentrated and subjective . but loss of sight have do I good , have turn the mind 's eye inward into the darkness of myself . I think the blind , as a general rule , be less narrow - minded than those endow with sight . less inclined , I mean , to judge their neighbour harshly , less arrogant in exact that every pulse keep time with their own . if eye be but the chink through which we focus on our brain censoriousness and bigotry , if ray of light be shafts and lance of ill will ; then well be it to have no crystalline len . far well to be blind , than print the world - distort puppet of myself . I , that small speck of dust , blow upon the shore of time , blow off when my puff shall come ; a speck ignore by moon and star ; too small   however my ambition leap   for earth to itch , whate'er I suck ; and yet a speck that be a mountain in the telescope of God ; shall I never learn that his be my only magnitude ; shall I wriggle to be all in all to my own corpuscle ? 
 CHAPTER IV . 
 be there any Mocha stone , fortification agate , or Scotch pebble , with half the vein and mottling , angle , flux and reflux , that chequer one minute of the human mind ? be ever machine invent to throw so many shuttle ? 
 at present I be gauge for little thread of thought - two minute since , the small thing I could think of be myself . now it be the large . must I grope from room to room , shall I never be sure where the table be , where my teacup stand ; never read , or write , or draw ; never tell when my hand be clean , except by smell soap ; never know   though small the difference   how my dress become I , or when my hair be right ; never see my own sad face , in which I have be fool enough to glory , never - and this be bad of all - never catch another 's smile ? 
 here be I , a full - grow girl , full of maiden 's thought and wondering , know well that I be shape so but to be a link in life ; must I never think of love or of be love , except with love like Isola 's ; sweet affection , very sweet ; but white sugar only ? 
 when my work be over , and my object gain , when my father 's spirit know the wrong redeemed , as a child I use to think I would lay I down and die . but since I come to woman 's fulness , since I cease to look at man and they begin to look at I , some soft change , I know not what , have come across my dream . 
 be my purpose alter ? be my tenor break ? not a whit of either . rather be they strong set and well lead , as my heart and brain enlarge . yet I see beyond it all , a thing I never use to see , a glow above the peak of hate , a possibility of home . " saw " I should have say , for now what have I to do with see ? 
 on the fourteenth morning , I have give up all hope . they tell I it be bright and sunny ; for I always ask about the weather , and feel most cruelly depress upon a sunny day . by this time I have learn to dress without Mrs. Shelfer 's aid . still , from force of habit I go to the glass to do my hair , and still draw back , as far as be allow , the window curtain . 
 off with my wet bandage , I be sick of it ; let I try no long to delude myself . 
 suddenly a gleam of light , I be sure of it ; faint indeed , and like a will of the Wisp ; but I be quite sure it be a gleam of light . I go near the window and try again . no , there be no more for the present , it be the sudden change produce it . never mind ; I know what I have see , a thing that come and cheat I in dream ; this time it have not cheat I ; it be a genuine twinkle of the sun . 
 I can do nothing more . I can not put another stitch upon I . I be thrill with the sun , like Memnon . I fall upon my knee , and thank the Father of light . 
 when the doctor come that day , and look into my eye , he see a decide change . 
 " Miss Valence , the crisis be over . with all my heart I congratulate you . another fortnight , and you will see well than ever . " 
 I laugh , and weep , and , blind as I be , could hardly keep from dancing . then I want to kiss the Doctor , but hear Mrs. Shelfer 's step , make a reckless jump and have it out upon she . 
 " bless I , why bless I , my good soul , if I be a young gentleman now - " 
 " why , Miss Valence , I be perfectly astonish , " say Doctor Franks , but I know he be laugh ; " if I have be request , only two minute ago , to pick out the most self - possess , equable , and courageous young lady in London , I should have say , ' I do not   want any look , I know where to find she , ' but now , upon my word - " 
 " if you be ask to point out the most delighted , grateful , and happy girl in London , you know where to come for she . let I kiss you , Dr. Franks , only once . I will not   rob your daughter . it be to you I owe it all . " 
 " no , to Providence , and yourself , and an uncommonly good conjunctiva . now be prudent , my dear child ; a little ecstasy must be forgive ; but do not   imperil your cure by over - excitement . it be , as I hop it would be , a case of epiphytic sloughing "   I think that be what he say , " and it may become chronic if precipitate . the long and more thorough the process , the less chance of recurrence . " 
 " oh I be satisfied with one eye , or half an eye . can you promise I that ? " 
 " if you will only follow my direction , I can promise you both eye , more brilliant than ever ; and Mrs. Shelfer say they be wonderfully bright . but what I order must be do . slow and sure . " 
 he give I short direction , all upon the same principle , that of graduation . 
 " and now , Miss Valence , good - bye . henceforth I visit you only as a friend ; in which I know you will indulge I , from the interest I feel in the case , and in yourself . Mrs. Shelfer 's wonderful young lady may be admit on Thursday ; but do not   let she look at your eye . girl be always inquisitive . if there be any young gentleman , lucky enough to explain your strange anxiety to see , you will make short work of he , when your sight return . your eye will be the most brilliant in London ; which be say a great deal . but I fear he will hardly know you , till your lash grow ; and all your face and expression be alter for the time . " 
 " one thing will never alter , though it can find no expression , my gratitude to you . " 
 " that be very pretty of you , my dear child . you kiss I just now . now let I kiss you . " 
 he touch my forehead and be go . he be the first true gentleman I have meet with , since the loss of Farmer Huxtable . 
 CHAPTER V. 
 when Isola come on the Thursday , and I obtain some little glimpse of she , she express her joy in a thousand natural way , well worth feel and seeing , not at all worth tell . I love she for they more and more . I never meet a girl so warm of heart . many woman can sulk for day ; most woman can sulk for an hour ; I believe that no provocation could have make Isola sulky for two minute . she try sometimes   at least she say so , but it be no good . 
 and yet she feel as keenly as any of the very sulky woman can do ; but she have too much warmth of heart and imagination to live in the fold of that cold - blooded snake . neither have she the strong selfishness , on which that serpent feed . 
 in the afternoon , as we still sit together , in rush Mrs. Shelfer with her bonnet on , quite out of breath , and without her usual ceremony of knock at the door . I could not think where she have be all the day ; and she have make the great mystery of it in the morning , and want to have it notice . up she run to I now , and push Isola out of the way . 
 " got them at last , Miss. get them at last , and no mistake . no more Dr. Franks , nor bandage , nor curtain down , nor nothing . save a deal of trouble and do it in no time . but what a job I have to get they to be sure ; if the cook 's mate have not   know Charley , they would not have let I have them , after go all the way to Wapping . " she hold up something in triumph . 
 " what be it , Mrs. Shelfer ? I be sorry to say I can not see . " 
 " and right down glad of it , I be , my good friend . yes , yes . or I should have have all my journey for nothing . but Miss Idols know , I will be bind she do , or it be no good go to College . " 
 " let I look at it first , " say Isola , " we learn almost everything at college , Mrs. Shelfer ; but even we senior sophist do not   know every thing without see it yet . " 
 " then put your pretty eye on they , Miss Idols ; I will be bind it will make they caper . I never see such fine one , nor the cook 's mate either . why they be as big as young whelk . " 
 " Mollusca , or Crustacea , or something ! " exclaim Isola , with more pride than accuracy , " what queer little thing . I must take they to my papa . " 
 " now , young lady , " cry Mrs. Shelfer in her grandest style , " I see I must explain they to you after all . they be the blessed shell the poor sailor put in their eye to scour they out , and keep they bright , and make they see in the dark against the wind . only see how they crawl . there now , Miss Valence , I will pick you out two big lively fellow , and pop one for you in the corner of each eye ; the cook 's mate show I how to lift your eyelid . " 
 " how kind of he , to be sure ! ' 
 " and it will crawl about under the lid , you must not mind its hurt a bit ; and it will not   come out till to - morrow when the clock strike twelve , and then it will have eat up every bit , and your eye will be bright than diamond . Charley have see they do it ever so many time , and he say it be bootiful , and they do not   mind give five shilling a piece for they , when they be scarce . " 
 " do Mr. Shelfer ever try they ? his eye be so sharp : perhaps that be the reason . " 
 " no . I never hear that he do , Miss. but bless you he never tell I half he do ; no , nor a quarter of half . " at this recollection , she fetch a little short sigh , her near approach to melancholy , for she be not sentimental . " Care kill the cat , " be her favourite aphorism . 
 " then when he come home , Mrs. Shelfer , pop one of these shell , a good big one , into each of his eye ; and let we know the effect to - morrow morning , and I will give you a kiss , if you do it well . " 
 this be the bribe Isola find most potent with everybody . 
 " Lor , Miss Idols , bless your innocent heart , do you suppose he would let I ? why he think it a great thing to let I tie his shoe , and he will not   only when he have have a good dinner . " 
 " well , " cry Isola , " I be astonish ! catch I tie my husband 's shoe ! I shall expect he to tie mine , I know ; and he shall only do that when he be very good . " 
 with a regal air , she put out the prettiest foot ever see . Mrs. Shelfer laugh . 
 " Lor , Miss , it be all very well for girl to talk ; and they all do it , till they know well . though for the like of you , any one would do anything a most . pray , Miss Idols , if I may make so bold , how many offer of marriage have you receive ? " 
 " let I think ! oh I know ! it be one more than I be year old . eighteen altogether , Mrs. Shelfer ; if you count the apothecary 's boy , and the nephew of the library ; but then they be all of they boy , papa 's pupil and that , a deal too young for I . they be all go to die , when I refuse they ; but they be all alive so far , at any rate . be not   it too bad of they ? " 
 " well , Miss Idols , if you get as good a husband as you deserve , and that be say a deal , he will tie your shoe may be for a month , and then he will look for you to tie his . " 
 " and long he may look , even if he have shellfish in his eye . why look , Mrs. Shelfer , they be all crawl about ! " 
 " bootiful , be not   it ? bootiful ! I wish Miss Valence could see they . and look at the horn they go rout about with ! how they must tickle your eyelid . and what coorious eye they have ! ah , I often think , Miss Idols , I like this sort of thing so much , what a pity it be as I be not   bear in the country . I should never be tired of watch the snail , and the earywig , and the tadpole . why , I like nothing well than to see they stump - legge thing come to table in the cabbage . I have not see one now for ever so long . oh that Charley , what dreadful lie he do tell ! " 
 " what about , Mrs. Shelfer ? " 
 " why , my good friend , he say they green thing with stripe on , and ever so many leg , turn to live butterfly , after they be dead . but I be too many for he there . yes , yes . the last one as I boil , I do not say a word about it to he , but I put it by in a chiney - teacup , with the saucer over , in case it should fly away . bless your heart , young lady , there it be now , as quiet as anything , and no sign of a butterfly . and when he tell I any lie , about where he be last night , I just go to the cupboard , and show he that ; and never another word can he say . and so , Miss Valence , you will not   try these little snail , after my journey and all ! " 
 " of course I will not , Mrs. Shelfer . but I be sincerely oblige to you for your trouble , as well as for all your kind nursing , which I can never forget . now let I buy those shellfish from you , and Miss Isola will take they as a present to her papa . " 
 " no , no , unless he will put they in his eye , Miss. I will not   have they waste . Charley will sell they again in no time . he know lot of sailor . most likely he will get up a raffle for they , and win they himself . " 
 away she hurry to take off the bonnet she have be so proud of , for the last two and twenty year . though I decline the service of the ophthalmist snail , my sight return very rapidly . how delicious it be to see more and more every day ! plenty of cold water be the present regimen . Vision be less a vision , every time I use it . in a week more , I can see quite well , though oblige to wear a shade . 
 one morning , dear Isola run upstairs , out of breath as usual ; but , what be most unusual , actually frown . have Cora tyrannise , or what ? through the very shade of her frown , come her sunny smile , as she kiss I . 
 " oh , I be so vexed . I have bring he to the door ; and now he will not   come in ! " 
 " who , my darling ? " 
 " why , Conny , to be sure . my brother Conrad . I have set my heart on show he to you , directly you could see . " 
 " why will not   he come in ? " 
 " because he think that you ought not to see stranger , until you be quite well . he have not get to the corner yet . I can run like a deer . send word by I , that you be die to see he . " 
 " not quite that . but say how glad I shall be . " 
 " I will say that you will not   get well till you do . " 
 " say what you like . he will know it be only your nonsense . " 
 off she dart ; she be quick as light in her movement , and soon return with her brother . 
 I lift my weak eye to his bright one , and recognise at once the preserver of my mother and myself . but I see , in a moment , that he have not the faint remembrance of I . my whole face be alter by my accident , and even my voice affect by the long confinement . when he meet I in the wood , he seem very anxious not to look at I ; when he save my life from the rush mountain , he have little opportunity . very likely he would not have know I , under another name ; even without this illness . so let it be . I will not reveal myself . I thank he once , and he repulse I ; no doubt he have a reason , for I see that he be a gentleman . let that reason hold good : I will not trespass on it . 
 he take my hand with a smile , the counterpart of Isola 's . he have hear of I so constantly , that I must excuse the liberty . a dear friend of his sister 's could be no stranger to he . a thrill shot through I at the touch of his hand , and my eye be weak . he see it , and place a chair for I far from the light . on his own face , not the sun , for the " drawing - room " window look north , but the strong reflection of the noon - day light be fall . 
 how like he be to Isola , and yet how different ! so much strong , and bold , and more decide , so tall and firm of step . his countenance open as the noon , incapable of concealment ; yet if he be the same   and , how can I doubt it ? , then at least there seem to be some mystery about he . 
 Isola , with the quickness of a girl , see how intently I observe he , and could not hide her delight . 
 " there now , Clara dear , I know you would like he . but you must not look at he so much , or your poor eye will be sore . " 
 little stupid ! as I feel my pale cheek colour , I could almost have be angry , even with my Isola . but she mean no harm . in spite of lecture and " college , " she be gentle nature personify ; and no professor could make anything else of she . all these thing run in the grain . if there be anything I hate , I be sure I hate affectation . but there be a difference between we . 
 probably it be this : I be of pure English blood , and she be not . that I know by instinct . what blood she be of , I be sure I can not tell . gentle blood at any rate , or I could not have love she so . how horribly narrow - minded , after all my objectivity ! well , what I mean be , that I can like and love many people who be not of gentle , but   I suppose   of ferocious blood ; still , as a general rule , culture and elegance be well match for nature , after some generation of training . my father use to say so about his pointer and setter . the marvel be that I , who belong to this old streak , seem to have get some twist in it . my grandmother would have swoon at the name of some people I love more than I could have love she . my mother would not . but then she be a Christian . probably that be the secret of my twist . 
 all this have pass through my mind , before I can frown at Isola . and now I can not frown at all . dear little thing , she be not eighteen , and she know no well . I have attain that Englishwoman 's majority three week ago ; and I be sorry for Isola . 
 to break the awkwardness , her brother start off into subject of art . he have hear of my drawing , may he see they some day ? I ask he about the magnificent stag . yes , that be his , and I have no idea how long it take he to do . he speak of it with no conceit whatever ; neither with any depreciation , for the purpose of tempting praise . as he speak , I observe some peculiarity in his accent . Isola 's accent be as pure as mine , or purer . her brother speak very good English , and never hesitate for a word ; but the form of his sentence often be not english ; especially when he warm to his subject ; and   what strike I first , for I be no purist as to collocation of word   his accent , his emphasis be not native . the difference be very slight , and quite indescribable ; but a difference there be . perhaps it be rather a difference of the order of thought than of language , as regard the cast of the sentence ; but that will not account for the accent ; and if it would , it still show another nationality . 
 there be a loud knock at the door . I be just prepare   with Isola 's help   my little hospitality . if London visit mean much talk and no food , I hold by Gloucestershire and Devon . I have a famous North Devon ham , and be proud of its fame . surely no more visitor for I . 
 no ; but one for Mrs. Shelfer . the Professor have hear of the eyeshell ; and what politeness , humanity , love of his daughter fail to do , science have effect . he be come to see and secure they . his child hear his voice . of course , we must ask he to come up . Mr. Conrad rise . Isola run to fetch her father . Isola love everybody . I do believe she love old Cora . Conrad be of sterner stuff : but surely he love his father . as for I - we be just get on so well - I want no Professor . Isola 's brother will not tell a lie . he do not remember , all at once , any press engagement . he hold out his hand , say simply , 
 " Miss Valence , I heartily beg your pardon for leave so suddenly ; and just when we be give you so much trouble . it would be impertinence for I to tell you the reason . it be a domestic matter . I trust you will believe I , that no light reason would make I rude . may I come again with Isola , to see your drawing soon ? " 
 he meet the Professor on the stair . the latter enter the room , under evil auspex for my good opinion . 
 CHAPTER VI . 
 if Professor Ross enter my room under evil auspex , it be not long before he send the bird the other way . for the first time , since my childhood , I meet a man of large and various knowledge ; a man who have spend his life in amass information , and learn how to make the most of it . a little too much perhaps there be of the second , and more fruitful branch , of the sour - sweet tree . once I have be fool enough to fancy that some of my own little bopeep at nature be original and peculiar . to Thomas Kenwood , Farmer Huxtable , and even Mr. Shelfer , a gardener , I have be quite an oracle as to the weather , the sky , and the insect about . moreover , in most of the book I have read , there be such blunder , even in matter that lie on nature 's doorstep , that , look back at they , I think I have cross her threshold . 
 as the proverb have it , nature always avenge herself ; and here be I , a mere " gappermouth "   I use a devonshire word , to be teach that I have not yet crop even a cud to chew . true , I do not expect   like Mr. and Mrs. Shelfer   that a boil caterpillar would become a live butterfly ; neither do I believe , with Farmer Huxtable , that hip and haw foretell a hard winter , because God mean they for the thrush ; but I know no more than they do the law and principle of thing . my little knowledge be all shred and patch . it do not cover even the small subject . odd thing here and there I know ; but a person of sound information know the odd and the even as well . my observation might truly be call my own ; but instead of be peculiar to I , nearly all of they have be anticipate century ago . I be but a gipsey straying where an army have be . 
 all this I suspect in less than ten minute from the Professor 's entrance ; he do not leave I long in doubt about it . it be just to myself to say that the discovery do not mortify I much . my little observation have be make , partly from pure love of nature 's doing , partly through habit draw from a dark spring . at first I have feel no pleasure in they , but it could not long be so . now they be mine as much as ever , though a thousand share they with I . 
 as the Professor lay bare my ignorance and my error , and prove that the little I do know be at second hand - which it certainly be not - I attempt no reply ; I be too young for argument , and too much interested to be impatient . so he demolish my ham and myself , with equal relish and equal elegance of handle . he seem to have no intention of do either , but manage both incidentally , and almost accidentally , while he open his mental encyclopadia . 
 at length , Isola , who be tired of lecture , such as she get and forget every day , feel that it be high time to assert her prerogative , and come to my rescue . 
 " come , Pappy , you fancy you know everything , do not   you ? " 
 he be just begin to treat of moss ; and I know that he be wrong upon several point , but do not dare to say so . 
 " my dear child , of the million thing I never shall discover , one be the way to keep you at all in order . " 
 " I should hope not , indeed . come now , here be another thing you do not   know . how long do it take to boil this delicious ham ? Clara know , and so do I. " 
 " upon that matter , I confess my total ignorance . " 
 " hear , hear ! Pappy , you can lecture by the hour upon isothermic law , and fluid , and fibrine , and adipose deposit , and you can not   tell how long it take to set this delicate fat . I will tell you what it be , Pappy , if you ever snub I in lecture again before the junior sophist , as you dare to do yesterday , I will sing out , ' ham , Pappy , ham ! ' and you will see how the girl will laugh . " 
 " no novelty , my dear , for they to laugh at you . I fear you never will learn anything but impertinence . " 
 his word be light , and he strove to keep his manner the same ; but his eye belie he . 
 Isola run round , and administer she never - fail remedy . there be that sweetness about her nobody could resist it . return to her seat , she give I a nod of triumph , and begin again . 
 " now , Papples , when you be good again , you shall have a real treat . Clara will show you her cordetto , will not   you , dear ? it be twice as big as your , and more than twice as pretty . " 
 I take it from my neck , where it have be throughout my illness . Isola tell I continually that it have save my sight ; and so old Cora devoutly believe , cross herself , and invoke fifty saint . long afterwards I find that Cora know it to be the heart of the Blessed Virgin , perpetuate in the material which her husband use . if so , it have be multiply as well . 
 Dr. Ross take my pretty gordit , and examine it narrowly , carry it to the window to get a strong light . 
 " beyond a doubt , " he say at last , " it be the fine in Europe . I have only see one to compare with it , and that have a flaw in the centre . will you part with it , Miss Valence ? " 
 " no ; I have promise never to do that . " 
 " then I must say no more ; but I should have be proud to add it to my collection . " 
 " to carry it about with you , you mean , pappy . you know you be a superstitious old Pappy , in spite of all your learning . " 
 Weak as my eye be , I could see the scowl of deep displeasure in his . Isola be frightened : she know she have go too far . she do not even dare to offer the kiss of peace . no more be say about it , and I turn the conversation to some other subject . but when he rise to depart , I find a pretext for keep Isola with I . 
 " good - bye for the present , Miss Valence , " Dr. Ross say gracefully - he do everything but scowl with an inborn grace - " I hope that your very first journey in quest of natural history will terminate at my house . I can not show you much , but shall truly enjoy go over my little collection with you whenever you find that your sight be strong enough . meanwhile , let I earnestly warn you to abstain from chemical experiment " - this be the cause of my injury assign by Mrs. Shelfer - " until you have a competent director . Isola , good - bye . I will send Cora for you in good time for tea . your attendance at lecture will be excuse . " 
 all my interest in the subject he have discuss , and in his mode of treat they , all my admiration of his shrewd intellectual face , do not prevent my feeling it a relief when he be go . he be not at all like his child . about they there be something so win and unpretentious , few could help like they at first sight . they do all they could to please , but without any visible effort . but with the Professor , in spite of all his elegance and politeness , I could not help perceive that he be not do his good , that he scorn to put forth his power when there be neither antagonist nor   in his opinion   duly qualified listener . nevertheless I could have tell he some thing he do not know concern lichen and moss . 
 when I be leave with my favourite Isola , that gentle senior sophist seem by no mean disconsolate at her papa 's departure . she love he and be proud of he , but there be time , as she tell I , when she be quite afraid of he . 
 " would you believe it , dear , that I could be afraid of old Pappy ? " - his age be about four and forty - " it be very wicked I know , but how be I to help it ? be you like that with your papa , when he be alive ? " 
 " no , I should think not . but I be not at all sure that he be not   afraid of I . " 
 " oh , how nice that must be ! but it be my fault , be not   it ? " 
 I could not well have tell she , even if I have know it , that the fault in such case be almost always on the parent 's side . 
 CHAPTER VII . 
 that same evening , when dear " Idols " be go , and I feel trebly alone , Mrs. Shelfer come to say that her uncle John be there , and would be glad to see I . though he have be several time to ask how I be , he have not see I since the first day of my blindness . 
 after express his joy and surprise at my recovery , he assure I that I must thank neither myself nor the doctor , but my luck in not have touch the liquid until its strength be nearly expended . 
 " have you any news for I ? " I ask abruptly . as my strength return , the sense of my wrong grow hotter . 
 " yes ; and I fear you will think it bad news . you will lose my help for awhile in your pursuit . " 
 " how so ? you talk of my luck ; I be always unlucky . " 
 " because I be order abroad on a matter too nice and difficult for any of my colleague . to - morrow I leave England . " 
 " how long shall you be away ? " 
 " I can not tell . perhaps one year ; perhaps two . perhaps I may never return . over and above the danger , I be not so young as I be . " 
 I feel dismayed , and stricken down . be I never to have a chance ? all power of earth and heaven and hell seem to combine against I . then come a gleam of hope , obscure immediately by the remembrance of his word . 
 " be you go to Italy ? " 
 " no . to Australia . " 
 thereupon all hope vanish , and for a time I could not say a word . at last I say - 
 " Inspector Cutting , the least thing you can do before you go , and your absolute duty now , be to tell I every single thing you find out , in the course of your recent search . something you must have learn , or you would not have do what you do . all along I have feel that you be hide something from I . now you can have no motive . now I be your successor in the secret ; I , and no one else . to no other will I commit the case . how much I have suffer from your secresy , none but myself can know . henceforth I will have no help . three month you have be on the track , and I almost believe that you have discover nothing . " 
 I speak so , partly through passion , partly in hope to taunt he into disclosure . his chief weakness , as I know well , be pride in his own sagacity . 
 " you shall suffer no more . I have good reason for hide it , one of they your own hastiness . now I will tell you all I know . in fact , as you well say , it have become my duty to do so , unless you will authorise I to appoint a successor before I go . " 
 " certainly not . my confidence in you can not be transfer to a stranger . " 
 " one chance more . let I report the matter officially . it be possible that my superior may think it more important than my new mission , which be to recover a large amount of property . " 
 " no . I will not allow it . I have devote myself to one object . I alone can effect it . it shall not pass to other . I feel once more that it be my destiny to unravel this black mystery ; myself , by my own courage . in ask your aid I be thwart my destiny . since then I have have nothing but accident . there be a proverb in some language , ' who cross destiny shall have accident . ' " 
 " Miss Valence , I could never have dream that you be so superstitious . " 
 " now tell I all you have do , all you have discover , and your own conclusion from it . " 
 he tell I all in a very few word , and his conclusion be mine . to any other except myself , the ground on which he have base it , would have seem insufficient . I take good care to secure every possible mean of follow up the frail clue . Ere he wish I good - bye , he offer one last suggestion . " if , during my absence , Miss Valence , you press your evidence far enough to require the strong hand , or if before you have do so you require a man 's assistance , apply at once to my son - you can always find he through Patty Shelfer . he be only a serjeant as yet , and not in the detective force ; but he have quality , that young man have , he have get all my ability , and more ! ah , he will be at the top of the tree when I be in my grave , please God . " 
 his shrewd eye soften as he speak , and I like he ten time as well for this little flaw in his sheathing . of course he know that I could not entrust myself to a young man , as I could to he . when he be go , with many good wish on both side , and a little keepsake from I , I feel that I have lose an intelligent , honest , and true friend . 
 CHAPTER VIII . 
 vigorous and elastic as I be , I can not deny that the air and weather have great dominion over I . it be always so with my own dear father . two day spend indoor , without any real exercise , would make he feel as uneasy as a plant in a cellaret . Crusty and crab , nothing could ever make he - not even gout I believe , if he have live long enough for it - but when he have lose his fishing , or shooting , or bit of gardening , too long , he be quite unlike himself . it be a bad time then to coax for anything - no song , no whistling , no after - dinner nap . 
 I too be not of a sedentary nature , though upon due occasion I can sit writing or draw for some hour together . but how fine a thing all the while to see any motion outside - a leaf that can skip , or a cloud that can run ! how we envy a sparrow his little hop , even across the gutter . it be now a long month since I have be out of door , except just to sniff the air , without any bonnet on . I have never be box and pannelle so long since first I crawl out of my cradle . it be a sharp bright frost - it seem to freeze hard in London than in the west of Gloucestershire , but not half so cleanly . 
 Isola come , like a tea - china rise bed in poplin and ermine . her close - draw bonnet of velvet , mazarin blue , be freak with snowdrop , nod , nod , nodding , not too many of they . I hail the omen of spring , and my spirit rise already . idol be up for a lark   as the junior sophist express it   and she have set her heart upon lead I such a dance . shall she ever set that sweet heart upon anything , and not obtain it at once ? who know ? never , I be quite sure , when another heart be the object . 
 " come , you grave old Grandmother . you be young than I , I believe , in spite of all your story ; and you be old enough in your way , for old mother Hubbard that live in a cupboard . oh my tippet and furbelow , if I wear as tall as you , and half as long in the waist , what a dress I would have . fifteen guineas at least . come along , you bed - ride dump of a Clara ; it be freeze like brick and silica , and I be in such spirit , and Giudice be frightening Tom out of his life in the kitchen . " 
 she dance round my little room , like a leaf when the wind be rise . the pixie - king of my gordit could not have be light of foot , nor half so lovely of form . how she manage to spin so between the ' stick , " none but herself can tell . what would poor Mrs. Shelfer have say ? in spite of her fear for the furniture , she would have laugh , I believe , and bless the pretty foot . 
 " come along , Clara child . do you think I be go to stand still here all day ? " 
 " if you call that stand still , pray give I the senior sophist 's definition of motion . " 
 " oh I want to skate , so dreadfully . and Pappy and Conrad will not   let I . they say it be not   become . but what on earth can be more so ? would not   I skim on one foot ? I will skate , in spite of they , Clara , if you will only keep I in countenance . " 
 " can you imagine I skate ? " 
 " no . I know you will not   do it , you be so fearfully grave . but there be more fun in you , when you like , or when you can not   help yourself , as I have   see you once or twice , than there be in a hundred such Merry - Andrews as I . at any rate we will go and see they . on with your bonnet now , I can not wait a minute . have something to cover your eye . Conny ' 11 be there I know . " 
 on go my bonnet , nothing loth to have an airing again . it be fade in the box . 
 " now lot of warm thing , darling . you have no idea how cold it be , and scarcely sun enough to thaw the long frost in your eye . let I look at they , Donna . oh if mine be half as bright . you can not   have get they in England . " 
 " now , Idols , do not   talk nonsense . every inch of I be English , and not an inch of you ; although your eye be so blue . you be Scotch all over , or else you be all swiss . " 
 for answer she begin sing " the Merry Swiss Boy , " and be go to dance to her song , when I dance she off down stair . Giudice be in the kitchen , with Tom , from the top of the coffee - mill , sputter anathema at he . a magnificent dog he be , of the race of maltese bloodhound , now so scarce , fawn - colour , long in the flank , deep in the jowl , pouch - eare , and grave of eye . he regard Tom no more than if he have be an old hat brush the wrong way ; and the bird , who be all in a flutter , he take for british butterfly . he come leisurely to I , walk one side at a time , and solemnly deposit his great moist nose in my hand . I know he then as the friend who address I , long since , in the Villa Road . 
 " why , you graven image " - a popular person always have fifty nickname ; Isola have a hundred at least , and she like they all - " what depth of secresy and statecraft be this ! you know how I love dog , and you never even tell I of this splendid fellow 's existence ! " 
 " well , Donna dear , do not   look so indignant . he do not   belong to I , and he will not   come with I unless he be tell , and then he make such a favour of it . see his long supple stride . he walk just like a leopard - do not   you , you pious panther ? I wonder he take to you so . he be not fierce at all , except when he ought to be ; but he hardly ever make friend . " 
 " whose dog be he ? ' 
 " Conrad 's to be sure . and I do believe Conny think more of he than he do of I . get along , you yellow mammoth ! why he would keep his head there all day ? " 
 " all dog love I , Idols . it be so when I be a child . they know how honest I be . " 
 " well , I believe you be , Donna ; and too honest sometimes . but I be honest enough , and Giudice do not appreciate it . come along , Judy . be you go to stick there all day ? " 
 away we go , and the great dog walk behind , keep his head most fairly adjust between we , never shift its place an inch , whether we walk or run - as we do where the street be empty , and when we get into the Park . 
 oh the cold air of heaven , fresh from the clear North Pole , where the Great Bear stalk round the Little Bear with the vigilance of a mother , how it tighten the clip of the joint , put a sting into every step , flush the cheek with Aurora , and sparkle in young eye ! for the nonce we forget who we be , never think how our clothe blow about , our spirit be on the north wind , what be we more than snow flake , let we glisten and lift on the air . 
 cross the Park   lightly furrow with snow at the drain , like our hair when we part it   we come to a broad sheet of ice . we have hear a long way off a crisp musical hollow sound , like tap a box with a hole in it . the ice be not like the old ice at Vaughan Park , but seam and channel , and up and down , and powder light grey with scraping from skate and shoe . thousand of people be on it , some skating , some slide , some rush about and play hot game with crooked stick , some sweep away with short broom , some cry thing for sale and offer skate for hire , many stand still and wistfully eye the land ; but all in the height of good humour , laughing , chaffing , holloaing , drinking , and order more . every now and then some great performer   in his own eye   would sail by the woman grandly   like a ship heel over , with his arm fold and foot over foot , and a long cigar in his mouth . for these one devoutly desire a fall . the skater of real eminence scorn this common show - off , and each have his special admirer form a ring around he , where he have cut his own circus of smoother and greener ice . 
 along the brink of firm land , stand nurse and child innumerable ; the maid on the giggle at every challenge bear to they from the glaze water , the little one tugging , and kicking , and frantic to get on . the background of all the cold scene , whiter as it recede , and break by gliding figure , be form by some low fringe islet , with open water around they , and crane - neck wild fowl wheel about , and warning board , and iceman push flat - bottom boat along . in the far distance , to the right , be two or three canvas tent , where they keep the range of the mercury , and the list of the accident . the long vista be close now and then , as high as hat and bonnet , by scud of the drift ice and snow . 
 here as we stand on the bank , Giudice forsook we shamefully , and bound over the ice , with a levity quite scandalous for a serious - minded dog , towards one of the charmed circle , where eminent skater whirl , like peg - top full of steam - engine . be it likely that we , two girl of spirit , would halt ignobly there ? first on the ice go I , hold Isola 's hand , and tempt she nothing loth . in spite of her boast about skating , Idols be frighten at first , and hold very tightly by I , and want to run back . but the little foot grow braver at every step , and she venture even to clap her hand and dance . to I the thing be no novelty , except from the number of people , and the puckering of the ice . I have even the courage to slide with one foot , but never with both at a time . as for the cracking and bend when some heavy man scour by , on purpose , I dare say , to frighten we , I laugh with my heart in my mouth . Isola be amazed . she never could have conceive that I have so much effrontery . what care I , if a hundred people star at I ? I be do nothing unseemly , and dozen of lady be there . the scene , and the air , and the spirit of youth set my blood all on the bound , and oh , blessing of blessing , my blessed sight be come back . how manly , and stirring , to feel , that a slip - and a limb may be break ; a crack - and one may be drown . 
 but , as usual , I suffer for my temerity . first we follow Giudice , and find he in the centre of the ring , where the great throng be gather , the dog skating with his master , who be one of the very good skater in the world . Giudice be graver than ever , but wistfully glance as he whirl round , at every point of escape . with his heavy fore foot on his master 's shoulder , and his tongue loll out , and his eye roll sadly at each reluctant caper , and his poor tail between his jerked leg , it be impossible not to see that his dignity and self - respect be suffer . so when Conrad come to speak to we , I earnestly beg that Giudice might be set free , which be do in a moment , to the great disappointment of the bystander , and the boundless delight of the dog , who come and gratefully kiss my hand . 
 " why , Donna , " cry Isola in a small pet , " Giudice take you for his mistress : he would never do that to I , if I coax he a hundred year . " 
 Through , the colour the north wind have spread on my cheek , I feel the warm blood rushing , and bend over the dog to hide it ; then much as I long to see Isola 's brother skate , I drag she off rather rudely towards the rougher part of the ice . Conrad look rather surprised and hurt , but resume his figuring with much apparent philosophy . 
 idol and I , with the flush in our cheek , and the flash in our eye , and our form all buoyant with innocent fun , come suddenly round a corner on a party of low - look man , who be cast flat stone , bowling , or curling , or play at drake , with a great tin can for their mark . we turn and be off in a moment ; but we have be observe by the sharp and slyest eye in London . a man give chase in half - skate fashion , have bone tie under his boot , in lieu of skate . we could easily have escape , in spite of his bone ; but be I go to run away , like a skittish servant - maid ? I draw up Miss Isola sharply , whether she would or no , and confront the enemy . it be Mr. Shelfer himself , the man so modest and bashful , who could never bear to look at I . though a dozen more come after he , I feel no alarm at all , know his wonderful shyness and diffidence . but his first address amazed I . 
 " now be your time , lad . at them , I say . here be the two prettiest gal in London . " 
 the low scoundrel ! I see that he be quite tipsy . but frighten as I be , for none but they be near , I could hardly help laugh at he . he have his usual slouch , and the long sly nose , and the pen - house gleam of the eye , and his gaunt cheek draw as if he be always suck they , and the chimneypot hat , that have once belong to some steady go Churchman , with the crown flap in , like the gill of a fish . all this be balance by the skill and comical courage of Bacchus , upon a pair of grate marrow - bone . behind he his countless pocket yawn and look brown on the wind . and this be the being bow down to by Mrs. Shelfer ! 
 " Clara dear , stop , Clara ! " the impudent sot cry out . 
 I have stop without that , and be already face he . for a moment he be abash , for my eye be full upon his ; but the other be come up . 
 " now this be what I call harmony , dash if it be not . why look at the tree and the bush . there be harmony in they tree , ay in every one on them . fine tree and pretty gal , they be the jockey for I . that be what I call natur ' and something like . house ! Lor , there be no harmony in house and pantile . fine tree be all harmony , and so be lovely woman . do not   tell the old gal at home . she never would understand . why idol there be a pretty duck as ever swam on the ice . but Clara 's a - - fine swan , and no mistake . duck be all very well , but a swan be the jockey for I . there be something to lay hold on there . give I a swan I say , and the harmony of they tree . Bob Ridley , I will lay you a tanner I kiss that there swan . ever see such eye , Bob , and look at the way she stand . wonder there be a bit of ice leave here . " 
 the low rogue have a long pipe " in his head , " - as Farmer Huxtable express it , - and at every leer sentence blow out a puff of smoke . 
 " bet you a tanner , Charley , you do not   kiss that stunnin ' gal , " cry his friend , as drunk as himself . 
 " here go , neck or nothing ; " and the niddering make a dash at I . I draw my clenched hand from my muff , where it have be tingle in my glove , and in his tipsy rush , his face come full against it . it be a very odd thing , and I know not how it happen . he reel on his bone from the collision , and stagger in star amazement . before he have time to recover , Conrad dash up like a hawk at an owl ; by some wonderful back - stroke he stop in an instant , wring Shelfer 's crooked stick from his hand , hook he under the collar , strike out again , and tow the poor wretch away backward , at the speed of a mile in a minute . the fire fly from his skate as he dash towards the open water . Giudice , at full gallop behind , try in vain to keep up . every man and woman there turn to watch the issue . shelfer throw out his hand wildly , and scream : he be utterly helpless , his tooth rattle more than the bone on his boot . at the edge of the open water , three hundred yard away , Conrad stop suddenly , like an engine in collision , unhooked Mr. Shelfer , and let he go with full impetus . sprawl and yell in vain , he fling up his arm , and fall backward into the water headlong . the iceman come run with boat , and rope , and grapnel . but before the first splash be over , another be see ; Giudice , at a sign from his master , plunge in , draw the poor man of harmony out , and lay he high and wet on the ice . he be take at once to the tent ; where , as I afterwards hear , he make a fine afternoon of it with the society 's man ; most of whom , it be needless to say , he know . be that as it may , the lesson do he good . he never insult a lady again , or   what be still bad   a poor honest girl , with no education , and no one to defend she . as for I , I really believe he never durst blink his sly eye in my direction again . 
 I love good justice , in or out of the pod . the bean be as sweet to I from the rough air of heaven as from a juryman 's pocket . but I think Master Conrad have overdo it this time . he have no right to risk the poor man 's life . and so I tell he when he come back , as calm as if he have cut a spread eagle . he assure I that he have not risk the man 's life at all . he know the depth of the water there by the island . it be five foot and no more . then I feel all of a glow and long to give he the kiss which have cost Mr. Shelfer so much . the next minute I feel humiliate , and burst into a passion of tear , to think what my father would say at his pet of grace and luxury be insult like that . idol and Conrad , not know my story , could not understand it at all . 
 they come home with I at once . Conrad , " under the circumstance , " venture to offer his arm , which I , under the circumstance , venture to accept . at the door he leave I ; but idol come in with Giudice , commission to see her safe home . she come in partly lest I should feel lonely , partly to arraign Mrs. Shelfer   already condemn by both of we girl   for dare to have such a reprobate drunken husband . 
 CHAPTER IX . 
 when Isola have tell Mrs. Shelfer everything , and a little more than everything   for her imagination be lively , the dominant feeling in the little woman 's bosom be not indignation , as we have expect , but terror . terror of two evil ; the first and chief evil , the possibility of Charley catch cold ; the other , the probability that he would crush Conrad , and tread he into the earth , at the early opportunity . I assure she warmly that Mr. Conrad could well defend himself , even if Shelfer should dare to meddle with he . 
 " oh , my good friend , you have no idea what a terrible fellow Charley be . why he break the head of the skittleman at the " Load of Hay . " so he tell I himself . ah , he be a terrible fellow , when he be put out . " 
 " but you forget , Mrs. Shelfer , he have not   be put out this time ; he be put in . " that Isola always love small joke . 
 " put in , Miss Idols ? " Mrs. Shelfer never understand any joke but her own - " oh yes , put into the water you mean . true , true , and serve he right   so long as he do not   take cold   for call I , his lawful wife who keep he together , ' the old gal at home ! ' but Charley be a terrible fellow , terrible . " 
 " terrible coward more likely , " I cry , " or he would never have dare his low insolence to I . I be sorry for it , Mrs. Shelfer , utterly as I scorn he , because it compel I to leave your house ; and you have be truly good and kind to I . " I think of Mrs. Huxtable ; but how different be the fibre of her kindness ! 
 " leave my house , Miss Valence ! no , no , my good friend , that will never do , not to be think of , and we so use to you and all , and Tom , and the blackbird , and the new squirrel ! a likely story , my good friend , and with your eyelash come ! and do you know who would come instead of you ? " 
 " of course not , Mrs. Shelfer . "
