 my dear Clara , I ought to know well . I be ashamed of myself . and after so many year ! but at the first glance of Fiordalisa , my fate be fix for this life and the other . I never have love before . I never have care to look at a girl ; in fact I despise they all . now I pay for that contemptuous folly . love at one glance , love once , for all , for ever , my heart stand still like the focus of a hurricane ; my speech and every power but that of vision fail I . I dare not try to leave the saddle , such a trembling take I . 
 it be a visitation unknown in our foggy plain , scoff at by our prosy race , but know full well in Southern clime , as the sunstroke of love . my own darling - I can call she nothing less - my own delicious darling be quite startled at I . whether she have a like visitation in a milder form , be more than I can say ; but I hope with all my heart she have ; for then , as the southern tale recite , God place her hand in mine . 
 how I get my horse tie up , how I follow she through the side entrance , and return her father 's greeting , I have not the least idea ; all I know be that she smile , and I want nothing more . but I could not bear to see she in the true homeric fashion still maintain in Corsica , wait on we like a common servant , with her beautiful arched foot glance under the brown pelone , and her taper white arm lay demurely on her bosom ; then at her father 's signal how she fly for the purple grape or the fragrant broccio ! but do what she would , it seem to become she more than all she have do before . as that form of love and elegance flit through the simple room , and those lustrous heavenly eye beam with hospitable warmth , Signor Dezio Della Croce , careworn man with beard of snow , seem at time no little proud of his sweet and only child , but be too proud to show his pride . as for I , he must have think that I speak very poor italian . 
 END of VOL . II . 
 BOOK IV .   continue . 
 CHAPTER X. 
 STORY of EDGAR VAUGHAN . 
 Child Clara , for your own dear sake , as well as mine and my sweet love 's , I will not dwell on that tempestuous time . if you can not comprehend it without word , no word will enable you . if you can , and I fear you do , no more word be want ; and , as an old man weary of the world , I know not whether to envy or to pity you . 
 Hither and thither I be flung , to the zenith star of ecstasy or the nadir gulf of agony , accord as my idol pet choose to smile or frown . though she be no silly child , but a girl of mind and feeling , she have a store , I must confess , of cloud as well as dazzle sunlight in the empyrean of her eye . her nature , like my love , be full of southern passion . it be like the air they breathe , the beauty they behold . one minute of such love compress in a thunder flood all the slow emotion steal through the drought - scrimp channel , where we dredge for gold deposit , through ten year of Saxon courtship . instead of lily - bloom , she should have be call the passion - flower . 
 my life , my soul - how weak our english word be - she love I from the first , I can take my oath she do , although her glory be too great for she to own it yet , though now and then her marvellous eye prove traitor . sometimes when she be rack I most , feign even , with those eye cast down , her pellucid finger point to point , and her little foot tap the orchid bloom , feigning , I say , in cold blood , to reckon her noble lover - long name all and horribly hateful to I - suddenly , while I tremble , and scowl like a true - bear Briton , suddenly up would leap the silky drooping lash , and a spring of soft electric light would flutter through they to the very core of my heart . 
 as for I , I abandon myself . I make no pretence of wait a moment . I fling my heart wide open to she , and if she would not come in , desert it should be for ever . 
 she do come . that life - blood of my soul come in , and would and could live nowhere else for ever . 
 it be do like this . one August evening , when the sun be sink , and the air be full of warmth and wooing sound , the cicale wake from his early nap , the muffro leap for the first dew - drop , the love - bird whispering in the tamarind leaf , Fiordalisa sit with I , under a giant cork - tree on the western slope . the tower be still in Vendetta siege , and the grave and reverend Signor know well than to come out , when the Sbirri be go to the town . lily - bloom be sit by I in a mass of flower ; her light mandile be lay by , that her glorious hair might catch the first waft of the evening breeze . all down her snow - white shoulder fall the labyrinth of tress , twin by I with red Tacsonia , and two pale carnation . her form be pillow in rich fern , that feather round her waist ; of all the frond and plume and stem , not one so taper , light , and rich as that . the bloom upon her cheek be deepen by my playing with her hair , and her soft large eye be beam with delicious wonder . 
 we know , as well as he who make we , that we love one another . none who do not love for ever could interchange such look . suddenly , and without a word , in an ecstasy of admiration , I pass my left arm round her little waist , draw she close to I - she be very near before - and look full into her wondrous eye , find no protest but a thrill of light ; then try her lip and meet her whole heart there . darling , how she kiss I ! no english girl can do it . and then the terror of her maiden thought . the recollection of her high - bear pride , and higher because God - bear innocence . how she weep , and blush , and tremble ; tremble , blush , and weep again ; and then vouchsafe one more entrancing kiss , to atone for the unwitting treason . even thus I would not be content . I want word as well . 
 " do you love I , my own Lily , with every atom of your heart ? " 
 " I have not leave one drop of blood for all the world besides . " 
 and it be true . and so it be with I . I tell her father that same night . and now in the heaven of gladness and wild pleasure , beyond all dream of earth , open the hell of my wickedness and crime ; which but for mercy and long repentance would sever I from my Lily in the world to come . to some the crime may seem a light one , to I it be a most atrocious sin , enhance tenfold by its awful consequence . 
 by my crime , I do not mean my sinful adoration , as cold man may call it , of a fellow mortal . nature have no time to waste , and unless she mean my Lily to be worship , she would not have lavish all her skill in make she so divine . no , I mean my black deceit , in pass for my brother . oh , Clara , do not   go from I . 
 like many another ruinous sin , it be commit without thought , or rather without deliberation . no scheme be lay , not even the least intention cherish ; but the moment bring it , and the temptation be too great . who could have that love pet gaze at he so , and not sell his soul almost to win she to his arm ? 
 Laurence Daldy be a lazy ass . I do not want to shift my blame to he , but merely state a fact . if he have not be a lazy ass , your father would be live now - ay , and my Fiordalisa . when he choose , he could write very good italian , and a clear , round hand , and oh , rare accomplishment for an officer , he could even spell . but his letter to Signor Dezio , scrawl betwixt two game of pool , be a perfect magpie 's nest of careless zigzag , wattle , and sand slap - dash . in those day a hasty writer use to flick his work with sand , which stanch but do not dry the ink . the result be often a grimy dabble , like a child 's face blotch with blackberry . 
 Lily and I have quite arrange how we should present ourselves . like two child we rehearse it under the twilight tree . " and then , you know , " my sweet love whisper , " I shall give you a regular kiss beneath the dear father 's beard , and you will see what an effect it will have . thence he will learn , oh sweetest mine , that there be no help for it ; because we corsican girl be so chary of our lip . " 
 " be you indeed , my beautiful Lily ? I must teach you liberality , to I , and to I alone . " 
 " sweetest mine , " she always call I from the moment she confess her love ; and so , no doubt , she be call I now in heaven . 
 the curtain hang in heavy fold across the narrow doorway of the long dark room . the hospitable board be gay with wine and dainty fruit , melon , fig , and peach , plum of golden and purple hue , pomegranate , pomi d'oro , green almond , apricot , and muscatel from the ladder of Cape Corso . through they and upon they play the mellow light from a single lamp , with dance lustre round it . all the rest of the room be dark . at the head of the table sit Signor Dezio Della Croce , wait for his guest and daughter . post high at the end window on a ledge of rough - hewn board , stand the ancient warder , who have live for fifty year among they , and whose great fusil command the only approach to the castle . 
 as we enter timidly , the maiden 's right hand on my neck , my left arm round her ductile waist , our other hand clasp firmly , I glance toward that noxious sentinel . 
 " never mind he , sweetest mine . do not   believe that he be there . Grandpapa , I call he , and he know all my secret . " 
 Signor Dezio look amazed , as we glide towards he . his life have be one series of crush blow from heaven . three brave son have be barbarously murder in Vendetta , and his graceful love wife have break her heart and die . the sole hope of his house , his petle Fiordalisa , though she call herself a woman and be full sixteen , he look upon she still in his trouble - tear chronology , as only ripe enough to be dandle on his lap . still he call she his " Ninnina , " and sing nannas to she , as he have be oblige to do after her mother 's death . 
 as he sit there , too astonished to smile , or frown , or say a word , Lily drop upon her knee before he , as a grecian maiden would . we English be not supple - jointed ; but for Lily 's sake , I could not stand beside she . then she place her soft right hand in the centre of my hard palm , fling the other arm round my neck , and with her eye upon her father 's , give I a long affectionate kiss . this do , she draw her father 's head down , and kiss his snow - white beard . now , she tell I , after this , any father who be obdurate , must accord to institution blame himself and no one else , if harm befall the maiden . 
 all this time , I speak not , and think of nothing except to screen my Lily . signor Dezio keep a stately silence , but the tear be in his eye , and the long white beard be quiver . lily bend her head , and wait for his word . 
 " Mother of God ! my little child , what be you think of ? " 
 " only thinking of be marry , father . " 
 " and set another Vendetta afoot , and be kill yourself ! signor " - turn haughtily to I - " this lady be betroth , from her early infancy , to her cousin Lepardo Della Croce . " 
 " oh , I hate he , " cry Fiordalisa , clasp her hand piteously . " ah , Madonna , I hate he so ; and thank our Lady , no one have see he for six year . he be dead no doubt in some Cannibal Island . saint of mercy , keep he . I see it in the Spalla , in the Shepherd 's Spalla , and I see my own love there , the eve before he come . " 
 " Grace of Holy Mary ! who read the Spalla for you ? " 
 " the hoary goatherd from Ghidazzo . " and up sprang Fiordalisa , fly to an inner room , and fetch from the dark niche in the wall the box of holy relic . with these she kneel before her father , and place she right hand on the box . 
 " my child , it be not needful . I believe you without an oath . never yet have you pass the boundary of truth . " 
 the old chief bow his head in thought . he have lose his last survive son by neglect the Spalla 's decree . the Spalla be the shoulder blade of a goat , polished , and use for divination ; upon it have be read Sampiero 's death , and the destiny of Napoleon . the old man who have forecast the latter be still alive , and of immense renown , and traverse the island now like an ancient prophet . he be the hoary goatherd of Ghidazzo . 
 Lily see that she be conquer ; she leap upon her father 's knee and hug he ; and her triumph be complete . while she weep upon his breast , and tell he all her little tale , and whisper in his ear , and while he kiss , and comfort she , and think of her dear mother , I rush out and leap the Vinea , and weep beneath the olive - tree . 
 at last the old man rise and call I , he durst not venture from the door ; but he do what be far well , he send my own love after I . at length when we return , and we find cause not to hurry , - 
 " Signor Vogheno , " he begin , " I have observe you well . I be a man of very keen observation " - Lily 's eye give I a twinkle full of fun - " or I should not be alive this moment . I have observe you , sir , and I approve your character . I can not say as much , sir , of all the englishman I have be privileged to meet . there be about they very much of the nature of a dog . forgive I , sir ; pray interrupt I not . I only judge by what I have see . God forbid that I should say so to you , while you be my guest . now you be one of my family , and entitle to the result of my observation . of the little island itself I know nothing at all , though I be inform that its institution be of a barbarous character . " 
 " vendetta for instance , " be on my lip , but Lily 's glance just save it . and I think of his three brave son . 
 " but , Signor belove , you be different from they ; indeed you have the nobility of the corsican nature . and what be most of all , my little child have fix her heart upon you . but she be very young , sir , quite a child you see . " I see nothing of the sort , but a bloom maiden figure , grow lovelier every day . Poor Lily drop her long eyelash , and smile through a glowing blush . so blush Lavinia under the eye of Turnus . 
 " this darling child be now the heiress to these land of mine . and if her cousin Lepardo , whose death she have see on the Spalla , be indeed remove from we , she be the very last of all the Della Croce . I can not easily read the billet of your brother . he do not write good Corsican of our side of the mountain , but some outlandish tuscan . there be something first which I can not well decipher , and then I see your name Signor Valentine Vogheno , and that you be the lord of very large estate , in some district call Gloisterio ? " he look at I inquiringly . 
 instead of explain that I be only the brother of the great Signor Valentino , I bow , alas I bow with a hot flush on my cheek . what could it matter , and why should I interrupt he , if he choose to deceive himself ? Lily charm away all hesitation , by clap her little hand , and cry , " sweetest mine , I be so glad . " 
 " then , upon two condition I will give you my daughter . the first , that you leave this island , and do not see our Lily , write to , or even hear from she , for a period of six month . if she have not outgrow her love , she will then be almost old enough to we d. I mean , of course , if Lepardo do not appear . the other condition be that you shall promise on the holy relic , and you as well , my flower , never to part with these old estate , but keep they for Lily while she live , and transmit they to her second child . " 
 a load of terror be off my heart - I think he be go to bind I to the accursed vendetta . even for my Lily , I could hardly have take that pledge . so I assent readily to the last stipulation , though it be base upon a virtual lie of mine . but with Lily 's eye upon I , brim as they be with tear at the first condition , and her round arm tremble to enfold I , could I stick at anything short of downright murder ? the first proviso I fight against in vain . even Lily coax and cry , without any good effect . 
 when at last we yield to the stern decree , the venerable father , as we knelt before he , join our hand together , and pour a blessing on we , which I do not lack . he have give I my blessing . 
 after this we sit down to supper , and the trusty musketeer , who have watch the whole scene grimly , and without hear all , know what the result be , he , I say , upon his perch begin to improvise , or haply to adapt , and sing to a childish air , some little verse upon the glad occasion . have exhaust his stock , down he leap without permission , and drink our health in a bumper of Luri wine . 
 Lily be now in due course of promotion . no long be she the handmaid , whose eye create and rejoice in countless mistake of mine . now she be sit by my side , as she have good right to be , and be lose in pretty rapture at my gallant attention . they be very nice , she own , but thoroughly un - Corsican . how I wish her father and the old fusileer away ! 
 CHAPTER XI . 
 " six long month to be away from Lily ! and perhaps forget she , and find some lovelier maiden . " 
 " by Lily 's side , all maid be burdock . and yet what if I do ? " 
 she show a small stiletto toy with a cross upon the handle , and grind her pearly tooth together . 
 " will it be for I , or she ? " 
 " both ; and Lily afterwards . " 
 " oh you wholesale little murderer ! three great kiss directly , one for every murder . " 
 " only if you promise , on the relic , never to look twice at a pretty maiden . " 
 and so we spend the precious time , - ten day allow I to prepare my yacht - in talk utter nonsense , and con fifty foolish scheme , to make we seem together . I be for depart at once , that the period might begin to run ; but Lily be for keep I to the last possible moment , and of course she have her way . it be fix that I should sail on the 10th day of September . my little boat , now call the " lily flower , " be bring from Calvi , and moor in a secluded cove , where my love could see it from her bedroom window . it be no long corsican law that I should live in the castle . the privilege of a guest be go ; and the rigorous code of suitorship begin . but to I and my own darling it make very little difference . I never leave Vendetta tower , as I lightly name it , until my pet be order off to bed ; and every morn I climb the height , after a long swim in the sapphire ripple , and meet my own sweet Lily sparkling from the dew of her early toilet . how she love I , how I love she ; which more than other let angel say ; for we could not decide . that ancient Corsican her father , albeit little verse in book , be as upright and downright a gentleman as ever know when his presence be not require . therefore he take my word of honour for his Lily 's safety ; and leave she to her own sweet will ; and her sweet will be to spend with I all her wake hour . for she as yet there be no fear of the blood - avenger . accord to their etiquette they can not shoot the daughter , until they have shoot the father . as to the son the restriction do not hold . the feud we be concern in have last now 120 year , and cost the life of 130 people . it lie between the ancient race of Della Croce , and De Gentili , and owe its origin to the discovery of a dead mule on the road to church . the question be which family should be exterminate first . for many year the house of Della Croce have be in the ascendant , have produce a long succession of good shot and clever bushman . at one time all the hope of the De Gentili hang upon one infant life , which be not think worth the taking . fatal error - that one life have prove a mighty trump . one after one the Della Croce fall before that original artist , who invent a patent method of trunke himself in olive bark and fire from a knot - hole . many a story Lily tell I of his devilish wile ; and in those story I rejoice , because she cling around my neck , and tremble so that I must hold she . happily now this olive - branch be dead , have receive his death - wound while he administer one to Lily 's young brother . ever since that , the feud have languish , and strict etiquette require that the Della Croce should perpetrate the next murder . but her father , say my Lily , with her sweet head on my breast and her soft eye full of fire , her father do not seem to care even to shoot the cousin of the man who have shoot her brother . 
 Darling Lily , my blood run cold , even with your beauty in my arm , to hear you talk of murder so . own pet , I shall change you . you heaven mean for love , and softness , and delight : human devilry have taint even you . it be not an easy task to change she . of all human passion revenge be far the strong . Clara , how your eye flash . you ought to have be a Corsican . it be not an easy task ; but love love difficulty . in my ten short day of delicious wretchedness , almost I teach Fiordalisa to despise revenge . and what do you think avail I most ? not the Bible . no , her mind and soul be swathe by Popery in the rag of too many saint . what help I most , and the only thing that help I at all , except caress , be the broad and free expanse of the ever change sea . her nature be all poetry , her throb breast an Idyl . upon my little quarter - deck I have a cushion niche for she , and there we sit and steer ourselves while the sailor sleep below . alone upon the crystal world , pledge for life or death together , drink deep draught of passion and thirsting still for more , what care we for petty hatred , we whose all in all be love ? how she listen as I speak , how her large eye grow enlarge . 
 at last those eye , pure well of love , be trouble with hot tear . the fatal day be come . Tokens we have interchange , myriad vow , and countless pledge , which even love could scarce remember . with all the passion of her race , and all the fervour of the clime , she bar her beautiful round arm , the part that lie most near the heart and touch it with the keen stiletto , then she throw her breast on mine , and I lay the crimsoned ivory on my lip . how the devil - excuse I , Clara - how the devil I get away , only phlegmatic Englishmen can tell . no Frenchman , or italian , would have leave that heavenly darle so . we put it off to the last moment , till it be quite dangerous to pass the rocky jaw . as my bad luck would have it , there be a purpling sunset breeze . my own love on the furth point , her white foot in the water , grow small and small yet , and stand upon tiptoe to be see for another yard ; my own darling love of age , she loose her black hair down her snowy vest , for I to know she from the rock behind ; then she wave and wave her sweet palm hat , fragrant of my Lily , - I have kiss every single inch of it , - until she think I could not see she ; and then , as my telescope show I , back she fall upon a ledge of rock , and I could see or fancy her delicious bosom heaving to the fury of her tear . we glide past the cavern mouth , and the silver beach beyond it , whence we have often watch the sunset ; and then a beetle crag take from I the last view of Lily . 
 however long the schoolboy may have bleed from some big coward 's bullying , or the sway of the rustle birch and the bosky thrill that follow , however sore he may have weep while hang head - downwards through the midnight hour , with a tallow candle between his tooth , or in the pang of noun heteroclite and brachycatalectic dinner ; yet despite these minor ill , his fond heart turn through after life to the scene of foot - ball and I - spy , to the day when he could jump or eat any mortal thing . and so it be with bygone love . even the time of separation or of bitter quarrel , the aching heart whereon the keepsake lie , the spasm of jealousy , the tenterhook of doubt ; remembrance look upon they all as treasure of a golden age . 
 over the darken sea , we bear away for Sardinia . hour and hour , I gaze upon the cushion , where my own pet darling use to lean and love I . to I they be fair than all the star , or the phosphorescent sea . from time to time our Corsican pilot keep himself awake , by chant to strangely mournful air some of the vocero or dirge , the burden of many age in that lament land . fit home for Rachel , Niobe , or Cassandra , where half a million gallant being , twice the number of the present population , have fall victim to the blood - revenge . so Corsican historian tell ; a thousand violent death each year , for the last five century . sometimes the avenger wait for half a lifetime , lurk till his moment come . before his victim have cease to quiver , or the shot to ring down the rocky pass , he be off for the bush or the mountain , and lead thenceforth a bandit 's life . 
 they tell I , Clara , that thing be well now , and this black stain on a chivalrous race be be purge by christian civilization . be it as it may , I love the island of my Lily still ; and hope , please God , to see it once more , before I go to she . 
 banish though I be , for the present , from the only place I care for , it seem still great severance to go far than I could help . therefore instead of return to England , I spend the winter in cruise along the western coast of Italy , and the south of Spain ; and coast back to Genoa . to Seville , and other place fame for beautiful woman , I make especial trip , to search for any fit to compare with my own maiden . of course I know none could be find ; but it give I some employment , and bitter pleasure , to observe how inferior be all . to my eye , bright with one sweet image , no other form have grace enough to be fit pillow for my charmer 's foot . how I long and yearn for some fresh token of she : all her little gift I carry ever in my bosom , but never let another 's eye rest one moment on they . not even would I tell my friend one word about my love ; it seem as if it would grow common by be talk about . to Peter Green I write , resign my commission , although I do not tell he that I have find the olive . no , friend Peter , those olive be much too near my Lily ; and I will not   have you or any other stranger there . I know she would not look at you ; still I would rather have you a thousand mile away . free trade , if you like , when I have make my fortune ; which by the bye be somewhat the maxim of that school . my fortune , not in olive , oil , or even guineas - all that rubbish you be welcome to - but my fortune where my heart and soul be all invest , and now , no more my fortune , but my certain fate in Lily . 
 at length and at last my calendar - like a homesick pair at school , we have make one for each other , thank God that it be not a leap - year - my calendar so often count , so punctually erase , begin to yield and totter to the stubborn sap of time . my patience long ago have yield , my blood be in a fever . another thing begin to yield , alas it be my money . Green , Vowler , and Green have behave most liberally ; but of course the expense of my vessel have be heavy on I ; and now my salary have cease . Peter Green write to I in the kind and most handsome manner , press I , if tired   as he conclude   of those murderous Corsicans , to accept another engagement in Sardinia . even without impart my last discovery , I have do good service to the firm . I smile at the idea of my be weary of Corsicans : even now the mere word send a warm tide to my heart . 
 it be not for the beauty of the scene , or the work of art , that I remain in Genoa ; but because it be the likeli place to see the Negro 's head . as we lay at the end of the mole , my glass command all that enter ; and every lugger or xebec that bear the sacred emblem - off my little dingy push from our rake stern , and with one man , now my friend because a thorough Corsican , I board she , at all hazard of imprisonment ; and crave for tiding of the sacred land . although , of course , I would not show the nest of all my thought , yet by beat about the bush , I get some scrap of news . the great Signor be flourish , and have harvest an enormous crop of olive : his lovely daughter , now become the glory of the island , have be ill of something like marsh - fever , but be now as bloom as the rose . they do say , but the captain could not at all believe it , that she have be betroth to some foreign olive - merchant . what disgrace ! the high blood and the sweetest maid in Corsica , to be betray to an oilman ! plenty of other news I gather - the good people be great gossip - but this be all I care for . meanwhile your father , Clara dear , reply most warmly to my letter , send I a sum on loan , which quite relieve I from cheese - paring . and now the wind be in the north , and it be almost time to start for the arm of Lily . if I wait any long , I should be too mad to bear the voyage . at the break of day we leave the magnificent harbour , and the cold wind from the maritime Alps chill all but the fire of love . up and down the little deck , up and down all day and night ; sleep I never would again , until I touch my Lily . on the evening of the 8th of March , we be near Cape Corso ; next day we coast down the west to the lively breeze of spring , and so upon the 9th we moor to the tongue of Calvi . at midnight we be under way , and when the sun could reach the sea over the snowy peak , we glide past the mountain crescent that look on the Balagna . in the early morning still , when the dew be float , we round the gray headland of Signor Dezio 's cove , and I climb along the bowsprit to glance beyond the corner . 
 what be that white dress I see flutter at the water 's edge ? whose be that red - striped mandile toss on high and catch again ? and there the flag - staff I erect , with my colour fly ! only one such shape on earth - only two such arm - out with the boat or I must swim , or run the yacht ashore . the boat have be tow alongside for the last six hour : Lily can not   wait for the boat any more than I can . from rock to rock she be leap ; which be the near one ? into the water she run , then away in blush terror - she forget all about the other man . but I know where to find she , she have drop her little shoe , she must be in my grotto . 
 there I press she to my heart of heart , trembling , weeping , laugh , all unable to get close enough to I . 
 " sweetest mine , ten thousand time , I have be so wretched . " her voice be like a silver bell . 
 " my own , I be so glad to hear it . but how well you look ! " 
 if she be lovely when I leave she , what shall I call she now ? there be not one atom of she but be pure perfection . I hold she from I for one moment , to take in all her beauty . she have a most delicious fragrance that steal upon my sens . toilet bottle she never hear of ; what she have be nature 's gift , and unperceive except by love . I have often tell she of it , but she will not   believe it . it be not your breath , you darling ; your breath be only violet ; it come from every fibre of you , even from your hair ; it be as when the wind have kiss a lily of the valley . 
 the ancient Signor be a man of very keen observation , do not delay our wedding any long than could be help . that evening we haul down the family fusileer , give he a goblet of wine , and send he about his business : for one night we would take our chance even of Vendetta . at supper - time the Signor be in wonderful spirit , and drink our health with many praise of our constancy and obedience . one little fact he mention worth a thousand propination ; his daughter 's fever have be cure by some chance news of I . he even go away to fetch a bottle of choice Rogliano , when he see how I be fidgette to get my arm round Lily . then after make his re - entrance , with due clumsiness at the door , he quite disgrace himself , while draw the cork , by even wink at I , as he say abruptly , 
 " Fiordalisa , when would you like to be marry ? " 
 my Lily blush , I must confess , but do not fence with the question . 
 " as soon as ever you please , papa . that is , if my love wish it . " but she would not look at I to ask . in the porch she whisper to I , that it be only from her terror of the bad Lepardo come . do the love creature fancy that I would believe it ? 
 once more we sail together over the amethyst sea ; she be as fond of the water as a true - bear Briton . in her thought and glance be infinite variety . none could ever guess the next thing she would say . thoroughly I know her heart , because I live therein , and sweet lodging never man be bless with . but of her mind she veil as yet the maiden delicacy , strictly as she would the glow rich of her figure . what amaze I more than all , be that while most corsican girl be of the nut - brown order , no sun ever burn the snowy skin of Lily : she always look so clear and clean , as if it be impossible for anything to stain she . Clara , you be always talk of your lovely Isola . I wonder where she get her name : it be no stranger to I . something in your description of she remind I of my Lily . I long to see the girl : and you must have some reason for so obstinately prevent I . 
 CHAPTER XII . 
 Though Lily and I be most desirous to keep thing as quiet as possible , by this time our engagement be talk of in every house of the Balagna . that paternal fusileer and my merry yachtsman , although they look the other way whenever we approach , would not permit the flower of Corsica , as she be now proclaim , to blush with I unseen . my sailor attend to her far more than to their business , and would have leap into the water for one smile of Lily . 
 it be the fashion of the island to make a wedding jubilee ; and the Signor be anxious to outdo all that have ever be do . we , absorb in one another , do our good to disappoint he ; but he scorn the notion of any private marriage . I never shall forget how he knit his silver brow when I make a last attempt to bring he to our view . " Signor Vogheno , to I you appear to forget whose daughter it be that love you . perhaps in your remote , but well regard island maiden may be steal before their father can look round . indeed , I have hear that they leap over a broomstick . that be not the custom here . Fiordalisa Della Croce be my only child - the child of my old age ; and not altogether one to be ashamed of . I can afford to be hospitable , and I mean to be so . " 
 the Corsicans be a most excitable race , and , when affront , seem to lash their side as they talk . by the time the good Signor have finish his speech , every hair of his beard be curl with indignation . but his daughter spring into his arm and kiss away the temp , and promise , if it must be so , to make herself one mass of gold and coral . so the Parolanti , or mediator , be invoke ; an armistice for a week be sign , and honour pledge on either side . free and haughty be the step of the Signor Dezio as he set forth for the town to order everything he could see ; and very wroth again he be , because I would not postpone the day for he to get a shipload of trumpery from Marseilles . this time I be resolve to have my way . besides the fervour of my passion and my dread of accident , the one thing of all other I detest be to be star at anywhere . and it be far bad to be star at by a foreign race . the Corsicans be gentleman by nature , but they could not be expect to regard without some curiosity the lucky stranger who have win their Lily . 
 I will not weary you , as I myself be weary , with all the ceremony of the wedding - day . all I want be my bride , and she want none but I : yet we could not help be touch by the hearty good - will of the commune . the fame of Lily 's beauty have spread even to Sardinia , and many a handsome woman come to measure her own thereby . clever as they be at such thing , not one of they could find a blemish or defect in Lily , and our fair Balagnese tell they to go home and break their mirror . 
 it be a sweet spring morning , and amid a fearful din of gun and trumpet , mandolin and fiddle , I wait with a nervous smile under the triumphal arch in front of my fictitious house . a sham house have be make of board , and bough , and flower , because it be most essential that the bride should be introduce to the bridegroom 's dwelling . here I be to receive the procession , which at last appear . first come fifty well - arm youth , crown with leaf and ribbon ; then four - and - twenty maiden dress alike , sing and scatter flower , and then a boy of noble birth , mount on a pony , and carry the freno , symbol of many scion . none of they I look at ; only for my Lily . on a noble snow - white palfrey , deck from head to foot with flower , her father walk at her side , come the bloom , the flower , the lily of they all , array in clear white muslin , self - possess , and smile . one glorious wreath be round her head ; it be her own black hair by her own sweet finger twin with sprig of myrtle . a sash , or fazoletto , of violet transparent crape , loop at the crown of her head , fall over the shy lift of her bosom , part like a sunset cloud , where the boddice open below the pear - like waist . to I she look like a white coralline rise through an amethyst sea . behind she come the authority of the commune . the sham key be already hang at her slender zone . it be my place to lift she down and introduce she formally . this I do with excellent grace , feel the weight of eye upon I . but when I get she inside , I spoil the fold of the fazoletto . I hear the old man shout , " who be ye gallant son of the mountain , who have carry off my daughter ? to I , indeed , ye seem to be brave and noble man , yet have ye take she rather after the manner of bandit . know ye not that she be the fair flower that ever be rear in Corsica ? " 
 " yes , old fellow , I know that well enough ; and that be the very reason why I have get she here . " one more virgin kiss , and with Lily on my arm , forth I sally to respond . 
 " friend we be , who claim some hospitality . we have pluck the fairest flower on all the strand of Corsica , and we bear she to the priest , fit offering for Madonna . " 
 " bide on , my noble friend ; then come and enjoy my feast . " 
 no more delay . the maid have get all they can do to keep in front of we with their flower . the armed youth stand on either side at the entrance to the church . the taper be already light , the passage up the little church be strew with flower myrtle . lily , hold her veil around she , walk hand in hand with I . 
 Fiordalisa Della Croce now be Lily Vaughan ; amidst a world of shout , shooting , and cornamusa , we be lead to the banqueting - room ; there they seat we in two chair , and a fine fat baby be place on Lily 's lap , to remind she of her duty . she dandle it , and kiss it as if she understand the business , and then present it with a cap of coral and gay ribbon . now Lily Vaughan throw off her fazoletto , and give I for a keepsake the myrtle in her hair . then all who can claim kin with she , to the fortieth generation , hurry up and press her hand , and wish the good old wish . " long life and grow pleasure , son like he , and daughter like yourself . " 
 after the banquet , we be free to go , have first lead off the ballo in the Cerca dance . thank God , my Lily be at last my own ; she fall upon my bosom weary and delight . Clara , remember this : the little church in which we be married be call St. Katharine 's on the cliff ; and I sign the record in my proper name , Edgar Malins Vaughan : the Malins , very likely , they do not know from Valentine , for I always write it with a flourish at the end . the Signor , with all his friend , escort we to the limit of his domain ; there we bid they heartily farewell , and they return to renew the feast . my little yacht be in the bay , and we see the boat push off to fetch we as have be arrange . we be to sail for Girolata , where the Signor have a country - house , lonely enough even for two such lover . three or four hour would take we thither , and the sun be still in the heaven . as no one now could see we , Lily perform a little dance for my especial delight . how beam she look , how full of spirit , now all the worry be over . then she trip roguishly at my side to the wind rocky step that lead to St. Katharine 's cove . the cove be like a well scoop in the giant cliff . as we descend the steep and narrow stair , my Lily tremble on my arm . the house and all the merry - maker be out of sight and hearing . of course we stop every now and then , for the boat could not be at the landing yet , and we have much to tell each other . 
 as we step upon the beach , and under the eave of a jutting rock , a tall man stand before we . his eye and beard be black as jet , and he wear the loose dress of a southern seaman . three sailor , unmistakeably English , be smoke and play card in the corner shade of the cliff . Lily start violently , turn pale , and clung to I , but face the intruder bravely . he be quite amazed at her beauty , I at his insolent gaze . 
 " Fiordalisa Della Croce , " he say with a pure tuscan accent , " behold I ! I be come to claim you . " 
 he actually lay his small , but muscular hand upon my Lily 's shoulder . she leap back as from a snake . I know it must be Lepardo . 
 " Sir , " I say , as calmly as I could , " oblige I by allow my wife to pass . " 
 the sneer , supercilious look which he hardly deign to spare I , be honest , compare to his foul stare at she . 
 " Signor , she be too beautiful . I must have my right . come for she when I be tired , if any can tire of she . " 
 and he thrust his filthy , hairy lip under my own pet 's hat . my muscle leap , and my soul be in the blow . down he go like a flail , and I think he be stun for an hour ; but while I be bear my pet to the boat , which now be close to the beach , up he leap , and rush at I with a dagger - a dagger like one which you know . I do not see he , but Lily do over my shoulder ; she spring from my arm and fling herself between we . he thrust she aside , and leap at I like a panther , aim straight at my heart . how he miss I I can not tell , but think it be through Lily . before he recover , I close with he , wrest away the weapon and fling it far into the sea . but one main thing I omit ; I ought to have stun he thoroughly . into the boat with Lily - I catch up an oar , and away we dash . the three english sailor be run up . as a wave take the boat about , one of they grasp the stern ; down on his knuckle crash my oar , and with a curse he let go . all right , all clear , off for the yacht for your life . I would show fight , for my blood be up , but what would become of Lily ? and we be but three against four , and none of we have arm . 
 meanwhile , that black Italian , I can never call he a Corsican , sneak away to a tuft of sea - grass for his double - barrel fusil . bow with all my might , I see he examine the priming , lie his red cap on a rock , and the glistening gun on the cap , and , close one eye , take steady aim , not at I , but at Lily . Poor Lily sit on the thwart at my side , faintly star with terror . no time to think ; oar and all I dash in front of my darling . a ping in the air , a jar on my wrist , a slight blow on my breast , and at my foot drop the bullet . it have pass through the tough ash handle . down , Lily , down , for God 's sake ; he be fire the other barrel . I fling she down in the bilge water ; the brute can not see she now . not quite so easily off . up a steep rock he climb like a cat , the cursed gun still in his hand . he win fifty foot of vantage , and command the whole of the boat . we be not eighty yard away . there he coolly level at my prostrate Lily . I have grey hair next morning . forward , I throw myself , over my wife ; I he might kill if he choose . one lurch of the boat - a short sea be run - and my darling 's head be show . he see his chance and fire . thank God , he have too little powder in ; my own love be untouched . the ball fall short of Lily , and pass through my left foot , in at the sole and out below the instep . luckily I have retain my dancing shoe , or my thick boot would have keep the ball in my foot . the brute could not see that he have hit any one , and he curse we in choice italian . 
 Poor Lily have quite swoon away , and know nothing of my wound . over the side of the yacht I lift she myself , stand upon one leg . no one else should touch she . so furious I be with that cold - blooded miscreant , that if I could only have walk , I would have return to fight he . my man , too , be quite up for it . but when Lily come to herself , and throw her arm round I and weep , and thank God and her saint , I find my foot quite soak in a pool of blood , and stiffening . poor little dear ! what a fuss she do make about it ! I would have bear ten time the pain for the smile and tear she give I . one thing be certain - under the mercy of God , we owe our life to each other , and hold they henceforth in common . 
 as , with a flow sheet , we double the craggy point , conceal close under the rock we see a low and snake - like vessel , of the felucca build . she be bank for three pair of sweep , and look a thorough rover . this be , of course , Lepardo 's boat . we now bear away for Ajaccio , dear Lily have implore I not to think of Girolata , where no medical aid could anyhow be procure . moreover , she want to fly from that dark Lepardo ; and I be quite willing to own that , despite my delicious nursing , I be not ambitious to stand as target again during our honeymoon . 
 CHAPTER XIII . 
 at first I think a great deal more of the pain than the danger of my wound ; but when I show it to the french surgeon at Ajaccio , he surprise I by shrug his shoulder formidably , and declare that it be the good God if I keep my foot . be of a somewhat sceptical turn , I think at first that he only want to gild the frame of his work ; but when I begin to consider it , I find that he be quite right . the fact be , that I have think much more of my bride than of my metatarsal . two of these be splinter where the bullet pass between they , and it be a question whether it have not be poison . many of the mountaineer be skilled in deadly drug , and use they rarely for the bowl , not so rarely for the sword and gun . 
 at one time there be symptom even of mortification , and my wife , who wait hand and foot upon I , join the surgeon in implore I to submit to amputation . 
 " sweetest mine ! do you suppose that I shall love you any the less because you walk on crutch , and all through your love of I ? and what other difference can it make to either of we ? I shall cry a great deal at first , for I love your little toe - nail more than I do my own eye ; but , darling , we shall get over it . " 
 as she love my toe so much , I resolve to keep they , if it be only for her sake ; and , after a narrow crisis , my foot begin to get well . to her care and tenderness I owe my recovery , far more than to the skill of the clever surgeon . six month elapse before I could walk again , and our little yacht be send to Calvi to explain the long delay . fond as I be of the " lily - flower , " I be anxious now to sell she ; but my darling nurse , although she know before our marriage that I be not a wealthy man , would not listen to the scheme at all ; for the doctor order I , as I grow strong , to be constantly on the water . 
 " not by any mean , my own , will we sell our little love - boat . I should cry after it like a baby ; and another thing , far more important , you can bear no motion except on board our Lily . Papa have get great heap of money , and he never can refuse I anything when I coax in earnest . " 
 conscious as I be of my vile deceit , I would rather have die than apply to Signor Dezio , albeit I be quite sure that he would soon have forgive I . so I write again to my good - natured brother and banker , and tell he all that have happen , but beg he not to impart it even to your mother . I have strong reason for suspect that he do not conceal it from she ; but as I never allude to the subject before she , she be too much a lady ever to lead I towards it . my motive for this reserve be at first some ill - define terror lest my fraud upon Signor Dezio should come to light prematurely . also I hate to be talk about among people whom I despise . afterwards , as you will perceive , I have other and far more cogent reason . 
 I need not say that your father - dear Clara , I ought to love you , if only on his account ! - your father write I a kind and most warm - hearted letter , accompany by a most handsome gift - no loan this time , but a wedding - gift , and a very noble one . also he press I to come home with my bride the moment I could endure the voyage . ah ! if I have only obey he , not Lily and Henry , but myself would have be the victim . 
 we return as soon as possible to Vendetta tower , and find the good Signor in excellent spirit , delight to see his sweet daughter again , and still more delighted by hope of a little successor to the gray wall and the olive grove . when this hope be realize , and a lusty young grandson be lay in his arm , he become so wild in his glory , that he go about boast all over the commune , feast all who come near he , forget the very name of the blood - revenge . many a time we remind and implore he to be more careful . he reply , that his life be of no importance now ; he have come to his haven among his own dear one , and be crown the old ship with flower . moreover , he know that the De ' Gentili be of a nobler spirit than to shed the blood of a gray - haired man , when institution do not very loudly demand it . and so I believe they be . 
 alas ! the poor old man ! - a thorough and true gentleman as one need wish to see - choleric albeit , and not too wide of mind ; but his heart be in the right place , and make of the right material , and easy enough to get at . he be free to confess his own failing , and could feel for a man who be tempt . deeply thankful I be that , before his white beard be lay low , I acknowledge to he my offence , and obtain his hearty forgiveness . Little Henry be on his lap , go off into smile of sleep , with his mother 's soft finger in his mouth . at first my confession quite take the poor Signor aback ; for I do not attempt to gloss the dishonour of what I have do ; but I tell he truly that the meanness be not in my nature , and although I have win my pet Lily , the road run through hemlock and wormwood . and now I perceive how uncalled - for and stupid the fraud have be . 
 when the old man recover a little from the shock cause by the dishonesty - towards which recovery the tear of his daughter and the smile of his grandson contribute - he be really glad to find that I be not a land Signor . he rub his hand and twitch his beard with delight , for now his little Enrico would never be take away to the barbarous english island . be he not rightful successor to the land of the Della Croce ? and what more could he possibly want ? what could he care for the property in Gloisterio ? however , he make we promise that if the present remarkable baby , Master Henry Vaughan , should ever enjoy the property in the unpronounceable county , Lily 's second child , if she have one , should take the corsican lordship ; for his great fear be , that the Malaspina and Della Croce estate should become a servient tenement to the frozen field of the North . to express and ensure his wish , he have a deed - poll prepare accord to his own fancy , read it to we and some witness , then sign , seal , and enrol it . this be one of the document which you , my brave Clara , rescue from that vile , stealthy ghost . 
 and now , for a short time , we enjoy deep , quiet , delicious happiness . the crime which have haunt I be confess and forgive . amply possess of the mean , and even the abundance of life , I be bless with strong health again , and freedom among the free . rich and good of all blessing , I have a sweet , most lovely , and most loving wife , and love she once and for all . no more beautiful vision have any poet imagine than young Lily Vaughan sit under the vine - leaf , her form more exquisite than ever , her soft - eyed infant in her lap wonder at his mother 's beauty , while her own deep - lustred eye carry to her husband 's , without the trouble of thinking , all that flow into her heart - joy at belong to he , hope of bliss to come , fear of over - happiness , pride in all the three of we , and shame at feel proud . then a gay coquettish glance , as quick youth warm the vein , and some humorous thought occur , a tickle for the baby , and a feint of cold - shoulder I . but , jealous as I be , desperately jealous , for my love be more passionate than ever , I can honourably state that Lily 's one and only trial to arouse my jealousy be an ignominious failure , recoil only on the person of the dear designer . however exact little Harry might be , I never grudge he his double share of attention . in the first place I look upon he as a piece of I , still hold on ; and , in the next place , I know that all he lay claim to be only a loan to he , and belong in fee simple to his father . 
 at this time I write to my brother again , announce the birth of our boy , and that we have make he his namesake ; dispense , too , with all further reserve on the subject of our marriage . this letter be never deliver to your dear father . that much I know , for certain ; and at one time I strongly suspect that our cold - blooded , crafty foe contrive to intercept it . but no ; if he have , he would have know well afterwards . 
 after that cowardly onslaught upon my bride and myself , I have of course learn all I could of the history of this lepardo . he be the only son of the Signor 's only brother , but very little be know of he in the neighbourhood , as he come from Vescovato on the east side of the island . he be say to have great ability and very great perseverance , and under the guardianship of his uncle have be intend and partly educate for the French Bar . but his disposition be most headstrong and sullen ; and at an early age he display a ferocity unusual even in a Corsican . neither have he the great redeem trait of the islander , I mean their noble patriotism . one good quality , however , he do possess , and that be fidelity to his word . with one of the contradiction so common in human nature , he would even be false in order to be true : that is , he would be treacherous wherever he be unpledged , if it assist he towards a purpose to which he be commit . while he be yet a boy , his intended career be cut short by an act of horrible violence . he dislike all the low animal , horse and mule especially ; and one day he be detect by a master of the Paoli College , scream , and yell at , and lash , from a safe distance , a poor little pony whom he have tie to a fence . the master , an elderly man , very humane and benevolent , rebuke he in the most cutting manner , and call he a low coward . the young villain run off , with his eye flash fire , procure a stiletto , and stab the poor man in the back . then he leap on the horse he have be ill - treating , and prick he with the dagger , ride away furiously in the direction of Bastia . the pursuer could not trace he through the wild mountain district , but it be believe that he reach the town and take refuge in an english brig , which be lie off the harbour , and sail for Genoa that evening . the pony be find dead , lie by the roadside with the brute 's dagger in its throat . no wonder Lily , who tell I all this , with true corsican rage in her eye , no wonder my Lily hat he . even as a little girl , for she be but ten year old when he disappear , she always feel a strong repugnance towards he . he be about six year old than Fiordalisa , and four year young than I ; so when he shoot at Lily , he must have be three - and - twenty . it be report that after his disappearance he take to a sea - fare life , and make himself very useful , by his knowledge of language , in the english merchant service . quarrel with his employer , he be say to have resort to smuggle in the Levant , if not to downright piracy . 
 Clara , for reason I can not explain , I wish you to follow my story step by step in its order , note each landing - place . to do this with advantage , you must have the date carve upon each of the latter : therefore I beg you to copy they as you pass . 
 I arrive in Corsica , as you hear , during the month of May , 1829 . on the 12th of August in that same year I first behold my Lily . that day I remember , beside other reason , because I have wonder , as I ride idly along , whether my brother be open his usual Highland campaign , and whether he would like to shoot the muffrone . lily and I be marry on the 21st of March , 1830 , when I be twenty - seven year old : and our little Henry first see the light on the 24th of December follow , more than two year before your birth . your father have no child as yet , I look upon my Harry as heir presumptive to these estate . although your birth appear to divest he of the heirship , it have since , through cause then unknown to I , prove otherwise ; and if he be live now , he would in strict law be entitle to this property after my death . but if he be alive , he never should have an inch of it , that be if I could prevent it ; because in strict righteousness all belong to you . and now I hold the property in fee simple , under an Act which abolish fine and recovery ; for I have suffer so much from remorse , ever since your dear mother 's death , that even before you save my life , dearest child , I enrol a deed in Chancery , which give I dispose power ; and as I think you know , I make thereupon a will devise the land to you . this also be one of the document you catch that vile hypocrite stealing . 
 to return to the old Signor . he be now as happy as the day be long , and desirous , as an old man often be , to set on foot noteworthy scheme , which might survive his time . of this desire I take advantage to inoculate he with some english view . it be rather late to learn another catechism , at threescore year and five ; but a green old age be his , hale and hearty as could be . " why should all those noble olive shed , and rot upon the ground , all those grape of diver colour be of no more use than rainbow ? why should all the dazzle marble slumber in the quarry , the porphyry of Molo , the verde antique of Orezza , the Parian of Cassaconi , the serpentine near Bastia , and the garnet of Vizzavona - nay even the matchless white alabaster - 
 " Mother of our Lord , I have get such pretty stuff in my cavern on the gulf of Porto . some one tell I it be the very fine alabaster . but then it would require cut out . " the last thought seem a poser . 
 " well , father " - so I call he now - " when Harry have finish his tooth , suppose we go all together in the yacht and see it . " 
 and so we do ; and it be worth a voyage all the way from London only to look at it . pillar of snow , pellucid , and fancifully vein , like a glaci shot with sea - weed ; clean - work moreover , and tough , and of even texture , as I prove to my Lily 's delight . there be now a small piece in the drawer of my walnut - wood desk . but I take home a square block with I , and under my wife 's most original criticism , work it into a rough resemblance of the baby Henry . perhaps I have a natural turn for sculpture , perhaps it be a wife 's flattery ; but at any rate the young mother be so charmed with it , that in one of her pensive moment she even make I promise , that if she die soon and alone , I would have the little recumbent figure lay upon her breast . 
 meanwhile the Signor be gayer than ever : he tell we to have no anxiety about anything less than a score of child ; to such effect would he work his great olive ground , quarry , and vineyard . some ingenious plan he form , which delight he hugely , but be past my comprehension . as fast as he quarry his alabaster , he would plant young vine in the hole , and every one know how the vine delight to run away over the rock . so at once he must set off for Corte , the central town of the island , to procure a large stock of tool well - temper in the Restonica . that turbulent little river possess a magic power . its water be say to purify steel so highly that it never can rust again . I have even hear that the cutler of Northern Italy import it , for the purpose of anneal their choice production . for my part , little as I know of commerce , I strongly recommend that arrangement for shipping and sell the alabaster should be make , before it be quarried . but the Signor scorn the idea . 
 have in prospect all the rich of Croesus , and in possession enough to make we happy , and have work , as we think , uncommonly hard , we all four indulge in a tour through Sicily and Italy , propose to visit and criticise the principal marble quarry . by the time we have do all this and enjoy it thoroughly - dear I , how my wife be admire in the sculptor 's studio ! - and by the time we have fall to work again , survey and geologise all the estate , take , or rather listen to , long earful of advice , settle all our plan summarily over the Rogliano , and reopen they all the next morning , by this time , I say , nearly three year of bliss have slip by , since my recovery from the linger wound ; and it be now the summer of 1833 . my love wife be twenty year old , and we be look forward to the birth of a brother or sister for Harry . meanwhile we have hear of your birth , which delight we all , especially my Lily . she use to talk , in the fond way mother discover , to Harry , now gravely perch up on a stool , about his little sweetheart away in the dark north country . 
 it be in the month of July 1833 that the Signor find he could no long postpone his visit to Corte . alone he would go , rid his favourite jennet , as sure - footed as a mule , and as hardy as a mustang . behind he he sling his trusty fusil , with both barrel load , for he have to traverse a desert and mountainous district haunt by bandit . he be to travel through by - way to Novella , and so on to the bridge where the road from Calvi and Bastia meet , put up in rude quarter there for the night , and follow the steep descent to the town of Corte next day . in vain we beg he to take some escort , or at least to let I go with he . no , I must stop to guard the Lily and the little snow - drop ; could he possibly take I at such a time from home , and do I think a Della Croce be afraid of bandit ? it be a Monday morning when he leave the tower , and he would be back on Saturday in good time for supper . he kiss and bless his Lily , and the little snow - drop as he call young Harry , who cry at his departure ; and then he give I too an earnest trembling blessing . by this time he and I have come to love each other , as a father and a son . 
 I go with he quite to the border of the commune ; and there , in a mountain defile , I light for he his cigar . with some dark foreboding , I wait till I see he reach and pass the gap at the summit of the rise . there he turn in the saddle to wave his last adieu , and his beard , like a white cloud , float on the morning sky . 
 CHAPTER XIV . 
 on the Saturday night , an excellent supper be ready : the Signor 's own particular plate be at the head of the table , and by it gleam , in a portly bottle , his favourite Rogliano . Little Harry , who could say anything he be tell , and know right well what be good , or at least what taste good - that beloved child be allow to stop up , that grandpapa might kiss he ; this be a sovereign specific , believe in the nursery creed , to ensure sweet sleep for both . 
 that silver beard never kiss the chubby cheek again . all night we wait and wonder : Harry be send to bed roar ; no grandpapa appear . the olive rustle at midnight , and out I run ; the door creak afterwards , and I open they , all in vain ; the sound of hoof come up the valley before the break of day ; but no step or voice of man , no bark of his favourite mountain hound , no neigh of the jennet to her sleepy brother - horse . 
 all Sunday we remain in terrible uneasiness , try to cheer each other with a hundred assurance that the dear old man must have turn aside to see an ancient friend live now at Prato . when Monday morning come , but bring no tiding of he , I set off , amid a shower of tear , to seek the beloved father . the old fusileer be leave on guard , and I take two young and active man , well acquainted with the mountain pass . all well mount , and well armed , we purpose to ride hard , and search the track quite up to the town of Corte . there , if indeed he have ever arrive , we should be sure to hear of he . but it prove unnecessary to go so far from home . 
 along that dreary mountain road , often no more than a shepherd 's walk difficult to descry , we find no token of any traveller either live or dead , until we come to the Ponte Leccia , where the main road meet . here our fear be double , and the last hope nearly quench ; for on ask at the shepherd 's hut , where Signor Dezio mean to put up , we find that he have sleep there on the Friday night , as he be return from the town . the shepherd 's wife , who have know he for year , assure we that he be in wonderful spirit and health , insist upon she sup with they - which be contrary to corsican usage - and boast much of the great thing he would do , and still more of his beautiful grandson . his goatskin wallet be full of sample tool , which be to astonish his english son , and he have a toy gun no big than the tail of a dog , with which he intend to teach the baby to shoot . tell we all these little thing , and show we her present , the poor woman cry at the thought of what must have happen to he . right early on Saturday morning he set off , as impatient as a child , to see his beloved one again , and exhibit all his treasure . for love of the Della Croce her husband have groom the mare thoroughly , and she neigh merrily down the hill at thought of her stable friend . moreover , the shepherd 's wife tell we that there have be in those part no bandit worth the name , since the death of the great Teodoro , king of the mountain , whose baby still receive tribute . 
 after rest our horse awhile , with heavy heart we begin to retrace our step through that awful wilderness . instead of keep together , as we have do in the morning , we now ride in parallel line , right and leave of the desert track , wherever the ground permit it . all this district be very barren and rugge , and the way wind up and down , often along the brink of crag , or through narrow mountain gorge . the desolation and loneliness grow more oppressive , as the shadow lengthen . 
 we have thoroughly search two - third of the distance homeward , and have cross some granite height whence the sea be visible ; the sun be low over Cape Bevellata , and the vapour from the marsh be crouch at the mountain 's foot . here as I ride to the left of my two companion , I hear the faint bay of a dog far down a deep ravine , that trend leftward from our course . put my jaded animal to his utmost speed , I make for the hollow which echo the dismal sound - a feeble bark prolong into a painful howl . turn the corner sharply I scar two monstrous vulture , who be hover over and crane at a dog . the dog so gaunt and starve , that at every bark the rib seem burst the skin , still be fight past despair with his loathsome enemy . he stand across the breast of the noble Signor Dezio . there lie that gallant cavalier , stark and rigid , with his eye wide open , and his white beard tip with crimson . there he lie upon his back , his kingly head against a rock , his left hand on his clotted breast and glue thereto with blood ; his right hand hang beside his chin whence it have slip in death , and in it still securely grasp be a trinket newly make , contain a little sheaf of the baby 's flossy hair tie with a black wisp of the mother 's . the poor old man have drag himself thither to die , and die with that keepsake on his lip . the fatal shot have be fire from above , and pass completely through his body . it pierce his lung , and I believe that he feel little pain , but gasp his simple life away . near he be his wallet , with the tool still in it ; I think he have be play with the toy gun when he receive the wound ; at any rate it lie separate from the rest , and at the old man 's side . 
 examine by the wan light , with icy awe upon I , the scene of this damned atrocity , I find that the hoary traveller must have dismount here , to eat his frugal dinner . a horn cup and a crust of bread be on a rocky shelf , and a little spring well down the dingle , with the mark of the dog 's foot here and there . the craven foe have be sneak along behind , and take advantage of the old man 's position , as he sit upon a stone to make certain of he from the granite loophole . we find the very place where the murderer must have crouch , but the cliff - side keep no footprint . the victim 's gun be go , and so be the spanish mare : no other robbery seem to have be commit . 
 this glen lead to a short but more difficult track towards home , which the Signor , in his impatience , must have resolve to try . reverently we lay he on the fresh horse ; while I with the faithful mountain dog on my saddle - for he be too exhausted to walk - rode on to break the melancholy news , and send assistance back . 
 to break bad news - the phrase be a failure , the attempt it imply a much bad one . Lily know all in a moment , and in her delicate state she receive so appalling a shock , that for a week she lie on the very threshold of death . at the end of that time , and three day after the old man 's funeral - at which for his daughter 's sake I allow no wailing or voceros - a lively little girl be bear , who seem to be none the bad for her mother 's bitter suffering . her innocent caress , or some baby doing optimise by her mother - though even as a new - bear babe she seem a most love creature - all those soft endearing way , which I could not understand , do more to bring my Lily 's spirit back than even my fond attention . 
 but as yet , though able to walk again , and nurse her child , whom she would not commit to another , my wife remain in a fearfully sensitive and tremulous condition . the creak of a door , the sound of a foot , the rustle of the wind - and she , so brave and proud of yore , start like a cicale , and shake like a forest shadow . in everything she fear the ambush of that sleuth cold - blooded reptile , on whom alone , truly or not God know , she charge the blood of her venerable father . but still she have the comfort of a husband 's love , a husband even fonder than when the flower fall on his path ; and still she have the joy of watch , with a mother 's tender insight , the bud promise of two sweet infant . infant I call they , why Master Harry be now a thorough chatterbox ! with all this love around she , she by far the lovely , the pride and glory of all , be sure to find her comfort soon upon the breast of time , even as small Lily find it in her own sweet bosom . deeply and long we mourn that ancient signor , chivalrous and true gentleman , counsellor of all thing . every day we miss he ; but could talk of it more as time flow on . Rogliano have no sparkle , Luri not the tint of old : never could I pour out either from his favourite flagon , without a thought of he who teach we the proper way to do it ; who ought to be teach we still , but be lie foully murder in his lonely grave at St. Katharine 's on the Cliff . 
 we have do our utmost to avenge he : soon as I could leave my wife , I have scour all the neighbourhood . the Sbirri too have do their good , but discover nothing . brave fellow they be , when it come to fighting , but very poor detective . only two thing we hear that seem at all significant . one of these be that a spanish jennet , like the Signor 's favourite " Marana , " but dreadfully jade and nearly starve , have be sell on the Friday after the murder , be the very day of the funeral , at the town of Porto Vecchio on the south - eastern coast . I send my coxswain Petro , an intelligent and trusty Corsican , to follow up this clue ; for I durst not leave my wife as yet . Petro discover the man who have buy the mare , and re - purchase she from he , as I have direct : but the description of the first seller do not tally with my recollection of Lepardo . however , it prove to be the true Marana ; and glad she be to get home once more . 
 the other report , that seem to bear upon the bloody mystery , be that a swift felucca , flush - build and bank for triple sweep , have be see lie close in shore near point Girolata , during the early part of the week in which the Signor leave home . and it be even say that two maltese sailor , belong to this felucca , have encamp on shore in a lonely place near Otta , and be likely to be find there still . 
 Lily be strong now , I determine to follow this last clue myself ; and so I put the little yacht into commission again , and man she with Calvi man , for all my english crew have be dismiss long ago . leave my wife and child under the care of the old fusileer , away I sail from St. Katharine 's , intend to return in three day ' time . all this coast I now know thoroughly , and Otta be not far beyond the poor Signor 's cave of alabaster . it be a wild and desert region , far away from any frequented road , and little visit except by outlaw . 
 we find no trace of any tent , no sign of any landing , and an aged fisherman , whom we meet , declare that no felucca or vessel of any sort have lately be near the bay . I begin to fear that , for some dark purpose , I have be beguile from home , and despatch upon a fool 's errand . the dreary coast be still the home of solitude , the alabaster cave untouched since our pic - nic survey ; the mark of which be undisturbe except by wind and weather . so I crowd sail , and hurry back to St. Katharine 's , with a strange weight on my heart . to add to my vexation , a strong north wind set in , and smartly as our cutter sail , we be force to run off the land . when at last we make the cove , it be unsafe for the yacht to anchor , and so I be compel to send she on to Calvi . 
 it be nearly midnight on the 2d of October , when Petro and myself plod up the woode hill on which the old tower stand . weary and dispirit , though glow every now and then with the thought of all my darling , in vain I call myself a fool for fear where no fear be . when we reach the brow of the hill , my vague alarm be double . the rude oil - lamp that mark the entrance , why be they unlighted ? I have especially order that they should be kindle every night , and Lily have promise to see to it herself . no challenge from the watchman , no click of the musket hammer , even the vinea be not in its place . in vain we knock and knock at the old chesnut door ; no one answer , no one come to open . none of the loophole show a light ; the house be dark and silent as the ivy . wild with terror I run to the little side - door , whence first my Lily meet I . this too be lock , or fasten somehow ; and only the echo of my knock be hear . Petro and I catch up a great bough of ilex , which myself have lop last week , rush at the door with the butt , and break it in with one blow . shriek for Lily , Lily , I fly from room to room , tumble over the furniture , blunder at the doorway . no voice of wife , no cry of child , no answer of domestic ; all as silent as if ten fathom under water . 
 have dash through every room , I turn to rush off to the hamlet , when my foot strike something - something soft and yielding ; be it a sack or bolster ? I stoop to feel it ; it be Lily , lay out , stiff and cold Dead , my Lily dead ! oh , God can never mean it ; would he let I love she so ? 
 for all intent of actual life , for all that we be make for , for all the soul 's loan of this world , I die that very moment ; and yet a mad life burn within I , the flare of hope that will not die . how I force she clench hand open , bow her rigid arm around I , throw myself upon she , breathe between her lip and listen , tear her simple dress asunder and lay my cheek upon her heart ; feel not a single throb , flood her cold breast with tear , and lie insensible awhile . then , as if awake , feel that she be with I , but somehow not as usual ; call she all our name of love , and believe we be in heaven . but there stand Petro with a light , sobbing , and how his beard shake ! - what right have he in heaven ? would they let he in without shaving ? I rise to order he out ; when he restore my wit awhile by point with his finger . 
 " look , look , Signor ! she be not dead , I see her eyelid tremble . " 
 wide she open those glorious eye , look at I with no love in they , shudder , and close they again . 
 Mad with rapture , I catch she up , send Petro headlong lamp and all , and kiss she enough to kill she . she be not dead , my Lily , my pet of eternal age . there she fall tremble , flutter , nestle in my arm , her pale cheek on my breast , her white hand on my shoulder ; then frighten at her nest shrink back , and gaze with unutterable reproach , where love like the fall lamp be flicker : then clung to I once more , as if she ought to hate , but could not yet help love . she die the next morning . Clara , I can not   tell you any more now . 
 CHAPTER XV . 
 before my own and only love depart , she know , thank God , she know as well as I do , that I have never wrong her pure and true affection . but it be long before I learn what have so distressed she . though she appear quite sensible , and look at I , every now and then , with the same reproachful harrowing gaze , it seem to I age , it must have be hour , before she could frame her thought in word . in an agony of suspense for she , for our child , for our love , I could hardly repress my impatience even at her debility . many a time she open her trembling lip , but the word die on they . at last I catch her meaning from a few broken sentence . 
 " how could he do it ? how could he so betray she ? and his own Lily that love he so - no , she must not be Lily any more , she be only Fiordalisa Della Croce . how could he come and pretend to love she , and pretend to marry she , when all the while he have a young wife at home in England ? never would she have believe it but for the proof , the proof that hateful man have show she . how could he shame his own love so , and his child , and the aged father - there be no hope for she but to die - to die and never see he more ; and then perhaps he would be sorry , for he must care about she a little . " 
 then she burst into such a torrent of tear , and press both hand on her bound heart , and grow white with terror . then as the palpitation pass , she look at I and know I , and creep close to I , forget all the evil , - and seem to sleep awhile . of course I see what it be ; daze as I be and wild at her sorrow and danger , I slowly perceive what it be . the serpent - like foe have be there , and have hiss in her ear what he think to be true - that I have do she a dastard 's wrong ; have win her passionate maiden love , and defile she by a sham marriage , while my lawful wife be live . 
 when once I know my suppose offence , it do not take long to explain the murderer 's error , an error which have spring from my own deceit . but my child , where be my child , Lily ? 
 in her ecstatic joy , she could not think for the moment even of her child ; but press I to her tumultuous heart , as if I be all she want . then she begin to revile herself , for dare to believe any ill of her noble husband .
