 " and even if it have be true , which you know it never could be , dear , - I must have forgive you , sweetest darling , because you could not   have help it , you do love I so , do not   you ? " 
 this sweet womanly logic , you , Clara , may comprehend - but where be the child , my Lily ? 
 " oh , in bed I suppose , dear : let I get up , we must go and kiss the darling . when I first come in , I could not bear to go near they , poor pet ; but now - oh my heart , holy Madonna , my heart ! " 
 she leap up as if she be shoot , and a choke sound rise in her throat .. her fresh youth fight hard in the clutch of death . " oh save I , my own husband , save I . hold I tight ; I can not die yet . so young and so happy with you . it be go ; but the next pang be death . hold I so till it come again . God bless you , my own for ever . you will find I in heaven , will not   you ? you can never forget your own Lily . " 
 her large eye rest on mine , as they do when she first own her love ; and her soul seem try to spring into the breast of mine . close to I she clung , but with less and less of strength . her smooth , clear cheek be on mine , her exhausted heart on my wild one . I feel its last throb , as the death - pang come , and she try to kiss I to show that it be not violent . frantic , I open my lip , and receive the last breath of her . 
 the crush of its anguish her heart might have bear , but not the rebound of its joy . 
 her body , the fairest the sun ever see , be lay beside her father 's in the little churchyard at St. Katharine 's , with the toy baby on her breast ; her soul , the most loving and playful that ever the angel visit , be still in attendance upon I , and mourn until mine rejoin it . 
 you have hear my great but not my only distress . for more than three month , my reason forsook I utterly . I recognise no one , not even myself , but seek high and low for my Lily . at night I use to wander forth and search among the olive - tree , where we so often rove : sometimes the form I know so well would seem to flit before I , tempt I on from bole to bole , and stretch vain hand towards I . then as I seem to have overtake and bring to bay her coyness , with a faint shriek she would vanish into hazy air . probably I owe these vision to capricious memory , gleam upon old hexameter of the Eton clink . true from false I know not , neither care to know : everything I do seem to be do in sleep , with all the world around I go to sleep as well . one vague recollection I retain of go somewhere , to do something that make I creep with cold . this must have be the funeral of my lost one ; when the Corsicans , as I be tell , flee from my ghastly stare , and would only stand behind I . they be a superstitious race , and they fear the " evil eye . " 
 all the time I be in this state , faithful Petro wait on I , and watch I like a father . he send for his wife , old Marcantonia , who be fame for her knowledge of herb and her power over the witch , who now beyond all doubt have get I in possession . decoction manifold she give I at the turn of the moon , and hang I all over with amulet , till I ring like a peal of cracked bell . in spite of all these sovereign charm , Lepardo might at any time have murder I , if he have think I happy enough to deserve it . perhaps he be in some other land , make sure of my child 's life . 
 poor helpless darling , all that be leave I of my Lily , as yet I do not know that even they be take . Petro tell I afterwards that I have ask for they once or twice , in a vacant wondering manner , but have be quite content with some illusory answer . 
 it be my Lily , and no one else , who bring I back to conscious life . what I be about to tell may seem to you a feeble brain 's chimera ; and so it would appear to I , if relate by another . but though my body be exhaust by unsleepe sorrow , under whose strain the mental chord have yield , yet I assure you that what befell I do not flow from but sweep aside both these enervation . 
 it be the Corsican 's belief , that those whom he have deeply mourn , and desolately miss , be allow to hover near he in the silent night . then sometimes , when he be sleep , they will touch his lid and say , " weep no more , belove one : in all , except thy sorrow , we be bless as thou couldst wish . " or sometimes , if the parting be of still more tender sort ,   as between two lover , or a newly wed couple   in the depth of darkness when the lone survivor can not sleep for trouble , appear the lose one at the chamber door , hold it open , and call softly ; " Dearest , come ; for I as well be lonely . " have thrice implore , it wave its cerement like an angel 's wing , and await the answer . answer not , if you wish to live ; however the sweet voice thrill your heart , however that heart be break . but if you truly wish to die , and hope be quench in memory ; make answer to the well - know voice . within three day you will be dead , and flit beside the invoke shadow . 
 perhaps old Marcantonia have warn I of this appeal , and beg I to keep silence , which for my child 's sake I be bind to do . all I know be that one night towards the end of January , I lay awake as usual , think - if a mind distemper thus can think - of my own sweet Lily . all the evening I have seek she among the olive - tree , and at St. Katharine 's Church , and even on the sad sea - shore by the moaning of the wave . now the winter moon be high , and through the embrasured window , the far churchyard that hold my wife , and the silver sea beyond it , glimmer like the curtain of another world . sit up in the widow bed , with one hand on my ache forehead - for now I breathe perpetual headache - I call in question that old church of one gay wedding and two dark funeral . be there any such church at all ; be it not a dream of moonlight and the phantom love ? 
 even as I sit gaze now , so on many a moonlight night sit my Lily gaze with I , whispering of her father 's grave , and look for it in the shrouded distance . her little hand use to quiver in mine , as she declare she have find it ; and her dark eye have so wondrous a gift of sight , that I never would dare to deny , though I could not quite believe it . have she not in the happy day , when we roam on the beach together , wait for the yacht and pretend to seek shell , have she not then tell I the stripe and colour of the sailor ' cap , and even the name of the man on deck , when I could hardly see their figure ? 
 ah , could she tell my own name now , could she descry I from that shore which mock the range of telescope , and the high lens of thought ; be she permit one glimpse of he from whom in life she could hardly bear to withdraw those gentle eye ? answer I , my own , in life and death my own one ; tell I that you watch and love I , though it be but now and then , and not enough to break the by - law of the disembodied world . 
 calmly as I now repeat it , but in a low melodious tone , sweet than any mortal 's voice , a tone that dwelt I know not where , like the sighing of the night - wind , come this answer to I : 
 " true love , for our child 's sake , and mine who watch and love you still , quit this grief , the spirit 's grave . all your sorrow still be mine , and would you vex your darling , when you can not comfort she ? though you see I now no more , I be with you more than ever ; I be your image and your shadow . at every sigh of your , I shiver ; your smile be all my sunshine . let I feel some sunshine , sweetest ; you know how I use to love it , and as yet you have send I none . I shall look for some to - morrow . Lo I , for ever your , be smile on you now . " 
 and a golden light , rich than any sunbeam , ripple through the room . I know the soft gleam like the sunset on a harvest - field . it be my Lily 's smile . a glow of warmth be shed on I , and I fall at once into a deep and dreamless sleep . you , my child , who have never know such loss - pray God you never may - very likely you regard all this incident as a dream . be it so : if it be a dream , Lily 's angel bring it . 
 CHAPTER XVI . 
 the next day I be a different man . all my energy have return , and all my reasoning power ; but not , thank God , the rigour of my mind , the petty contempt of my fellow - man . nothing be more hard to strip than that coat of flinty closeness form upon Deucalion 's offcast in the petrify well of self . though I have do my utmost , and pray of late for help in do it , never have I quite scale off this accursed deposit . this it be that so estrange your warm nature , Clara ; a nature essentially like your father 's , but never allow free scope . you could not tell the reason , child never can ; but somehow it make you shiver to be in contact with I . 
 Petro and Marcantonia would have be astonish at my sudden change , but they have lately dose I with some narcotic herb , procure , by a special expedition , from the Monte Rotondo , and esteem a perfect stregomastix ; so of course the worthy pair expect my recovery . no long do they attempt to conceal from I the truth as to my poor infant , who have be carry off on the day of my return . what I learn of the great calamity , which then befell I , be this . 
 towards sunset , my dear wife , with her usual fondness , go forth to look for the little yacht return from the gulf of Porto . our darling Harry , then in his third year , be with she , and the young nurse from Muro . Lily sit upon the cliff , watch a sail far in the offing , probably our vessel . then as she turn towards the tower , a man from the shrubbery stand before she , and call she by her maiden name . she know her cousin Lepardo , and suppose that he be come to kill she . nevertheless she ask he proudly how he dare to insult she so , in the presence of her child and servant . he answer that it be her name , and she be entitle to no other . then he promise not to harm she , if she would send the maid away , for he have important thing to speak of . and thereupon he lay before her document and letter . 
 meanwhile the tower be surround by his comrade ; but they durst not enter , for the trusty fusileer keep the one approach up the steep hillside ; and his grandson , a brave boy , stand at the loop - hole with he . the maid , however , with her little charge , be allow to pass , and she join the two other woman in weak preparation for defence . the period of attack have be choose skilfully . so simple and patriarchal be the corsican mode of life , that very few servant be keep , even by man of the high station ; and those few be not servant in our sense of the word . it happen this night that the only two man employ upon the premise , beside the old fusileer , have be send into the town for thing wherewith to welcome I . 
 however , the faithful gunner , with his eye along the barrel , keep the foe at bay , and seem likely to keep they there , until the return of the man ; while his sturdy grandson split his red cheek at the warder 's conch . but they little know their enemy . Lepardo Della Croce be not to be baulk by an old man and a boy . at the narrow entrance a lady 's dress come flutter in the brisk north wind . poor Lily totter across the line of fire , her life she never think of ; what use to live after all that she have hear ? close behind she , and in the dusk invisible past her wind - toss drapery , steal her scoundrel cousin ; whom , like tree set in a row , or feather - edge board see lengthwise , a score of lithe and active sailor follow . no chance for the marksman ; like tile they overlap one another , and poor Lily , upright in her outraged pride , cover the stoop graduated file . french and English , Moorish and Maltese , a motley band as ever swore , they burst into a hearty laugh at the old gunner 's predicament , the moment they have pass his range . all within be at their mercy . true he keep the main gate still , and all the door be bar ; but gate and door be lubber 's hole for seaman such as they . up the ivy they clamber , along the chesnut branch , or the mere coigne of the granite , and into the house they pour at every loop - hole and window . one thing must be say in their favour - they do very little mischief . they be keep thoroughly under command , and a wave of their captain 's hand drive they anywhither . all he want be possession of my child , and of some valuable property which he claim in right of his father . 
 have secure both object , he order his man to depart , allow they only to carry what wine and provision they find . but the three domestic , and the ancient sentinel and his boy , be bind hand and foot , and conceal in a cave on the beach , to prevent any stir in the neighbouring hamlet . Poor Lily be leave where she fall , to recover or not , as might be . my own darling be not insult or touch ; the man be afraid , and Lepardo too proud to outrage one of his kin . moreover , his word be pledge ; and they say that he always keep it . soon after dark the robber set sail , and slip away down the coast , before that strong north wind which have so baffle I . but for I a letter be leave , full of triumph and contumely . it be address to " Valentine Vaughan , the Englishman ; " " Signor Valentine " be the title confer on I by the fusileer , and adopt by the neighbourhood . to my surprise that letter be write in English , and English as good as a foreigner ever indite : I can repeat it word for word : - 
 " SIR , - I be reluctant to obtrude good counsel , but with the obtuseness of your nation you be prone to the undervaluing of other . it be my privilege to amend this error , while meekly I revindicate my own neglect right . from I you have steal my bride and my good inheritance , and in a manner which the person unverse in human nature would be incline to characterise as dastardly and dissolute . furthermore , you have render the heiress of the noble house in Corsica a common Englishman 's adulteress . if I have hear this on the day of your mocking marriage , not the poor victim but you , you , would have be my direction . now I will punish you more gradually , and long , as you deserve . your unhappy adulteress know the perfidy of your treachery , and your two poor bastard shall take refuge with I . the inquiry with respect to my drown they to - night be dependent upon the star . but if I shall spare they , as I may , because they can not come between I and my property , I will teach they , when they be old enough , to despise and loathe your name . they shall know that in the stead of a father 's love they have only have a vagabond 's lust , and they shall know how you seduce and then slew their mother ; for death , in my humble opinion , appear in her face to - night . although she have betray I , I be regretful for she : but to you who have disgrace my name and plunder I , as a man of liberal and exalted view I grant a contemptuous forbearance ; so long , that be to say , as you remain unhappy , which the wicked ought to be . of one thing , however , I bid you to take admonishment . if I hear that you ever forget this episode of debauchery , and return to your english wife and property , no house , no castle that ever be edify , shall protect you from my dagger . remember the one thing , as your proverb tell , I be slow and sure . 
 lepardo DELLA CROCE . " 
 CHAPTER XVII . 
 instead of enrage or madden I , as the writer perhaps expect , this execrable letter do I a great deal of good . I determine to lower that insufferable arrogance ; and bring all my thought to bear upon one definite object , the recovery of my darling and the punishment of that murderer . I do not believe that he have destroy they , or be likely to do so ; for have not their mother 's spirit refer to they as live ? 
 without delay , my yacht be prepare for a lengthened cruise ; the tower commit to Marcantonia and the gray sentinel ; and with Petro for my skipper , I sail on the following day . alas , the three month now elapse during my delirium , have they not like the sea itself close across the track ? all the neighbour know be this , the felucca have pass Point Girolata , and have be see in the early morning , stand away due south . all the villager , and even the man from the mountain , throng the shore as I embark , and there invoke Madonna 's blessing on poor Signor Valentine , so basely rob of wife and child . 
 when we have round Girolata , we bear away due south , and in less than fifteen hour make the sardinian shore in the gulf of Asinara . here we coast along the curve , inquire at every likely place whether any such vessel have be sight as that which we be seek . but we could learn nothing of she until we be off the Gypsum Cape ; where some fisherman tell we , that at or about the time we speak of , a swift felucca , build and man exactly as we have describe , glide by they and bear up for the town of Alghero . we too bear up for Alghero , and soon discover that the roving vessel have undoubtedly be there : even Lepardo , the captain , be describe by the keen Sardinians . but she have only lain to for a few hour , and clear again for Cagliari . for Cagliari we make sail as hard as the stick could carry , and arrive there on the fourth day from Cape Girolata . 
 the pirate , if such they be , have offer their vessel for sale at Cagliari ; but , fail of a satisfactory price have sail away again , and after much trouble I find out that their destination be Valetta . to Valetta also we follow , feel like a new boy at school who be mystify by the expert - innocent of much Greek themselves - with a game which mean in English , ' send the fool on far . " 
 when at length we reach the maltese capital - where I be not sorry to hear once more my native tongue - we find the felucca snugly moor near the " Merchant 's yard , " and be refit as a pleasure - boat for a wealthy Englishman . this gentleman know a good deal about ship , but not quite enough . please with the graceful line and clean run of the felucca , he have give nearly twice her value for she ; as he soon perceive when the ship - carpenter set to work . he be in the vein to afford all possible information , be thoroughly furious with the condemnable pirate - as he call they , without the weakness of the composite verb - who have rob he so shamefully of his money . he tell I that my child have be ashore , and Harry be much admire and kiss in the Floriana . one thing the sailor do which would have surprise a man unacquainted with the Corsicans , or perhaps I should say the islander of the Mediterranean . they deck my little babe with flower and riband , and bear she in procession to the church of St. John of Jerusalem ; and there they have she baptize , for Lepardo have find out that she have never undergo the ceremony . I be anxious to see the record , but be not allow to do so ; therefore I do not know what the little darling 's name be , if she be still alive : but they tell I that the surname enter be not Vaughan , but Della Croce . it be say that the sailor have become very fond of she , the little creature be very sweet - temper and happy , and a pleasing novelty to they . very likely they name she after their own felucca . 
 the crew be now disperse , some to their home , and some on board ship which have sail , I be throw completely off the scent . all I could learn , at a house which they have frequent , be that Lepardo , the commander , have long ago leave the island . whither , or in what ship , he have sail , they could not or would not tell I : he have always plenty of money , they say , and he spend it like a prince . but Petro , who be a much well ferret than I , discover , or seem to have do so , that the kidnapper and murderer have take passage for Naples . my heart fall when I hear it ; almost as easily might I have track he in London . at Naples I have spend a month , and know the lie ingenuity , the laziness in all but lie , of its swarm thousand . however , the little yacht be again put under way , and , after a tedious passage , we see the queen of city . here , as I expect , the pursuit be baffle . 
 I will not weary you with my wandering , off more often than on the track , up and down the Mediterranean , and sometimes far inland . if I mark they on a map , however large the scale , you would have what child call a crinkly crankly puzzle , like Lancashire in Bradshaw . once , indeed , I rest at the ancient tower , near my Lily 's grave , which I always visit twice in every year . I have some vague idea , now in my old age , that though we vaughan det any display of feeling - except indeed at time when the heart be too big for the skin - we be in substance , without know it , a most romantic race . whether we be that , or not , be matter of small moment ; one thing be quite certain , we be strut well and stable . we be not quick of reception , but we be most retentive . never be there man of we who ever love a woman and cast she off through weariness ; never be there woman of our house who play the jilt , when once she have pass the pledge of love . and after all I have see of the world , and through my dark misfortune few man have see more , it be my set conclusion that strong tenacity be the foremost of all the virtue . my enemy have it , I freely own , and through all his wickedness it save he from be contemptible . 
 for a time , as I say before , I pause from my continual search , and abode in the old gray tower . that search now appear so hopeless , that I be half inclined to believe no well policy could be find than this . some day or other the robber would surely return and lay claim to the land of the Della Croce . at present he durst not do it , while under the ban of piracy and the suspicion of his uncle 's murder . moreover , I think it my duty to see to the welfare of my child 's property . under the deed - poll of the old Signor , his friend at Prato and myself be trustee and guardian . but I could not live there long : it be too painful for I to sit alone in the desolate room where my child ought to be toddle , or to wander through the shrubbery and among the untended flower , every one of they whisper " Lily . " formerly I have admire and love that peculiar stillness , that rich deep eloquent solitude , which mantle in bucolic gray the lawn and glade of Corsica . but when I so admire and love , I be a happy man , a man who have affection near he , and could warm himself when he please . now though I have no friend or friendship , neither care for any , solitude strike I to the bone , because it seem my destiny . 
 after strive for half a year to do my duty as a hermit Signor , I find myself , one dreary morning , finger my pistol gloomily , and fit a small bullet into my ear . my thumb catch in the guard of the Signor 's locket , and jerk it up my waistcoat . it be the same which the poor old man have press to his die lip . there be Lily 's hair and Harry 's , and a tiny wisp of down since add , belong to baby - name unknown . look at they and see how Lily 's bind they together and to I , I feel ashamed of my cowardly gloom , and resolve to quit myself like a man in my duty towards the three . I ride at once to Prato , and persuade Count Gaffori to come and live at the tower . like his old friend the Signor , he have only himself and his lovely daughter to think of ; but unlike Signor Dezio he have lose nearly all his paternal property , through political trouble . therefore it be for he no little comfort and advantage to be place at the head of a household again , and restore to some worldly importance . nevertheless , his sense of honour be so nice and exact , that I think I should never succeed in bring he to my view ; and indeed I must have fail but for his daughter 's assistance . a very sweet elegant girl she be , and she have be a great friend of my Lily 's . if I could ever have love again , I should have love that maiden : but the thing be impossible . 
 the old Count promise to come and settle at Veduta tower - which name , in light day , I have corrupt into " Vendetta " - and live there to assume the management of the estate , in trust for my lost infant , as soon as his arrangement could be make . I see nothing that need have delay he a day ; however , he declare that he must have a month to get ready , and he be plainly a man whom nature mean not to be push . so I employ the interval in have my dear old " Lilyflower " overhaul at Marseilles , copper , and thoroughly paint . I could not bear to alter our little love - boat , as my darling call it , even in outward appearance ; but like our love she have labour through many a temp ; unlike it , she need repair . however , I save from the painter 's brush our favourite quarter - deck bench , whereon through the moonlight watch my Lily seem still to recline . 
 and so my life for some year wander on , a worthless , unsettled , forlorn existence , only refresh at interval by return to the scene of past happiness . if I have really wrong Lepardo Della Croce , he could hardly have wish for a well revenge . but in truth I have never wrong he . even if I have never come near his betrothed , it be quite certain she would not have accept he . and he , by his own desertion , have leave she free to choose . 
 late in the autumn of 1812 , when I have abandon all hope of ever recover my little one , except by one of those eddy of Providence , which we man call accident , and in which I place my confidence to this hour , at that season , I say , I land at Gibraltar , be wind - bind in the strait . we be make for Lisbon , where I be to ship some english watch , gun , and fine cutlery for Ajaccio . what a loss of rank for the " Lilyflower , " to turn she into a trading smack ! well , I could not see it so ; and I be sure her late mistress , who with all her sweet romance be an excellent hand at a bargain , would have think it far more below my dignity for I to sponge on our child . there be plenty of money in hand at Veduta tower ; but have retire from stewardship , I do not feel myself justify in draw upon my child . therefore , and for the sake of the large acquaintance and great opportunity gain , I have renew my connection with the firm of Green , Vowler , and Green . somehow , I could not bear to revisit the shore of England ; otherwise I be sure that with the knowledge I now possess of the Mediterranean port , and a house of such standing and enterprise to back I , I should quickly have make my fortune . my vessel , moreover , be much too small for the fruit - trade , even if I could have lower she to an uncleanly freight ; but she be just the craft for valuable good in small compass . I know the Corsican fondness for arm and first - rate cutlery ; and the tool the poor Signor Dezio mean to astonish I with , certainly do astonish I by their wonderful badness . true , the material be good , but all the water of the Restonica will not convert a hammer into a handsaw . although hardware be not at all in his line of business , Peter Green most kindly undertake to send I a cargo of first - rate Sheffield and Birmingham good , by a return fruit - schooner . these , consign to his Lisbon agent , I could fetch away , as I please , or want they . have arrange with a shrewd merchant of Ajaccio to take my good wholesale , and save the dignity of all the Vogheni from haggle , I have already make six trip , and in spite of the most tyrannical douane perhaps in all the world , I as a Corsican , import good in a Corsican bottom , have clear very nearly three hundred per cent . on my outlay . we be now on our seventh voyage , to reship the last of the second english consignment , when a violent gale from the west meet we right in the tooth , and we be force to bear up for the anchorage . a first - rate sea - boat the " Lilyflower " be , although she have be build for racing , and for two or three year have beat all competitor , whenever there be wind enough for a cat to stand on the sheet . but one hot June day she get beat in a float match , when the light bung go fast , and her prig of a " noble owner " sell she in disgust , and build a thing that draw as much water as a nautilus . in she he be happily upset , and could hardly find a sheet of paper to hold on by . know some little about yacht , from my pool and reach experience , I buy the famous racing cutter at about a quarter her value ; and even in these , her olden day , she could exhibit her taffrail to the smart fruit - clipper - the name be then just invent - that ever race for the Monument . her register be fifty ton , but she carry eighty . 
 land at Gibraltar , I keep clear of my countryman , not that I dislike they , but because - well I can not tell why ; and stroll away to the spanish and moorish quarter . 
 it be a windy evening , and in front of a low refreshment house some sailor and spanish girl be dance . a squabble arise among they ; something I think it be about a young girl 's dress . knife be draw , and two man be stab in less than the time I be speak . I just save the life of one , just save it by half an inch . a fine - look Spaniard lie under a Moor , who have trip he up in their quick way . the point of the knife have flash through the Spaniard 's shirt and his flesh be cut , before the swing of my stick - upwards luckily - have jerk the Moor off his body . if I have strike downward , or a millionth part of a second later , the blade would have stand in the heart . but I know those fellow by this time . the Moor lie senseless from the quick upper - cut on his temple , and the knife be quiver where the impulse have fail it . 
 now if Petro and I hold deliberate choice - " proairesis " Oxford call it - not to be turn into knife - sheath , our only chance of develop into action that undeniable process of " nous , " be to be find in the policy , vulgarly call " cut and run . " at a shrill signal , from ship and from shore , the Moors come swarm silently and swiftly . their yellow slipper and coffee - colour leg seem set upon spring by excitement . some of the Spaniards stand bravely by we , and with their aid we hurry the wound man into our boat , and push off just in time . unlike the corsican peasant , our pursuer carry no fire - arm , and before they could get any , we be at safe distance . 
 have send for an english surgeon , we keep the poor sailor on board the yacht , until he be quite out of danger . we Britons be not , as a general rule , an over - grateful race ; we hate to be under an obligation , and too often illustrate the great philosopher 's saying , that the doer feel more good will than the receiver of a kindness . moreover , the Spaniards , in the neighbourhood of the Rock , could hardly be expect to love we , even if we be accustom , which it be needless to say we be not , to treat they with decent courtesy . therefore I be surprised at the deep and warm gratitude of this wound man . a thing that enhance his debt to I - for life , in my opinion , be very little to owe - be that he love a young girl , the one over whom they have quarrel , and he be about to marry she . 
 discover who I be , for he know nothing of I at first , he see that he could be of no little service to I . the only obstacle be a solemn oath ; but from this , he believe , he could soon obtain release . with an Englishman 's honest and honourable repugnance to any breach of faith , I be long reluctant to encourage this absolution . but the thought of my helpless child , rob of their inheritance , and , still bad , of a father 's love , and dependant on the caprice of a superstitious villain , this , and the recollection of my desolate wrong , overpower all scruple . and be it not a wise course , and more truly Christian , to port the helm than to cross the bow of another man 's religion , at any rate so long as it be Christian also , though frog in a pensioner 's coat ? 
 be duly absolve - for which he would not allow I to pay - the spanish sailor tell I all he know . he have be Lepardo 's mate , on many a smuggling run , and in many an act of piracy off the coast of Barbary . but he have never like his captain , no one ever do ; though all the crew admire he as the clever man in the world . after the felucca be sell and her crew disperse , the mate have follow for a while the fortune of Lepardo . he tell I thing about he which I know not how to believe . however , I will not repeat they , because they do not seem to bear upon my story . the name of my little girl he could not remember , for he be not at the christening , and she be always call the baby . be a good - natured man he take kindly to the child , and tell I anecdote of they which bring the tear to my eye . 
 after two or three month spend at Naples , they all leave suddenly for Palermo , on account , as the mate believe , of my unexpected arrival ; and here he lose sight of his commander , for tired by this time of an idle life , and see no chance of any more roving adventure , he accept a berth in a brig bind for the Piraus , and now after many shift and change be first mate of a fruit vessel sailing from Zante to London . the most important part to I of all his communication be that , on their previous voyage , they have carry to England Lepardo Della Croce and my two dear child . that murderer and kidnapper have take the lead in some conspiracy against the government of the two Sicilies , and through the treachery of an accomplice have be oblige to fly for his life . disguise himself he contrive to reach Gibraltar , and take refuge on english ground . he be now very poor and in great distress , but still clung to the child , of whom he appear to be fond , and who believe he to be their father . the " Duo brachione " touch there , as usual , for supply , Lepardo meet his old mate ashore ; and beg for a passage to England . they take he to London , and there of course lose sight of he . he be greatly alter , the mate say , from the lepardo of old . morose and reserve he have always be ; but now misfortune have cover he with a skin - deep philosophy . but his eye contract and sparkle as of yore , whenever my name be mention ; and the mate know what his intention be , in case he should find I a happy man . the simple mate be still more surprised at the alteration in my child ; as pretty a pair , he say , as ever he set eye on . but they be keep most jealously from the notice of the crew , and even from their ancient friend 's attention ; they be never allow to be on the deck , except when the berth be be clean . they seem to fear their reputed father , a great deal more than they love he . 
 upon hear this last particular I seize the mate by the hand , and feel something rise in my throat : I be so delighted to learn that the pirate have not succeed in carry nature by boarding . the next day I leave Petro to see to the hardware business - to which we be bind by charter - while I set sail in the " Duo Brachiones " for the arm of my darling little one . 
 CHAPTER XVIII . 
 they put I in the very hammock where that murderer of all my happiness have sleep , and no wonder that I could find no rest there . soon as I know the reason , I be allow to change , and creep into the little berth where my innocent pet have lie in each other 's arm . here I sleep much well than a king , for I even fancy that it smell of Lily . if little Lily , as she shall be call , whatever the rogue have christen she , if my little beauty - for that I be sure she must be - ever come to light , when I be in my grave , remember one thing , Clara , you will find her breath and general fragrance just as her mother 's be . such thing be hereditary , especially among woman . 
 after a long and stormy passage , and a fortnight spend in repair at Bordeaux , we pass the familiar Essex marsh by night , and be off the Custom House by the last day of the year . when that tedious work be over - talk as we please of the douane , our own be as bad as most of they - feel quite out of my latitude , and not a bit like an Englishman , I betook myself to a tavern near London Bridge . there everything seem new , and I could not walk the street without yaw into the wrong tide . but one old London custom hold its ground with time . paper a week and a fortnight old still stray about in the coffee - room . be tell that the journal of that day be " in hand , " as they always be , I take up a weekly paper of some ten day back to yawn over it till supper time . it be too late for I to think of disturb Peter Green by a sudden arrival , and so I have order a bed at this hotel . 
 the weekly gazette in my hand be one of those which use the shear with diligence and method . under the head " Provincial News , " I find the following paragraph : - 
 " SEASONABLE BENEVOLENCE . - we understand that in these time of severe and unmerited pressure upon the agricultural interest - the true back - bone of old England - the head of one of our most ancient and respected county family have announce his intention of remit to all his tenantry no less than twenty per cent . upon their rental . he have also bespeak a lavish and most princely repast - shall we say dinner - to be provide on Christmas eve for every man , woman , and child upon his large domain . when we announce that mine host of the Elephant be to be major domo , and our respected townsman George Jenkins , who purchase as our reader be aware the gold medal ox at Smithfield , be to cater for the occasion , need we say anything more ? at the risk of gratuitous insult to the intelligence of the county , we must subjoin that the honour gentleman to whom we allude be Henry Valentine Vaughan , Esquire , of Vaughan Park . be not such a man , the representative of time - honour sentiment , and who to a distinguished degree add the experience of continental travel , be not such a man , we ask , a thousand time fitter to express in the Senate the opinion and wish of this great county , than the scion , we have almost say spawn , of the Manchester mushroom , whom a Castle that shall be nameless be attempt to foist on the county ? we pause for a reply . - Gloucester Argus . " 
 my dear brother 's distinguished degree be that of B.A. after a narrow escape from pluck . Clara , do not   look offend . your father have very good ability , but spend most of his Oxford time in pigeon match at the Weirs , and expedition to Bagley wood , which later in life he would have look upon as felonious . 
 this paltry puff would never have be reprint by a London journal of eminence and influence , but for the suggestion at the end , which happen just to hit the sentiment of the more exalted editor . now this weekly paper be sure to circulate among refugee from the continent , by reason of its well - know antipathy towards they ; and there happen to be in this very number a violent tirade against our Government for display what we delight to call the mighty Agis of England . I see the danger at once , and my heart turn sick within I . my gay and harmless brother in the midst of his Christmas rejoicing , and a stealthy murderer creep perhaps at that very moment towards he . 
 but even if it be so , be there not some chance of Lepardo discover his mistake , when in the neighbourhood where the Vaughans be so well know ? yes , some chance there be , but very little . bind upon such an errand he would not dare to show himself , or to make any inquiry , even if they seem needful . and the mention by that cursed gossip of what he call " continental travel " - your father 's wedding tour - would banish all doubt of identity , have any be entertain . even suppose that cold - blooded fiend should meet my poor brother , face to face , in the open daylight , it be not likely that he would be undeceive . Lepardo and I have meet only once , and then in hot encounter . my brother be like I in figure , in face , and in voice ; and though I be somewhat tall and much dark of complexion , the former difference would not attract attention , unless we stand side by side ; the latter would of course be attribute to the effect of climate . from the gamekeeper 's evidence , I be now inclined to believe that Lepardo , while lurk in the low coppice , among the holly bush , must have cast his evil eye on your poor father 's face , and convince himself that he behold his enemy . 
 flurry and frighten , I look at the date of the paper . it be twelve day old . possibly I might yet be in time , for most likely the murderer would set out on foot , accord to corsican practice , with the travel - stone bind on his knee . even if he have travel in modern fashion , he would probably lurk and lie in ambush about the house , endure hunger and cold and privation , until his moment come . could I leave for Gloucester that night ? no , the last train would have start , before I could get to Paddington . so I resolve to go by the morning express , which would take I to Gloucester by middle day . 
 after a sleepless night , I be up betime in the morning , and go through the form of breakfast while the cab be send for . presently a waiter come in with the morning paper , the paper of New year's - day , 1843 . what I see and what my feeling be , you , my poor child , can too well imagine . that day I could not bear to go . it be cowardly of I , and perhaps unmanly ; but I could not face your mother 's grief and the desolate household . therefore I persuade myself that I have discharge my duty , by visit all the London police station , and leave the good description I could give of Lepardo . the following day I leave London , and arrive , as perhaps you remember , long after dark , and during a heavy fall of snow . there at the very threshold I begin amiss with you , for I outrage your childish pride by mistake you for the housekeeper 's daughter . with a well - bear child 's high self - esteem , and make no allowance for the dim light , you believe it to be a sham intend to mortify you ; and it poison your heart towards I . but you be wholly mistaken . my mind be full of your mother and of the terrible blow to she ; to you , whom I have never see , and scarcely even hear of , I never give a thought ; except the mistaken one that you be not old enough to be sensible of your loss . little do I imagine what a fount of resolute will , and deep feeling , find a vent in the kick and scream of the large - eyed minnikin , that would not be order away . 
 you be entitle , Clara , to know all that I have do towards the discovery of your father 's assassin , and all that I can tell to aid your own pursuit . the hair find in your mother 's grasp be beyond a doubt Lepardo 's ; that lay upon your father 's bosom be , of course , my Lily 's . it be to show that her suppose seduction have be expiate . the one thing that most surprise I be that the murderer leave no token , no symbol of himself . in a Vendetta murder they almost always do , as a mark of triumph and a gage to the victim 's family . hence I believe that Signor Dezio be not kill in Vendetta , but by his nephew for gain . how Lepardo get into the house I have no idea , or rather I have none , until you tell I of the secret passage , and Mrs. Daldy 's entrance . till then I always think that he have clamber up , as he do at Veduta tower . but unless there be a traitor in the household , he must have be there more than once , to have know so well your father 's sleep room . 
 it would have be waste of time for I to concern myself about the county police . that body of well - conduct navvy - Lepardo would have outwit they , when he be five year old . neither do I meddle with the coroner and his jury , but leave they to their own device and indigenous intellect . these display themselves in much puzzle - head cross - questioning , sagacious look , and nod , and wink of acute reservation . it be , as most often it be , a bulldog after a hare . Lepardo might safely have be in the midst of they , ask for a chair , and make suggestion . as " amicus curia . " 
 but with the London police it be somewhat different . they show some little acuman , but their fundamental error be this - they pride themselves on their intelligence . no man of any real depth ever do such a thing as this . he know very well that whatever he be , there be half a million more so ; that the age of exceptional intellect expire , at least in this country , with Mr. Edmund Burke , and be not likely to rise from the dead . now we be all pretty much good useful clod on a level : education , like all good husbandry , tend to pulverisation ; and if the collective produce be great , let we be at once thankful and humble . 
 the London police , be proud of their intelligence , declare that there could be no doubt about their catch the criminal . they laugh at my belief that he might walk through the midst of they , while they would touch their hat to he , and beg he to look after his handkerchief . at one time , I think , they be really on his track , and I go to London , and stay there , and do my good to help they . but they be all too late ; Lepardo , if he it be , have leave for Paris the week before . to Paris I follow , but find no trace of he there . then I go on to Corsica , think it likely that he would return to his old piratical way . moreover , I want to see how my child 's estate be manage , and to revisit St. Katharine 's . 
 all be calm and peaceful . Lily 's grave and her father 's be blend in one rich herbage . there all the bloom of my life be droop , like the yellow mountain - rose , whence if a single flower be pluck , all the other blossom fall . 
 Count Gaffori receive I kindly . his daughter be married and have two child , who play where Lily 's boy and girl should by right be play . I could not bear it , and come away , have nothing now to care for . wherever I go the world seem much of a muchness to I ; and to my own misfortune the blood of my brother be add . I find the " Lilyflower " still under worthy Petro , and return in she to England , and she still be mine . Petro would not come ; he be too true a Corsican to leave the beloved island now his hair be grey . so I set he up at Calvi with a vessel of his own , and now and then I receive a letter from good Marcantonia . they have promise to watch for the reappearance of our fearful enemy ; and Petro have swear to shoot he , if ever he get a chance . 
 after my return to England , I set to work with all my energy to improve this property . in this , if in nothing else , I have thoroughly succeed . much opposition I have to encounter ; for the tenant regard I as a mere interloper , and their heart be with you and your mother . when I call they together to - morrow , as I intend to do , abandon all my right , title , and interest , and declare you their Signora , it be my firm belief that they will hardly think I worth curse before they worship you . this old retainership be a thing to be proud and yet ashamed of . it be a folly that make one glory in be a fool . why , after you leave for Devonshire   much , as you know , against my will , I could not ride out without be insult , and even the boy call I " Jonathan Wild . " but this be due , in some measure , to your father 's gay geniality , and hearty good - will to all man , contrast with my satiric and moody reserve . neither be your youth , and sex , and helplessness , lose upon that chivalrous being - if he only know his chivalry - the sturdy English yeoman . 
 why do I let you go ? well , I believe it be one of the many mistake of my life ; but I have a number of reason , though personal dislike of you be not , as you think , one of they . no , my child , I have never dislike you ; not even on the night when you come and denounce I , with the dagger in your hand . I must indeed have be bad than I be , if I could have nourish ill - will against a young thing , whom I have make an orphan . by some instinct , you know from the first that the deed be mine , although I be not the doer . I would have love you , if you would have let I , my heart yearn so over child . but of my reason for let you go , the chief perhaps - set aside that I could not stop you - be this consideration . for year I have long , and crave in my heart of heart , to tell your mother all , and obtain her gentle forgiveness . but any allusion - no matter how veil and mantle - to the story of her loss throw she , as you know well , into a most peculiar state , wherein all the power of mind and body seem to be quite suspend . with a man 's usual roughness of prescription for the more delicate sex , I believe firmly that total change of living , and air , and place , and habit , would relax this wonderful closure , secure my forgiveness , and re - establish her health . the shock I receive at her death be almost as terrible as when my brother die . when I stand beside you at her grave , I be come with the full intention of tell you all my story , and beg you to return with I , and live once more in your father 's house . but your behaviour to I be so cold and contemptuous , that I forget my crush debt to you ; and humiliation become , for the moment , impossible . I mean , however , to have write to you that evening , before you should leave the village ; but   as you now be aware   that very evening , I be smite helpless . partially recover , after month of illness , I be deeply distressed to find that you have leave your good friend in Devonshire , and be go , my informant could not say whither . neither have I learn your whereabouts up to the time of my last illness , when I be make inquiry , of which your enemy reap the benefit . for the rest , you know that I never mean to rob you of your inheritance , though bigote nonsense enable I . to - morrow , please God , I will put it out of my power to do so . Mrs. Daldy 's motive you have long since perceive . fail my child , and the attainted Lepardo , her son be the heir to all the land of the Della Croce . she have hold I much in her power , by her knowledge of part of my history . Henry 's baptismal entry , as well as that of my marriage , be in the packet she stole . one word more , my darling - and from an old man , who have wander and suffer much , you will not think it impertinent . leave your revenge to God . in his way - which we call wonderful , because the step be unseen - he will accomplish it for you , as righteousness demand . any interference of ours be a worm - cast in his avenue . though I be stricken and die , he , if so please he , will bring I my child before I die , that I may bless he , and tell my Lily . " 
 I fall upon the old man 's neck - old he be , though not in year - and as I weep I kiss he . how could I have wrong he so , and how could I keep myself from love one so long unhappy ? if sorrow be the sponge of sin , his fault be wipe away . 
 CLARA VAUGHAN 
 BOOK V. 
 CHAPTER I. 
 at this time and place , I , Clara Vaughan , leap from the pillion of my Uncle 's pensive mule , and be upon the curb - stone of my own strange life again . how I wander with he through the olive grove of Corsica , how I weep for his love Lily , that ancient signor , and the steal babe ; and how , beyond the vomito of word , I loathe that fiend who have injure whom or what most I know not , unless it be his own soul , if he have any , and for God 's sake I hope he have - all this , though I be too weak of language , will , perhaps , be understand . 
 to myself I would hardly confess the interest I could not discard in the pure and constant love of that impassioned pair ; for what have I any long to do with Pyramus and Thisbe ? no more of love for I . you will not see I droop , and fret , and turn to a mossy green . no nonsense of that sort for I : I have a loop at either side entitle self - respect , which will keep my skirt from draggle . neither will I rush into the opposite extreme , pronounce all love a bubble because my own have burst , take to low - neck dress , and admire cat more than baby . no ; I be only eighteen , not yet eighteen and a half ; I have love with all my heart , and a free true heart it be , albeit a hot and haughty one ; if it be despise , outraged , and make nothing of , though I can never transfer , I will not turn it sour . the world be every whit as fair , child be quite as pretty , flower have as rich a scent , and goodness as pure a charm , as if that silly maiden Clara have not leap before she look . and yet how I wish that I could only think so . 
 before I go on with my tale , I must recur to one or two little matter , that everything may be as clear as it lie in my power to make it . for although I be but a " female , " as Inspector Cutting observe , I be do my good to make everything as clear as if tell by a male . 
 in the first place then , when my Uncle have recover from the exertion of tell his tale , I acquaint he with my discovery of the letter upon the bed - hanging . they confirm his account of the fearful Vendetta usage , and explain the point which have be to he most mysterious . 
 secondly , as to the anonymous letter which have lead I first to London ; like the detective policeman , he now attach but little importance to it . he have do his good , at the time , to trace the writer and follow the clue , if there be any . but he have meet with no success . his reason for pass it on to I , be that he hop to create some diversion of my thought , some break in the cloud of my sorrow . 
 next , to show the full meaning of Mrs. Daldy 's manoeuvre . through her connexion - which she have carefully cultivate , when it begin to seem worth her while - with her husband 's kindre near Genoa , she have learn some portion of my poor Uncle 's history ; for , as he himself observe , the islander be much addict to gossip , as indeed all islander be , and continental too for that matter , especially in hot climate . now there be no lack of intercourse between the Balagna and Genoa . of course our chasten hypocrite make the most of her knowledge in a hundred way , and by her sham sympathy and pretend aid - for up to the time of his illness the desolate father still seek and seek - she even secure some little influence over her brother - in - law . how often be it so : though we know people to be false , we do not believe , when our heart be concern , that they be so false to we . moreover , when she find he shatter in body and mind by paralysis , she commence an active bombardment , pull out the tompion from every gun of mock religion . but , as in her treatment of I , she display , in spite of all her experience and trial , a sad ignorance of unregenerate human nature . my Uncle be not the man , palsied or no , to be terrify by a Calvinist : and he know too much of her early day , and certain doing at Baden , to identify she at present with the angel that stand in the sun . and this prison - eyed mole make another mistake . not content with one good gallery , she must need work two run , side by side , in a very mealy soil . the result be of course that they run into one , and she have to dig her way out . if she have work , heart and soul , for my Uncle 's money only , which he rightly regard as his own , and at his own disposal , I believe she might have get most of it . at any rate , under the will which I catch she carry off , she be to take half of the large sum which he have lay by ; I mean if his child do not come to light , and prove their legitimacy . but twenty - five thousand pound would be nothing to her dear son , who have inherit his father 's extravagance , or to herself , who love high play . therefore , believe I out of the field , she begin to plot for the Vaughan estate as well , and furthermore for the magnificent property in Corsica . of the Vaughan estate she have no chance - albeit she have the impudence to propose a compromise with I - of Veduta tower she have some prospect , if the right heir , the poor child , should never appear , or establish their claim , and if she could procure the outlawry of Lepardo . 
 believe my Uncle to be die by inch , she make a bold stroke for possession of the most important document ; and , but for Giudice and I , no doubt she would have succeed . but she have dash far out of her depth , and have little chance now of reach the coveted land . I hope she feel that everything be order for her good . 
 another point which seem to require some explanation , be the discovery by the assassin of the secret entrance , an access quite unknown to the family , the servant , or any other person , except , at a later time , Mrs. Daldy . the house , as I say before , be build upon the site and partly embody the fabric of a still more ancient structure . probably these narrow stair , now enclose in the basement of the eastern wall , have save many a ripe priest from reel , in the time of the Plantagenets . they lead , I think , from the ancient chapel , long since destroy , to the chaplain 's room , and perhaps have be reopen secretly during the great rebellion , when the Vaughans be in hot trouble . Beatrice Vaughan , the cavalier 's child , who be now suppose to begin her ghost walk at the eastern window , glide probably down this staircase , when , as the legend relate , she escape mysteriously from the house , in her father 's absence , rouse the tenant , and surprise the Roundhead garrison in their bed . the house be soon retake , and Beatrice , in her youthful beauty , give up to the brutal soldier . she snap a pistol at the puritan officer , and fly like a bird along this corridor . at the end , while try perhaps to draw the old oak slide - though nothing be say of this - she be catch by the gloating fanatic , and stab herself on the spot rather than yield to dishonour . the poor maiden 's tomb be in the church , not far from the chancel arch , with some line of quaint Latin upon it . her lover , Sir William Desborough , slit that puritan officer 's nose and cut off both his ear . I wonder that he let he off so lightly ; but perhaps it be all he be worth . Major Cecil Vaughan marry again , and the direct line be re - establish . 
 the chapel well , as it be call , dark and overhang with ivy , be a spring of limpid icy crystal , span by and form a deep alcove in the ancient chapel wall , which , partly for its sake , and partly as a buttress for the east end of the house , have be leave still stand . this old well have long time be disuse , hiding , as it do , in a wild and neglect corner out of sight from the terrace walk ; and the gardener , who find the pump less troublesome , have condemn the water as too cold for their plant . the mouth , with its tangled veil of ivy and periwinkle , be also mask by a pile of the chapel ruin , now dignified with the name of a rockwork . some step of jagged stone lead through the low black archway to the crouch water , which be so clear that it seem to doubt which be itself and which be stone . 
 this peaceful , cold , unruffled well , form the antechamber to the murderer 's passage . for on the right - hand side , not to be see in the darkness , and the sublustrous confusion , by any common eye , be a small niche and footing - place not a yard above the water . it need some nerve and vigour to spring from the low stepping - stone sideway to this scarcely visible ledge . none , of the few whose eye be good enough to espy it , would be tempt to hazard the leap , unless they know or suspect that the facing would yield to the foot , that it be in fact a small door purposely colour and joint like the slimy green of the masonry . in this well the murderer must have lurk ; and he might have do so from one year 's end to another . there with the craft of his devilish race - my Uncle may admire they , but not I - and with their wonderful power of sight , he must have find this entrance , and rejoice in his hellish heart . 
 as for Mrs. Daldy , she find it out at the other end , most likely . unless my memory fail I , I speak long ago of some board which sound hollow to the ring of my childish knuckle . these be in the skirting - if that be the proper name for it - under the centre of the great oriel window . the oak slide , when press from below , run in a groove with but little noise , and without much force be use : but it require some strength to move they on the side of the corridor . it be the sound of these slide board which have first draw Judy 's notice : but as they be in deep shadow , I neither perceive the opening , nor give he the opportunity . that woman would never have dream of the thing , if she have not surprise I one day when I be pry about there ; she must have return alone , and be , as we have see , a superior cabinet - maker , discover the secret which baffle I . as I do not want Judy to catch cold by watch there any long , I have this horrible passage wall up at either end , and build across in the middle . 
 have thus make good my arrear , I be at liberty to proceed . when my Uncle have pause from his many sorrow , which he do with a mellow dignity not yet understand by I ; and when I , in the fervour of youth , have offer much comfort kindly receive , but far well let alone , I ask he for one thing only : - the most minute and accurate description he could give of that Lepardo Della Croce . his answer be as follow : - 
 " my dear , I have see he once only , and that more than twenty year ago , and in an interview of some excitement " - I should think so indeed , when one try to kill the other - " but I will describe he to the good of my recollection . he be rather a tall man , at least of about my own height , but more lightly build than myself . his hand and foot be remarkably small and elegant . his face be of the true italian type , a keen oval with a straight nose , and plenty of width between the eye , which be large and very dark . his forehead be not massive , but well - form , and much whiter than the rest of his face . the expression of his countenance be that of shrewdness and versatility , with a quickness eager to save both you and himself from the trouble of complete your sentence . but all this be common enough . one thing I see , or fancy , which be not quite so common . as I deal he that blow with my fist , my eye for one flash meet his , and his leap towards one another , as if he have a strong cast in they . before that , and afterwards too , there be no appearance of any distortion : if there be any at that moment , it arise from the start of terror or fury jerk the muscle awry . his voice be flexible and persuasive , and soft as a serpent - charmer 's . I think he must be a most arrogant man ; profoundly convinced of his own ability , but seldom care to vindicate they . just the man to get on in the world , if he be only what be call respectable . just the man to break a woman 's heart , and crush the spirit of a meek and humble child . ah , I would forgive he his sin against I , though not his wrong towards you , if I could only learn that he have be kind to my child . " 
 this description dwelt on my mind for day and day of thinking . it do not altogether apply to the man whom I have observe so closely at the meeting of the conspirator . that man be of middle height , and though his face be oval , there be scarcely the average width between the eye . and he do not seem to I like an arrogant man , cold except when excite ; but rather of a hasty , impassioned nature , sure to do its utmost in trifle . could it be that I have watch and hat the wrong man ? it might be so ; and it be not unlikely that Mr. Cutting himself know not which be the guilty one . like most of the London policeman - my Uncle have teach I this - he be too proud of his sagacity to be in truth very sagacious . experience he have , and all that ; but he would not have do in Paris . the real depth , that go below , and yet allow for the depth of another , must be in the nature , can rarely exist in a small one , and in a large one be seldom work but for theoretical purpose . therefore shallow man overreach in daily life , and fancy they have blind those who know they thoroughly , and know themselves as well . 
 so far as my experience go , large - natured man abhor cunne so much , that they fear to work the depth of their own intelligence , because it seem akin to it . so they be cheat every day , as a strong man yield to the push of a child ; and the fool who cheat they chuckle in the idea that they have do it by fine sagacity , and without the victim 's knowledge . 
 CHAPTER II . 
 at my earn entreaty , the idea of assemble the tenant especially be allow to drop , and I be to be induct at the Midsummer dinner , which be very near at hand . a deed have be prepare by the London solicitor , recite the fact and assure all the estate to I , as my father 's proper heiress . my Uncle also desire to settle upon I all the personal property , except a sum of 10,000 l. , which he would reserve for his child , to enable they , if ever they should be find , to establish their claim in Corsica : then if the son obtain his right , his sister be to have the money with all expenditure make good by he . but I would not hear of it . it would have make I a rogue . by his skill and economy , my Uncle , during the nine year of his management , have save more than 50,000 l.   from the proceed of the estate . but he have add at least an equal amount to the value of the land , by carry out most judiciously the improvement begin by my father ; and the whole be now consider the well - manage estate in Gloucestershire . 
 therefore , when he abandon his legal right , in the most honourable manner , it would have be horribly shabby and unlike a Vaughan , to hold he accountable for the back rent . I beg he to leave the whole of it for the benefit of his poor child , request only , and unnecessarily , that the hypocrite might not have sixpence . another thing I entreat , that he would prolong his guardianship , and stewardship , if his health allow it , until I should be of age , that be to say , for two year and a half . see how earnestly I desire it , he undertake to do so , though he make the promise with a melancholy smile , add that he hop his child would be find ere then , if he be to see they at all . 
 when the rent - dinner be over , and the glass have be replace , my Uncle , who have not be there as usual , lead I into the great old hall . feeble as he be , he enter with a grace and courtesy not always to be discover in the mien of prince . the supper - as the farmer call it - have not begin till six o'clock ; and now the evening sunshine glance through the western window , and between the bunch of stone grape into the narrow doorway , steal in from the Vinery with sandal of leafy pattern . the hall be deck with rose , no other flower but rose ; yet who could want any other , when every know rise be there ? even the bright yellow blossom of the Corsican rock - rise , a plant so sensitive that to steal one flower be to kill all the rest . from time out of mind , some feudal custom of tenure by the rose have be hand down in our family . 
 all the guest rise as we pass , which make I rather nervous , albeit I know every one of they from my childhood up . then my Uncle , lean on I , speak a few word from the step , plain and simple word without flourish or pretence . what he say be know long since , and have be thoroughly discuss in every house of the village . he finish by set I in the black oak chair of state - which he have never use - and present I with a rose ; then he turn round and propose my health . when I take the rose , an exquisite crested moss , kiss it and place it in my bosom , accord to the usage , such a shout arise , such an English hurrah , that it must have echo to the other bank of the distant Severn . at first I be quite frightened , then I burst into tear as I think of he whose chair I sit in , whose memory still be echo in that mighty shout . it be not only love of right , or sympathy with a helpless girl , that move those honest bosom , but the remembrance of he who have be so pleasant to they , humble , kind and just , in one word , a gentleman . 
 but as they come up , one by one , and beg to take my hand , and wish I joy and long life with all their heart , I find that I be right in one thing ; I know they well than my Uncle do . instead of be rude or cold to he , as he expect , they almost overwhelm he with praise and admiration . but all this I must not dwell on , for my story hurry hence , and its path be not through rose . 
 Annie Franks , who still be with we , and do not mean to go until she have finish all the Froissart novel , and such a dear good girl she be , that we hop they would last for ever , Annie Franks bring I next day two letter of aspect strange to " good society . " one I know at a glance to be from Tossil 's Barton , though the flourish be amazing , and the lead - pencil line rub out . the other , a work of far less ambition and industry , be an utter stranger ; so of course I take it first . nevertheless , I will treat of it last , because it open the stormy era . 
 Dear Sally 's gossip be not to be serve up whole . even if it be interesting to other as to I , my space permit no dalliance with farm - yard , no idyls of Timothy Badcock , nay , nor even the stern iambic of Ebenezer Dawe . only to be just and clear , I may not slur it all . the direction be remarkable . the farmer be always afraid of not be duly explicit , for he believe that letter be deliver throughout England as in the parish of Trentisoe ; where all , except those for the parson and Tossil 's Barton farm , be set upside down in the window at Pewter Will 's , the most public - house in the place . the idea be ingenious , and , I believe , original - have be suggest by the Queen 's boy , whose head Mrs. Huxtable punch . it be that no one could read the name upside down , except the owner of the name and therefore of the letter . sound or not , I can not say , have have no experience ; but there be this to be say for it , that no one would try the puzzle who do not expect a letter , unless indeed he be of precocious genius , and from that Trentisoe be quite safe . 
 upon the present " papper - scrawl , " after a long description of I , patronymical , local , and personal , the follow injunction and menace be add , " not be stick top I turve I on no account in no public house . she be in her own house now again , thank God and dang they as turn she out I say , so mind you carr it there . a deal of money there be in it , and no fear of Joe because he know it , and there live a man in Gloucestershire know I well by the name of Thomas Henwood . good look sharp I say . I be up to every one of you . John Huxtable his name , no mark this time . God save the queen . " 
 so the farmer have actually learn to write , although as yet to a strictly limited extent . of course he have not write any of the above except his name ; but that be his , and do he credit , though it nearly describe a circle . 
 after the warm congratulation and return the five - pound note , which I have send for interest , with an indignant inquiry from father whether I take he for a Jew , and after several anecdote and some history of butter sell at Ilfracombe market , Sally proceed thus : 
 " now what do you think , Miss Clara dear ? no you never would guess as long as you live - father be go to London town , and I , and Jack , and Beany Dawe . none of we have sleep two grunt of a pig , ever since it be make up , only father , and he always sleep without turn . now mind if I tell you all about it , you must not tell again , Miss Clara , because there be ever so much money upon it , and we do hear they have put it on some London paper and no business of theirs . two great gentlefolk , the great of any about these part , have be and make up a bet for my father to wrestle along with a great big chap as they call the North Country champion . seem as some great northern lord be boast in London one dinner - time , Speaker 's dinner they call it because there be a deaf and dumb dinner next day , this here great lord be tell up as how Sam Richardson be the strong man in the world . so our Sir Arthur spake up for Devonshire , and lay he a quart - pot full of sovereign as he would find a well man in the West country . and so I do not   know the right of it , nor father nor mother either , but it be make up atwixt they that Farmer Huxtable , that be my father , Miss , should try this great North country chap at the time of the great Xabition - you never show I the way to spell it , Miss , so I go by the light of nature , as you use to say , Miss - and should take good of three fall for 200 l.   a side . that will be 400 l.   for we , when father get it , and all his expense pay , and they say the other folk will not   allow no kicking , so he must be a soft - shelled chap ; but father feel no call to hurt he , if so be he can help it . mother do not   want father to go , but he say he be bind for the honour of old Devonshire , or maybe they will take a man not good enough to make a standard . 
 and please , Miss , when we bring home the money , I be to go to Miss Bowden 's , in Boutport Street , and our Jack to be put to a day - school not more than six mile away , and then I hope he know himself , and look high than that minx of a Tabby Badcock . what do you think , Miss Clara , you would never believe it I know , but only a week ago last Tuesday I come sudden round the corner , and catch she a kissing of our Jack in the shed there by the shoot . and after all you teach she , Miss ! Jack he run away , as red as mangawazzle , but that brazen slut , there she stand with her leg out , as innocent as a picture . never a word I say , but with no more to do I put her head in the calf ' stommick as we make the cheese with , in a bucket handy . it would have do you good to see she Miss , she do cry so hard , and she smell of it for a week , and it cure our Jack , up to Sunday anyhow . mother come out at the noise , but her see that she deserve it , and the runnet be no account , except for the pig , because it be go by . I hope she know her manner now and her spear in life with her sheep 's eye , and not come try to catch any of my family . 
 well , Miss Clara please , father want mother to go ; but no , say she , " with all they " - she ought to have say " they " Miss , now have not   she ought ? - " with all they young pig , and the brown cow expect every day , and Suke no head at all , and all the chiller and little Clara " - she call her " Clara " now , Miss , - " why farmer what be think of ? " then father rub the nose of he , you know the way he do it , Miss , and he say , " I must have some one . London be such a wicked place . " Mother look up very sharp at that , and say quite peart , " take your daughter , farmer Huxtable , if you want to be keep respectable . " so I be to go Miss ; and go I would not   without Jack and leave he along of that sly cat Tabby , and she get sweet again now ; besides I want he to choose a knife I promise he , same as he see to Coom one time , if he would not   let Tabby kiss he with seven blade and a corkscrew , and I will give eighteen penny for it , that I will . and Beany Dawe must go to show we the way about , and see as they do not   cheat we , because his father be once to London town , and tell he a power about it . 
 if you please , Miss Clara , father be put in training as they call it in these part , all the same as a horse . he run up and down Breakneck hill , with the good bed on his back , nine time every day , and he do not   drink no cider , no nor beer , nor gin and water , and mother hardly know he , he be come so clear in the skin ; but he say his hand shake still from the time I teach he to write , and please , Miss , what do you think of the way he be go to sign this ? I can not   get he to put his thumb right , no nor his middle finger , and he stick his elbow out every bit as bad as Tabby , and he say he like the pot - hook over the fire well , but for all that I believe I shall make a scholard of he , particular when he give up wrestling , which he have swear to do if he throw this Cumberland chap , and stick to his Bible and Prayer - book . 
 please , Miss , not to be offend , but excuse we ask if you like to see the great wrestling . Father say no , it would not be fitty , and that be the bad of be a gentlefolk ; but mother say what harm , and she be sure the farmer do it twice as well with you there , and you shall have the good seat in the place next to the two judge , and such a pretty handkerchief they send down all spot the same as a Guernsey cow , how the people in church do stare at I , and you shall have two of the good , Miss , but I be afraid it be make too bold ; but you never see any wrestling , Miss , and I be sure you would enjoy it so . it take place in the copandhagen field , next Saturday week . do come , Miss Clara dear , it will do you so much good , and you see father , and I , and Jack , and Beany Dawe . " 
 I need recount no more of poor Sally 's soft persuasion . the other letter be of a different vein : - 
 " HONOURED Miss , - Balak and I after a deal of trouble and labour night and day and throw up our vacation have at last succeed in find you know who . personal interview will oblige , early inconvenience . no more at present not be safe on paper , from your most obedient servant and suitor 
 BALAAM and balak - you know who . - 
 Poscrip . - Balak say a sharp young lady quite sure to know what be right , but for fear of accident please a little of the ready will oblige , large family both of we have and it do take a deal of beer more than our proper vacation no one would guess unless they be to try and bad beer too a deal of it . for self and partner . - BALAAM . " 
 CHAPTER III . 
 when my Uncle see that letter , he declare that he would go to London with I . no power on earth should prevent he . not even his self - willed Clara . it be not revenge he want : even though it be for his innocent brother , whose wrong he could not pardon . no , if the small - minded wretch who have spend his life in destroy a fellow - creature 's , if that contemptible miscreant lie at his foot to - morrow , he would not plant foot upon he ; but forgive he heartily , if he have the grace to desire it . but for his child , - for they he must go to London . only let he see they once before he die . no torpid limb for he . who say he be old - and he only forty - seven ? 
 one thing seem rather strange to I . he long , yearned I should say , to look upon his little Lily even more than on the child he know , his son , his first - bear Harry . " why , Clara , " he use to say , ' she be nearly as old as you , and you be a full - grow girl . on the 21st of this month " - it be now July - ' she will be eighteen ; I can hardly believe it . I wonder what she be like . most likely she take after her lovely mother . no doubt of it , I should say . do not   you think so , Clara ? " 
 " of course , Uncle , " I would reply , know nothing at all about it , " of course she do . how I should like to see she . " 
 perhaps fifty time a day , he would ask for my opinion , and I would deliver it firmly , perhaps in the very same word and without a shade of misgiving ; and though of no value whatever , it seem to comfort he every time . but the prolonged excitement , and the stress of imagination exert on Lily junior , tell upon he rapidly in his worn and weak condition . long for his company , assistance , and advice , I wait from day to day , even at the risk of leave Balaam and Balak without good beer . all this time , my imagination be busy with weak surmise , faint suspicion , and tangle recollection .
