 at last , I could delay no long . Tuesday be the late day I could consent to wait for , and on the Monday my Uncle be more nervous and weak than ever . it be too plain that he must not attempt the journey , and that the long suspense be impair his feeble health . so for once I show some decision - which seem to have fail I of late - without tell he any more about it , I get everything ready , and appear at his bedroom door , only to say " good bye . " Annie Franks , who be go with I , for a short visit to her father , hang back in some amazement , doubt whether she have any right to be there , and drag off her leg by the coil of my strong will . my poor Uncle seem quite take aback ; but as it could not be help , he speedily make up his mind to it . " the carriage be at the door ; " which announcement to english mind preclude all further argument . 
 " good bye , Uncle dear , " I cry , as cheerily as I could , " I shall be back by the end of the week and bring your Lily with I . give I a good kiss for she , and now another for myself . " 
 he be sit up in the bed , with a Cashmere dressing - gown on , and pore over some relic of olden time . 
 " good bye , my darling , and do not   be long away . they have rob I enough already . " 
 after give Judy the strict order , I hurry off in fear and hope , doubtful whether I ought to go . Annie linger and give he a kiss , for she be very fond of he . he whisper something about I , which I do not stop to hear , for I want to leave he in good spirit . 
 after a rapid journey , I see dear Annie safe in the arm of her father and mother , and find Mrs. Shelfer at home , and in capital spirit , all the bird ,   c. well , and no distress in the house . Charley be do wonder , wonder , my good friend , stick to his work , yes , yes , and not inside the public house for the good part of the week . leastway so he say , and it would not do to contradict he . and she really do believe there be only three bill over - due ! 
 my little room be snug and quiet , and the dust not more than half an inch thick . Mrs. Shelfer use to say that dust furniture be the bad thing in the world to wear it out . accord to her theory , the dust exclude the air , especially from the joint , and prevent the fly - blow coming . however , I make she come up and furbish , while I go out to post a letter for Messrs. Balaam and Balak , request they to visit I in the morning . 
 when thing be set to right a little , and air , which Mrs. Shelfer hat , flow in from either balcony , I buy a fine crab and some Sally Lunns , and beg for the pleasure of my landlady 's company at tea . this she gladly give I , for the little woman love nothing well than suck the hairy leg of a crab . but she be so overcome by the rumour of my wealth , that she even fear to eject the piece in her ordinary manner , and the front rail of her chair be like the beam of a balance . infinitely rather would I be poor myself , than have people ceremonious to I because I be not poor ; and to tell the honest truth , I believe there be a vein of very low blood in I , which blush at the sense of rich and position . why should I have every luxury , that be if I choose to have it , while man and woman of a thousand time my mind , and soul , and heart , spend their precious life in earn the value of their coffin ? 
 this thought have weary many a mind of pure aerial flight , compare whereto my weak departure be but the hop of a flea ; so I lose the imago , but catch the larva , upon the nettle , practice . Mrs. Shelfer be soon at ease ; and we talk of the price of cat 's meat , and how dear sausage be , and laugh - myself with sorrow - over the bygone day , when dripping play the role of butter , and Judy would not take a bone because he think I want it . 
 then we talk over the news . Miss Idols have be there , bless her sweet face , yes , ever so many time , to look for letter , or to hear tiding of I . but she be not one bit like herself . she never tease the poor little woman now ; the poor little woman wish very much she would . oh , I should hardly know she . she do not know which bird it be that have the wooden leg , and have forget the difference between a meal - worm and a lob . and she do not care which way she rub the ear of the marmoset . Mrs. Shelfer believe , but for the world it must not be tell again , that Isola be deeply in love , unrequited love , perhaps one of the weteranarian gent . they do say they have some stuff as would lead a girl like a horse . but whatever it be , Mrs. Shelfer only know that she could not get at the right of it . girl have grow so cunning now - a - day , what with the great supernatural exhibition , and the hat they have take to wear flat on the top of their head , not at all what they use to be when she and Charley be young . then a young woman be not afraid of show what her neck be like ; now she tuck it in cotton wool like a canary 's egg . and what be they the well , sly minx ? she see enough of it in the Square garden , and they show their little sister ' leg for pattern of their own , oh fie ! " 
 " come , Mrs. Shelfer , no scandal , if you please . what news of your Uncle John ? " 
 " ah , Miss , you must ask the shark , and the lobster , and the big sea - serpent . they do say , down at Wapping , that the ship be cast away among the cannibal island , and the people eat a policeman , and he upon his promotion . what a pity , what a pity ! and his coat four and sixpence a yard , ready shrunk ! but they native be outrageous . " 
 " Nonsense , Patty , I do not   believe a word of it . sailor be dreadful story - teller , ever since the day of Sindbad . have any one besides Miss Isola , Mrs. Elton , or any one , be here to ask for I ? " 
 " no , Miss , Mr. Conrad never come after the day you serve he so dreadful ; and Miss Idols say he go back and spoil 300 l.   worth of work ; but that great lady with the red plush breech , and the pink silk stocking , and the baker 's shop in their hair , she be here twice last week , and leave a letter for you . and Balaam be here several time , and Balak along of he ; but I bang the door on they both , now I hear they be out of the business , and a nice young man set up who do not   bother about the gun . " 
 " Lady Cranberry 's letter may lie there , and go back the next time Ann Maples come . but the bailiff I must see . if they come to - morrow , let they in immediately . and how be all my friend at the Mews ? " 
 her reply would fill a chapter , so I will not enter upon it , but go to bed and miss the sound of dear Judy 's tail at the door . in the first course of my dream , Mr. Shelfer pass on his bedward road , have politely take his shoe off at the bottom of the stair ; in do which he make at least three time the noise his shodden foot would have inflict . 
 in the morning I take my old walk round the Square , and then sit down and try to be patient until the bailiff should come . of course I do not mean to go to my darling Isola , nor even to let she know that I be so near at hand , although my heart be burn to see her sweet face again . I even keep away from the window , though I want to watch for the bailiff , and strictly order Mrs. Shelfer not to tell she , if she should call , a word about my be there . however , it be all in vain . Mr. Shelfer go out after breakfast to his play - work in the Square , and the smell of his pipe invade my little room . I think he must have leave the front door open ; at any rate I hear , all of a sudden , a quick patter of run foot , and such a crying and sobbing , and Mrs. Shelfer hurry out to meet it . 
 " you can not , Miss , you can not   indeed - not for a thousand pound . the room be let , I tell you , and you can not   go up . oh dear , oh dear , whatever be I to do ? " 
 " Patty , I will go up . I do not   care who be there . my heart be break , and I will die on my darling 's bed . if you stand there , I will push you . out of the way , I tell you . " and up fly Idols , in a perfect mess of tear . what could I do but fly to meet she , and hug my only pet ? what with her passion of grief , and sudden joy , at see I , she faint away in my arm . I get she somehow to the sofa , and kiss she into her sens again . when she come to herself , and feel sure it be not a dream , she nestle into my bosom , as if I have be her husband , and steal long glance at I to see whether I be offend . her pretty cloak lie on the floor , and her hat beneath the table . for a long time she sob and tremble so that she could not say a word , while I keep on whisper such vain word as these : 
 " never mind , my pet . there , you have cry enough . tell your own dear Clara who have dare to vex you . " 
 to see that sweet child 's misery , I feel in such a rage , I could have box her enemy 's ear . but I never think that it be more than a child 's vexation . at last , after drink a tumblerful of water , and give room to her palpitating heart , she contrive to tell I her trouble . 
 " why , dear , you know my pappy - pappy I use to call he - he be not my papa at all , he say himself he be not ; and that be not the bad of it , for I could do well enough without he , he be always so dreadfully cross , and do not   care for I one bit . I could do without he very well , if I have a proper papa , or if my father be dead and have love I before he die ; but now I have no father at all , and never have any in the world ; I be only an outcast , an abandon - oh , Clara , will you promise to forgive I , and love I all the same ? " 
 " to be sure I will , my dearest . I be sure , you have do no harm . and even if you have be lead astray - " 
 she look at I with quick pride flash through her abasement , and she take her arm off my shoulder . 
 " no , you have quite mistake I . do you think I would sit here and kiss you , if I be a wicked girl ? but who be I to be indignant at anything now ? he tell I - be you sure the door be shut ? - he tell I , with a sneer , that I be a base - bear child , and he use a bad word than that . " 
 she fall away from I , her cheek all crimson with shame , and her long eyelash droop heavily on they . I catch she to my heart : poor wrong one , be she a whit less pure ? I seem to love she the well , for her great misfortune . of course , I have guess it long ago , from what her brother tell I . 
 " and who be your father , my pretty ? any father must be a fool who would not be proud of you . " 
 " oh , Clara , the bad of it be that I have not the least idea . but from something that hard man say , I believe he be an Englishman . I think I could have get everything from he , he be so beside himself ; but when he tell I that dreadful thing , and say that my father have lie to my mother and ruin she , I feel so sick that I could not speak , till he turn I out of the house , and strike I as I go . " 
 " what ? " 
 " yes , he turn I out of the house , and give I the blow of disgrace , and say I should never look on his face again . he have win his revenge - I can not tell what he mean , for I never harm he - and now I might follow my mother , and take to - I can not   repeat it , but it be bad than death . no fear of my starving , he say , with this poor face of mine . and so I be go to Conny , dear Conny ; I think he know it all long ago , but could not bear to tell I . and I sit on some step in a lonely place , for I do not know how to walk , and I pray to see you and die : then old Cora come after I , and even she be cry , and she give I all her money , and a morsel of the true cross , and tell I to come here first , for Conny be out of town , and she would come to see I at dark ; and perhaps the Professor would take I back when his rage be over . do you think I would ever go ? and after what he tell I to do ! " 
 such depth of loathing and scorn in those gentle violet eye , and her playful face for the moment so haughtily wild and implacable - Clara Vaughan , in her stately rancour , seem an iceberg by a volcano . 
 I see that it be the moment for learn all that she know ; and the time for scruple be past . 
 " Isola , tell I all you have hear , about this dastard bully ? " 
 " I know very little ; he have take good care of that . I only know that he do most horrible thing to unfortunate cat and dog . it make I shudder to touch he at one time . but he give that up I believe . but there be some dark and fearful mystery , which my brother have find out ; that be if he be my brother . how can I tell even that ? whatever the discovery be , it make such a change in he , that he care for nothing afterwards , until he see you , Clara . I be not very sharp , you know , though I have learn so much , that perhaps you think I be . " 
 " my darling , I never think such a thing for a moment . " 
 " oh , I be very glad . at any rate I like to talk as if I be clever . and some people say I be . but , clever or stupid , I be almost certain that Conny find out only half the secret ; and then on the day when he come of age , that man tell he the rest , either for his own purpose , or holy Madonna know why . " 
 " when be your brother of age ? " 
 " last Christmas Eve . do not   you remember what I tell you at the school of design that day ? " 
 " and when be your birthday , Isola ? " 
 " I be sure I do not   know , but somewhere about Midsummer . they never tell Conny when his be , but he know it somehow . come , he be clever now , Clara , though you do not   think I be . be not   he now ? tell the truth . " 
 " I be think of far more important matter than your rude brother 's ability . whence do you come to England and when ? " 
 this be quite a shot in the dark . but I have long suspect that they be of southern race . 
 " I be sure I do not   know . I be quite a child at the time , and the subject have be interdict ; but I think we come from Italy , and at least ten year ago . " 
 " and your brother speak Italian more readily than English . can you tell I anything more ? " 
 " nothing . only I know that old Cora be a Corsican : she boast of it every night , when she come to see I in bed , although she have be forbid . but what do she care - she ask - for this dirty little english island ? and she sit by my bed , and sing drone song , which I hardly understand ; but she say they be beautiful nanna . " 
 how my heart be beat , at every simple sentence . none of this have I hear before , because she durst not tell it . 
 " any other question , Donna ? " she be recover her spirit , as girl always do by talk . " why , my darling , you ought to have a wig . you beat all the senior sophist . " 
 " yes . now come and kiss I . kiss I for a pledge that you will never leave I . I be rich again now : you can not   tell how rich I be , and nothing to do with my money , and nobody likely to share it . if you be my own sister , I could not love you more ; and most likely I should not love you a quarter as much . and my Uncle long to see you so . you shall come and live with I , and we will be two old maid together . now promise , darling , promise . kiss I , and seal the bargain . " 
 " Clara , I would rather be your servant than the queen of the world . only promise first that you will never scold I . I can not bear be scold . I never use to be ; and it will turn all my hair gray . " 
 " I will promise never to scold you , unless you run away . " 
 she sweep back her beautiful hair , throw her arm round my neck , look in my eye with a well - spring of love , and kiss I . oh , traitorous Clara , it be not the kiss - deeply as I love she - but the evidence I want . I know that with her ardent nature she would breathe her soul upon I . the exquisite fragrance of her breath be like the wind steal over violet . I have notice it often before . my last weak doubt be scatter ; yet I play with she and myself , one sweet moment long . 
 " Darling , what scent do you use ? what be it you wash your tooth with ? " 
 " nothing but water , Clara ; what make you ask in that way ? " 
 " and the perfume in your hair - what be it ? oh , you little Rimmel ! " 
 " nothing at all , Donna . I never use anything scented . not even Eau de Cologne . I hate all the stuff they sell . " 
 " how very odd ! why , I could have declare that your lip and your hair be sprinkle with extract of violet . " 
 " oh , now I know what you mean . I never perceive it myself , but number of people have fancy that I use artificial perfume . but that man - oh , what shall I call he ? and only this morning I call he ' pappy ' - he always account for everything , you know ; and he say it be here - herod - I can not   say it now , the long english word , but I could at college - no matter , it mean something in the family . my mother , he say , be so well known to possess it , that she have an italian name among the servant for it ; though her real name be quite a different flower . Clara , why do you look at I so ? and what be you cry for ? " 
 " because , my own darling dear , I have not love you for nothing . you be my own flesh and blood . you be my own cousin , I tell you , my dear Uncle 's daughter ; and your name be Lily Vaughan . " 
 she draw her arm from I , and leap up from the sofa ; she be so amazed and frightened . she look at I most sadly , believe that I be mad ; then she faint again , and fall back into my arm . 
 when I have bring her round , and prop she up with a pillow - for cushion be very scarce - the strain of the mind be over , my brain begin to whirl so that I could neither think nor act . for a long time I could not have enough of kiss and hug idol . I play with her hair , as if I have be her lover ; and then pat and caress she , as if she have be my baby . and have I no thought of another , who ought to be do all this to I ? yes , I fear that it lie in the depth of my heart , strong than maid 's love of maiden , or even than my delight at the joy come to my Uncle . 
 then I hat myself for my selfishness , and catch up my Lily and rub she , and make her understand thing . I fling a decanter of water over both she and myself , which save we from hysteric . 
 poor little thing ! she be not like I . Strong Passion be a stranger to she , and she fall before his blow . I have fight with he so long , that I meet he like a prize - fighter , and counter at every stroke . up run Mrs. Shelfer , in the height and crest of the wave , when backwards or forwards , cry or laugh , hang on a puff of wind . she come with a commonplace motive ; she think we be play at cricket with her beloved stick . her arrival make a diversion , though it have no other effect , for I walk the little thing out , and lock the door behind she . 
 then I get my darling new cousin into my arm , and kiss she , and march she about the room , and make she show her Vaughan instep . excuse the petty nonsense - what woman be quite free from it ? - but for many generation our foot have be arch and point : of course it do not matter ; still I be glad that her be of the true Vaughan pattern . then , as she so hat all the stuff they sell , I shower over her an entire bottle of the very good Eau de Cologne . it be a bit of bullying ; but all girl of high spirit be bully . and it make her eye water so dreadfully , that she cry as hard as I do . 
 CHAPTER IV . 
 it must be own that my evidence at present be very shadowy . yet to myself I seem slow of hand for not have grasp it before . to the mind there be nothing conclusive , to the heart all be irresistible . I have not set down a quarter of the thought that now dawn upon I ; and it would be waste of time to recount they , when actual proof be forthcoming . and poor Idols give I small chance of think clearly , in the turbulent flood of her question . 
 " and be you quite sure , quite certain , Clara darling , that I have a lawful father , one who be not ashamed of I , and be not ashamed of my mother ! and why do he never come for I ? and do you think he will love I ? and be dear Conrad my own brother ? I do not   seem to understand half that you have tell I . " 
 at length I kneel down , and thank God - rather late in the day , I must own - for his wonderful guidance to I . while do so , and remembering , as I always do then , my mother - reveal in sudden light I see the justice of God 's providence . long as I have grope and grope , with red revenge my lead star , no breath of love or mercy cheer the abrupt step of a fatalist , so long have he vouchsafe to send I check and warning , more than guidance . by loss of wealth and dear friend , by blindness and desertion , and the crush blow to maiden 's pride when her heart be fling back in her face , by sad hour of watch and weep over the bed of sickness , by the history of another 's wrong - bad than my own , and yet forgive - by all these mean , and perhaps no less by the growth of the mind , and wide view of life , the spirit , once so indomitable , have learn to bow to its maker . stoop thus it see the path , which stiff - neck pride could not descry . 
 not first and sole , as it would have be two year since , but side by side with soft thought , come the strong belief that now God have reveal to I the man who slay my father . and what humiliation to all my boasted destiny ! I have grasp the hand that do the deed , smile to the eye that glare upon it , laugh at the sally of the mind that shape it . enough of this ; ere it go too hard with christian feeling . my bosom heave , my throat swell , and my eye flash as of old . 
 before I have time to resolve what next to do   for Isola would not let I think , we have another interruption . that girl have a most ill - regulated and illogical mind . and the fault be fundamental . if the lovely senior sophist have ever get her degree , and wear the gown of a Maiden of Arts , it could only have come by favour , after the manner of kissing . her enthymem be quick enough , and a great deal too quick I believe ; but as for their reduction or eduction into syllogism - we might as well expect she to make a telescope out of her boot - tag . and now at once she expect , and would not give I room for a word , that I should minutely detail in two sentence , with marginal annotation , and footnote , query , conjecture , and various reading , all incorporate into the text , everything that have ever , anywhere , or by any mean , befall she " genuine father . " not be thucydidean enough to omit the key - word in the sentence , and mash ten thought into one verb , I could not meet the emergency ; and my dear cousin lose her patience , which be always a very small parcel . 
 " at any rate , Clara , tell I one thing clearly . be you quite certain that Conny and I be not - not - " 
 " not base - bear , " I say - why be mawkish in Oscan - English , when Saxon be to be have ? - " no , my darling , you be as lawful as I , your cousin Clara . we Vaughans be a passionate race , but we never make wreck of woman , and scoundrel of ourselves . that we leave for Corsicans , and people bring up to lie . " 
 the sneer be most unjust , and dreadfully unkind , but far too natural for I , so long pen in , to resist it . I see that I have grieve my pet , so I beg her pardon , and revile myself , till all be right again . then suddenly she leap up and cry , with her hand upon her bound heart - every look and gesture must have be like her mother 's . 
 " let I go now , Clara . what be I think of ? let I start at once . and you say my own father be very ill . he will die without see I . on with your thing , while I run to the cab - stand . I have money enough for both . " 
 she wrench at the door - handle in her hurry , forget that I have lock it ; rich colour leap into her cheek , and her feature and form seem to dance , like a flickering flame , with excitement . no wonder her mother have love , and be love , with such power of passion . 
 " idol , take it easily , or I will not   let you go at all . I rather fancy , we must have some evidence , before my Uncle own a little chit pick up in London . he be a clever and cautious man , and will expect something more convincing than your beautiful eye and sweet breath . do you expect , you impetuous jumper , that he will know you by instinct ? " 
 poor little thing , how her face fall , and how the rose fade out of it ! that look of hers go to my heart ; but I know what the mother have die of , and fear lest her image and picture should perish in the same manner . so I say again : 
 " do you suppose , my dear , that your father would know you by instinct ? " 
 " well , perhaps I do , Clara ; if I think about it at all . I be sure I should know he so . " 
 at this moment , two heavy knock , like a postman 's , but not so quick , sound through the house . I know what they mean , one be Balaam , the other be Balak . Isola clung to I , and turn pale ; she think it be some one pursue she . I tell she hastily whom I expect , and send she to Mrs. Shelfer 's room . my heart beat high , when with many a scrape and bow , the worthy but not ornamental pair sidle heavily into the room . 
 to my greeting they answer I never a word ; but Balaam stand solemnly at the end of the little table , and beckon to his partner to fasten the door . this be do with some pantomime , which mean " by your leave , if you please , Miss , " the two man , who look none the lean for their arduous exertion , stand side by side before I . tired of this nonsense I exclaim impatiently ,

 Balaam wink at Balak , and receive a ponderous nod , begin to digest it leisurely . 
 " have you bring I to London for nothing ? what do you mean by all this mummery ? I shall ring the bell in a moment , and have you both show out . " 
 Balaam 's tongue revolve in his mouth , but burst not the bond of speech , and he try to look straight at both window , - till my hand be on the bell - pull . 
 " Balak , I tell you so . Lor , how much well it be for you to take my advice , than for I to take yourn ! Balak say , Miss , as we come along , the young lady would be sure to know what be right , and turn up handsome afore she ask we nothing . now , say I , that be not   the carakter of my experience , the woman most always want - "

 here a long interchange of signal take place , and even whisper behind a hat . 
 " well , Miss , I say ten , and that quite enough till you have time to judge . but Balak say nothing under twenty , consider all the beer , and some of it country brewer ' - " 
 " your advice be well than Balak 's ; I agree with you on that point ; and I will take it in preference . here be ten pound . " he look rather take aback , but could not well get out of it . Balak smile grimly at he . 
 " if what you tell I prove really valuable , I will give you a cheque for another ninety ere long , and the residue hereafter : but not another farthing , if you keep I in this suspense . do I look likely to cheat people of your class ? " 
 " no , Miss , we hop not ; nor of any other class , I dare say . still there be so many rogue in the world - " 
 " you have take my money ; speak on . " 
 what they tell I at wearisome length , and with puzzle divergence , and quantity of self - praise , need not occupy many line . they have trace the Jelly - corse , as they call Della Croce , from Somers Town to Lisson Grove , where they stay but a very short time , Lepardo Della Croce , under some fictitious name , give lesson in French , spanish , and italian , at school in Portland Town and St. John 's Wood . but he only seem to play with his work , though he never break any engagement to which he really pledge himself . he be always reserve and silent , accept no invitation , and gather his real subsistence by night at chess - club and billiard - room , where his skill be unequalle . his only friend be italian refugee , his only diversion the vivisection of animal . it must have be about this time that he see the newspaper paragraph , and do what he do to I . then he change his name again , and live awhile in Kensington ; he have be in London year before , and seem to know it well . here a nobleman , whom he have teach some new device at billiard , take he up and introduce he to a high class of pupil , and obtain he some back - door palace appointment . he dub himself " Professor , " and start as Dr. Ross . but still he miss the excitement and change of his once adventurous life , and several time he break loose , and leave his household , for week and month together . then the two lovely child , whom all admire but none be allow to notice , be attend wherever they go , by a dark - browed italian woman . suddenly they all leave Kensington , and go to live at Ball 's Pond ; the reason be some threaten exposure of the Professor 's cat - skin propensity . his love of vivisection have become the master - passion , and he would gratify it at all hazard . there be to some nature a strange fascination in the horrible cruelty perpetrate under the name of science . through its influence he even relax his strict reserve a little , and form the acquaintance of a gentleman connect with the college at Camden Town ; to which suburb after a while he remove , because he find it impossible to pursue his inhuman research under his own roof comfortably . here , by mean of his new ally , who could not help admire his infinitely superior skill , he be appoint lecturer at several school for young lady , where smattering of science be deal in . and now he be highly respect by people who do not know he , and idolise by young lady too clever to care for pet parson . of course he become conceited ; for his nature be but a shallow one , and his cunning , though sharp and poisonous , have no solid barb at the end . so he sneer , and grimace , and snigger , and before an ignorant audience make learn man stammer and stutter , amazed at his bold assumption , and too honest and large of mind to suspect they , at short notice . 
 but the skill of his hand be genuine , and his power of sight most wonderful . I have since be tell - though I do not believe it possible - that he once withdraw and bottle nearly half the lung of a dog , tubercular after distemper , while the poor sufferer still gasp on , and try to lick his face . oh that I be a man ! how can I hear such thing and not swear ? all animal , except one , hat he by instinct . the only one , not sagacious enough to know he , be his fellow - man . man , or at any rate woman , think he a handsome , lively , playful , and brilliant being . and yet , upon the honour of a lady I declare - let those who know nothing of honour despise it as an after - thought - that when he first enter my room , in his graceful and elegant way , there run through I such a shudder as first turn the leaf towards autumn , such a chill of the spinal marrow as make the aura of epilepsy . 
 Darling Judy hat he from every bristle of his body , not only through instinct , but for certain excellent reason . the monster 's most intimate friend be a gallant polish patriot , who have sacrifice all for his country , and live here in dignified poverty . this gentleman and his wife could only afford one luxury ; and that , by deny themselves many a little comfort . they have the fine dog in London , one who have save his master 's life from the squat - nosed son of the Czar . this glorious fellow , of maltese family , be the father of my Giudice - whom in his puppy day the polish exile give to Conrad and pretty girl Isola . Slowski , now an ancient dog , have a wen behind his shoulder , which grow and grow until the Professor could scarcely keep his hand from it . but he know that any operation , in so severe a case , be nearly sure to kill a dog so old and weather - beat . the owner too know this , and would not have it meddle with . Lepardo Della Croce swear at last that he would taste no food until he have trace the root of that wen . Judy , then a pretty pup , gambol into the room and see his poor papa - but I will not describe what a dog can not even bear to think of . poor Slowski die that night , and the Pole knock down the survive brute , who shoot he next day upon Hampstead Heath . however , the gentleman slowly recover ; but during his illness the frenzied wife overstep the bound of honour - accord to their idea ; she take advantage of Cora , in the absence of Lepardo , and learn some of his previous crime , by practise on the poor woman 's superstition . then she find , through the firm of Green , Vowler , and Green , that my Uncle be still alive , trace out the history of the atrocious deed , and write the letter which have bring I to London . soon afterwards , when her husband recover , she be sorry for what she have do , and open her lip on the subject no more ; at least in this country , which they soon forsook for America . 
 in this brief epitome , I have tell , for the purpose of save trouble , a great deal more than I learn at the time , a great deal more than Balaam and Balak would have find out in a twelvemonth . but it make no difference : for my conclusion and action be just the same as they would have be , if I have know all the above . " and so you see , Miss " - be Balaam 's peroration - " we have have a downy cove to deal with , for all his furious temper . "

 Balaam and Balak look very blank , and declare it would take they a week to write out half they have tell I . this objection I soon remove , by offer to make an abstract of it , which I could do from memory , and then let they read and sign it . by this time they be both afflict with thirst , which I send they away to quench , while I draw up a rough deposition . but first I call darle Idols , and tell she that now I have evidence which would satisfy even a sceptical father .

I hop he might live for year , but I fear he might die to - morrow ; so hang over every one 's mind that fatal third stroke of paralysis . her own entreaty and coaxing tell much upon my resolution ; if none could resist she when happy , who could withstand her distress ? so Balaam and Balak be order most strictly to watch that demon 's abode , and at any risk give he in charge if he make attempt at departure . to ensure due vigilance , I reclaim the 90 l.   cheque , and give one payable three day afterwards . they grumble and do not like it ; but in the course of all my rough usage , I have learn one great maxim - never trust , beyond the length of a cork , any man who be slave to the bottle . 
 CHAPTER V. 
 Eager as Isola be to see her true father at last , she press I strongly to call at her brother 's lodging on our way to Paddington , and take he with we if possible ; or at any rate learn where he be , and how long he would be absent . but I refuse to do anything of the kind . though not half so proud as of old , I could not quite stoop to that . " you know , dear , " she continue , " Conny will think it unfair of I to get such a start of he with the real good papa ; and it would be so much nice to have he there to help . and I be terribly frightened , though of course you can not   understand it . " 
 " Isola , no more nonsense . for your sake , and my poor Uncle 's , I would do anything honest and proper : but neither can I travel with your brother Conrad , nor can I go near his lodging . I be not quite reduce to that , however I be trample on . " 
 " but , darling , they need not see you . and you know he have make some wonderful mistake . " 
 of course I know it , and tell myself so fifty time in a minute ; but it be a likely thing that I would tell his sister so . 
 " he have , indeed , a very grave mistake , if he ever think I will forgive he . no mistake ever make by man can be plead for what he have do . even if he believe , by some excess of absurdity , that my father have murder his , instead of his murder mine   which be much near the mark , would even that justify his rudeness , low rudeness , and personal violence to a lady ? what he do I never tell you ; and he , I should hope , be too much ashamed to speak of it : why he actually push I ; thrust I , Clara Vaughan , away from he , till I almost fall on the floor ! " 
 " oh , Donna , how your eye flash ! and you call I excitable ! let I put your hair back . there now , give I a kiss . I be so sorry for Conny . he love you with all his heart , and you look as if you could kill he . but no doubt the new good papa will put every thing to right . " 
 " will he indeed ? let we go and see . " 
 we get to Paddington just in time to catch the two o'clock train , have telegraph first to my Uncle that I be come to take his advice , before do anything more . this be true , so far as it go , and as much of the truth as I then dare to administer . this message be send , not for the sake of find the carriage at Gloucester , but in order to break the suddenness of our arrival . through all my joy I dread what be to come , and know not how to manage it . idol talk fast enough all the way down the line . as yet she have see scarcely anything of our quiet , rich english scenery ; and although the great Western exhibit it rather flatly , some part there be , below Swindon , which fill the mind with content . but our mind could not be so fill , be full of excitement already . near Stroud poor Idols be in the great ecstasy , and expect I to know the owner of every pretty meadow . 
 but after we enter my Uncle 's carriage - or mine , I suppose , it should now be call - dear Isola fall away into the deep silence . she store her wonder inwardly , nor show the sweet depth of her eye , until she spring out at the foot of the old stone step , tread by so many hundred of her ancestor . then she look up at the long gray house , with the dusk of July around it , and bat of three variety flit about the gable ; and I see beneath her dark eye - lash the tremulous light of a tear . 
 after lead my sweet new cousin - whom everybody star at , and who fear to look at the pavement - to my own snug quarter , I leave she there under kind Mrs. Fletcher 's charge , and run to my Uncle 's favourite room . already my breath be short , and my heart up and down with excitement , and I have but the presence of mind to know that I be sure to make a mistake of it . I see a great change in he , even since the Monday ; but he be the first to speak . 
 " my dear child , kiss I again . you be nearly as tall as I be , since my upright way have depart . from the moment you go away , I have do nothing but miss you , every hour and every minute ; and last night I sleep never a single wink . let we give it up , my darling . God have send you to I to make up for both daughter and son . " 
 " well , Uncle , that be all very fine , but I doubt it strongly . " I be force to be flippant a little , for fear of break down . " it be my firm belief that proud Clara will still have to wash at the pump . " 
 he know what I mean ; it be an old tale , in our neighbourhood , of a nobleman 's second wife who would not allow her step - child even the use of a yellow basin . 
 " what ! do you mean to say " - and he begin to tremble exceedingly - " that you have find any trace , any clue even , to my poor darling ? "

 " Dear Uncle , we must not be certain yet . I mean that I have find something , or fancy I have find something , which - which - I mean if properly follow up - may lead in time - but you know how sanguine I be . "

 " alive , I think , dear Uncle ; and I hope to find they soon , if you will calmly advise I . " 
 " you have find they . no more fencing . I know it by your eye . all the truth this moment , unless you wish to kill I . " 
 he stand up as if to seize I , for I have withdraw from his grasp , but his poor leg would not carry he ; so I be oblige to seize he instead . he fall sideways on a chair , and vainly try to speak ; but his eye never falter from mine . 
 " Dearest Uncle , I tell you the truth . of course I can not be certain yet , and it will not   do to make a mistake ; and so I want more evidence . " 
 " I want no more . only let I see they . " he speak very slowly , and the muscle of his face twitch at every word . 
 " now , keep your mind calm and clear , to help I , my dear Uncle ; for I know not what to do . have you anything , any token at all , of their beloved mother ? " 
 my object be to divert his mind , for I see the approach of coma , and now tremble more than he do .

 here his chin fall on his breast , and he could not lift the key , but his eye still shine with intelligence , and follow I everywhere . ribbon and all I take the key , and ring the bell for Jane , the most careful and kind of nurse . I order she , in a whisper , to give my Uncle a glass of very strong brandy and water , if she could get he to swallow it ; and away I run upstairs , hop to relieve he . then suddenly it strike I that I have no right to open that box , without the presence of a competent witness . I know at once what box it be , from the constant anxiety my poor Uncle have show about it . who have such right to be my witness as his darling daughter ? so back I fly to my own room , and drag the bewildered Isola down the broad corridor . the poor little thing be frightened so that she could hardly breathe . I have no especial object in open that old box , at that particular moment , much as I have often long to know what its content be . my presence of mind be lose , and all I could think of be , that I might find something there to break that awful suspension of life , so likely to end in death . 
 the box be in a panel closet by the head of my Uncle 's bed . when I hand idol the light to hold , she take it as if in a dream ; her cheek be as white and transparent as the wax , and she hold the candle so that a hot flake splash on my neck . the lock of the long box turn most easily , and the hinge move without creak : most likely it have be pore over every day , for many year . the lid be arch and hollow , with strap of fade web inside it . 
 in beautiful order , so fair that I hardly dare to touch they , lie the clothe and trinket , the letter and little relic , the glove and pocket - handkerchief , the fairy slipper , the wedding - dress , the coquettish veil , and saucy hat of the dead . I be not over sensitive , thank God , or I should not be live now ; but the sight of those thing upset I more than any distress of my own . the small parcel of silver paper , screw at the end and pin in the middle , the pin put stupidly as man always put they , the light gay dress make for some sweet figure , fold with such care , and yet quite out of the plait , and label with the date when last the dear one wear they , even a wither fern - wreath and a sprig of shrivel myrtle - I could not thrust my commonplace hand into these holy treasure ; if I could I should never deserve to be myself so remember . but one thing strike I , as thought profane always strike we crookedly ; if the poor lady could have be weep to life again , how much well would she have find all her thing arrange , than she have ever keep they ! that be to say if she resemble she wonder and cry daughter , who kneel down and want to kiss every article in the box . her little white hand be as busy as mouse among they ; and long - draw sob be tumble with interjection . 
 " now , my dear idol , you must not disturb these thing . your father will be so vexed . " 
 would he though ? - say I to myself - not if he know whose hand it be that do it . she pay no attention to I . 
 " now just put back that silver knife , with the bit of peach - skin upon it : and leave the stone as it be . "

 " now , you foolish child , you be really too bad . " and with that I give she a little push . in her heedless way , she fall almost into the box , and her light form lie amongst her mother 's dress . a sudden thought flash across I . 
 " Isola , off with that nasty dark frock ! " 
 " Nasty , indeed , Clara ! why you say this morning how very pretty it be . " 
 " what have that to do with it ? pull it off , or I will tear it . now , out with the other arm . " 
 in a moment or two , I have all her beauty gleam in white before I ; and carefully take from the box a frock of pale blue silk , I lift it over her head , and draw her dimpled arm through the sleeve ; then I fix it in front with the turquoise button , and buckle the slender zone . her blue eye look on in amazement , like violet at a snow - storm . then I lead she to the mirror , and proud as we both have always be of her beauty , the same thought strike we now . I see it in the mirror , by the toss of her point chin and the coy bend of her neck : she see it there as clearly , by the flash of my tear - bright eye . neither of we have ever see that lovely of all girl look half so lovely before . the glow of pride and beauty 's glory mantle in her cheek ; and her eye be softly beam down the avenue of lash , from clear depth of azure . I never see such eye as she have , among all our english beauty . some perhaps be as fine of colour , and as liquid , though not so lustrous : but the exquisite arch of the upper lid , and the rich short fringe of the low , cast a tremulous light and shade , which dull Anglo - saxon feel not . like moonbeam play through a mantled bridge . 
 the dress fit she exactly . it have be make for a slender , buoyant figure , as graceful and pure as a snow - wreath , yet full of warm motion and richness . indeed , I must confess , that , although correct enough for the time and clime of the owner , it show too much of the lift snow for our conception of maidenhood : so I draw a gauzy scarf - perhaps a true fazoletto - over the velvet slope of the shoulder , and imprison it in the valley . this be nicely arrange , I hang her chalcedony charm from her neck , and fasten it to her waist - band . then I catch up her cluster hair , nearly as thick and long as my own , after the corsican fashion , snoode it close in ripple with a pink and white - striped mandile , and tell she to love herself in the glass , while I run off to the hot - house for a truss of Stephanotis . this , with a glossy sprig of Gardenia leave to back it , I fasten cleverly into the clear mandile , on the curve of her elegant head , and my darling be complete . then I kiss her sweet lip , and admire she , more than she admire herself . 
 " Clara , it do not matter how much trouble you take ; you can not   make I look a quarter so well as you do . " 
 " not quite so tall , my darling , nor anything like so naughty ; but a thousand time more lovely . " 
 " well , I wish I could think so . I be always long to change with you . " 
 " do not   talk nonsense , my pretty ; if I be a man I should die for you . now I glory in you as a Vaughan . come along . " 
 I lead she through the gallery and to the door of her father 's room , before she have time to think . she do not know but what I be take she back to my own room , along another passage . at the sick man 's door I leave she , while I go in to see how much might be safely venture . 
 my Uncle be lean back in his deep reclining chair , with his weak eye fix most eagerly on the door . in vain he strove to hide his disappointment , and to look at I with gratitude . the wandering mind too plainly hop for something dearer than a brother 's child . 
 dismiss Jane through the other room , that she might not encounter Isola , I sit down to examine he . the brandy and water have rally his vital power , but make he hot and feverish . he kiss my hand to atone for some sharp and impatient expression , and I see that the moment be favourable . 
 " Uncle dear , what will you say to I ? I have bring you another new visitor , the lovely girl in London . you know she well by name . you have often long to see my sweet darling Isola . and she want to see you so much . only you must promise I one thing honourably . be gay and sprightly with she ; she be timid in this old house . " 
 " my dear , I can not   see she to - night . you do not   mean that of course . give she my good apology . you say she be very sweet - temper ; I be sure she will excuse I . " 
 " if she would , I will not . nor would you excuse she , if you know whom she resemble . "

 " yes , and here be the key . I find a portrait of a lady " - I have not show this to my cousin - " very like beautiful Isola . " 
 he begin to tremble again , so I think the quick the well . place the lamp - shade so that a dim light fall on the door , I run out to fetch his daughter . 
 " now , do not   be a baby , Isola . remember how ill he be . keep as much in the shadow as possible ; and if he should guess who you be , pretend not to care a bit for he . " 
 " I will try my very good , Clara . but I do not   think I can do that . " 
 she shake so much that I be oblige to support she , as she have support I that evening when first we meet . Stiffly I bring she in , and begin to introduce she , hold she back all the time . 
 " Uncle Edgar , this be my dear friend , of whom you have hear so often , Miss Isola " - Ross I could not say . " why , Uncle - why , Idols , darling - " 
 it be all in vain ; I might as well have spar my device . from the moment she cross the threshold , his eye have be leap towards she . the paralysed man bound forward , as if with galvanic life . his daughter meet he as wildly . " my Lily , my Lily , " be all he could sob , " my own Lily come from the grave ! " with a father 's strength he clasp she , and her dark lock be shower with silver . as for tear - but I leave they together when I have see both safe on the sofa . 
 CHAPTER VI . 
 to our surprise and delight , the genuine Papa , instead of be bad the next day , look more like himself than he have do at any time since the fever . but in spite of add importance , and the sense of parental dignity , he sit hand in hand with his beautiful daughter by the hour together , play with her cheek and hair , as little girl do with dolly . and all the time he be talk to she about her darling mother , and make she answer he in Italian , and make she kiss he every other minute ; and find out a thousand time , as a novelty every time , that she be the very image and model of her mother , and yet he be not sure that her smile be quite so sweet ; then to make up for depreciation he need must kiss she again , and say , yes , he think it be , though it be quite impossible for any other to be so - and thus they go on , till I think there never would be an end of it ; albeit I do my utmost to keep away from they both . 
 know that I be in their way , and feel rather out of spirit , I go my old accustomed round of place , sacred in my memory to a certain father and mother of my own . how long I weep at their simple graf , how I kneel to their God and mine , thank he from my desolate heart for the light now shed upon I , and how I pray that they might both be look down on I now and crave heavenly guidance for I through the peril yet to come - these , and the rest of my doing there , can not well be tell except to the ear of orphan . the cloud of an overcast existence seem to be open rapidly , and though they could never disclose my sun and moon again , some happiness it be to know even how those have set . and more than all , the foul aspersion upon my father 's memory , which all the while I scorn it so , have lie heavily on my thought , this be now prove liar 's spittle , and my sweet darling father have offend not even a villain . a thousand time I implore his pardon for the splash have ever descend upon the hem of my garment , though shake off straight - way with loathing . 
 in the midst of my dreamy thought , and while I sit between the two low headstone , upon the very spot where I hope my own head may lie , the tremulous beauty of the Golden Thuja , which I have plant there , be push aside too carelessly , and something far more beautiful plant itself in front . it be my cousin Lily . I have be strictly forbid ever to call she " Isola , " or even " idol , " again , as savour of the evil one . Lily Vaughan be beam with young delight and happiness : the fresh west country air , sweet from the tropic gulf - stream , have crown the April of her cheek with a June of rose . 
 " oh , Donna , I be so glad I have find you at last . what make you run away from I and my Papa ? I have lose my way all over the world . what a lovely world it be , Donna ! " 
 " do not   call I that name here . do you not see where you stand ? " 
 she glance at the headstone engrave with initial and date , and at once understand it all . for a long time she be silent , a long time I mean for she ; and her soft eye glisten at once with awe and pity . at last , she creep close to I , look at the ground , and whisper with a deep sigh : 
 " how you must hate I , Clara . " 
 " hate you , my darling ! what for ? " 
 " oh , because I have get such a dear papa , and you have none at all . and much bad than that , because - because - oh , I do not   know how to tell you . " 
 " tell I all you mean . let there be no misunderstanding between we . " 
 " because my mother and my father seem somehow to have kill - though I be sure they would rather have kill themselves - your poor papa and mamma . " and she lean on my mother 's headstone , and sob till I fear for her heart . 
 I put my arm around her waist , draw she towards I , and sit on my father 's grave , with his niece upon my lap . 
 " Dearest , I could not be the child of those who sleep beneath we , if it be in my nature now to feel as you imagine . year ago , I might have do so ; though I hope not even then . orphan as I be and helpless , already I perceive that I have not live for nothing . my father , I believe , my mother , I be sure , would have lay down life with pleasure to see I lead from wayward childhood even to what I be . oh , Lily , you can not   think how they love I . " and at the tender memory , come tear , the voice of silence . 
 Lily say not a word , but gather and plait a wreath of flower , wherewith , as in a nuptial tie , she bind the white headstone together - anything so as not to disturb I just then . even that trifle , a graceful idea bear of her southern origin , even that for the moment touch I deeply . time there be when our soul seem to have take hot bath in the spring of memory , and every pore of they be open . 
 " Darling Lily , come - how proud they would have be of you - come and kiss I in this presence , and promise that , whatever happen , none shall ever thrust cold hand between your heart and mine . that we will bear , and trust , and love ; nor , if a shadow steal between we , blink it till the substance follow , but be frank and open - the very breath of friendship - and when doubt begin to grow , for the devil be sure to sow it , have it pluck away at once , each by the other 's hand . kiss I , dear ; your weakness be that you be not so outspoken as I be . never let I vex you , without know it . " 
 the innocent creature kiss I , and promise solemnly . 
 " oh , Clara , " she cry , " how on earth do you find it out ? sometimes you have vex I dreadfully , for you do not   care much what you say ; but I always think it be my fault , and I never tell you of it . but it never make I love you a single bit the less . " 
 " yes , it do for the moment , though you may soon have forgive it . but a love which be always undergo forgiveness , be like glass steep in water , you may cut it in two with a pair of common scissor . " 
 " well , I should like to see the scissor that would cut I away from you . I will have a great piece off your hair , Clara , if you talk such nonsense . now come ; my father want you . " 
 " have you tell he ? " 
 " yes , everything about dear Conny and you ; and he say you be a noble girl , but uncommonly thick - headed about your own concern , though as quick as lightning for other . now , I will not   have you look so pale ; let we run and get some colour . see , I will get first to that tree . " 
 " will you indeed ? " I win the race by a yard , and be glad that the exercise make excuse for the quick rise of my bosom . after all that have happen , I would not have her imagine that I still care for her brother . like a girl all over , she say not another word , determine that I should begin it . 
 " let we walk fast , Lily , if my Uncle wish to see I . " 
 " no , there be plenty of time . it will do he good to sleep a little . " 
 " oh , then it be nothing important . I rather fear that it might be . " 
 " do not   be at all afraid , darling . he want to show you how nicely he make the Chalcedony Spalla that use to be round my neck . he make it for my mother , in remembrance of something . " 
 " oh , nothing more than that . I think you speak of something - at least you seem to imply - " 
 " nothing that you need blush about , nor stammer either , proud Donna . you know you prove to I yesterday , when we be in the cab , that you do not care for Conny any more than you do for a flake of London soot , which happen to come in at the window , and fall upon your glove . and you be kind enough to compare he to that individual smut . " 
 " oh , Judy , Judy , " I cry , as the dog come bound to meet we - " darling Judy , you love Clara , if nobody else have sense enough . " 
 and half an hour ago , Lily and I in dramatic language , vow eternal affection ! 
 " oh , Clara , darle Clara , do not   you know that I be in fun ? I think you be so clever . and now to see you sob over that great muff of a dog ! Judy , I hate you , get out of the way " - the judicious would not stir - " take your great hulking paw from cousin Clara 's neck . there then , make the most of that ! oh , I have hurt my hand so , and he be only wag his tail . but I be so delighted , my own pet , that you love poor Conny still . " 
 " and pray , who say I do ? " 
 " nobody , only I . all dear Papa say be this , that there be a great mistake , and he soon perceive what it be ; and I ask he to take my opinion about it , because I be a senior sophist . and he pretend not to know what a senior sophist be . and I tell he it be my degree , not from that man , you know , but fairly earn at the College ; though they do have the impudence to say that the Professors be go to pluck I , until I give they a smile . " 
 " true enough , no doubt . but I know all that long ago . what more do my Uncle say ? " 
 " that he would tell you his opinion , but he would rather not talk about it to I . and he could not bear I to go out , for fear I should be steal again . and I do believe he have have I watch all the way . here I come , Pappy ; large as life you see , and three time as natural . " 
 " yes , my own treasure , three time as natural to I , as my life have be without you . but wheel I indoor , young maiden . no other man in the world have such a pair of horse . I want to talk to Clara , in my own room alone . lily , go to Mrs. Fletcher , I can not   have you rove about so . " lily obey he instantly . 
 " wait one minute , Uncle dear ; I want to go and fetch something . " 
 I run to my own room , and find the deed of gift , which have not be return to the lawyer . this I take to his study and place it in his hand . 
 " what be the matter , Clara ? have you turn conveyancer , and detect some informality ? " 
 " no , dear Uncle . but I want you to cancel this . I can not allow you so to rob your child . " 
 I will not say what he call I in his surprise and delight . it seem to I quite uncalled for ; I have only do what my conscience tell I be just . but as for accept my offer - he would not hear of it twice . " darling , it would be wrong . it would be downright robbery ; and no plea whatever for it , on the score of paternal duty . you be the proper heir , the child of the eld son , the true representative of our ancient family . all the rest be a quibble and quirk , of which , even without your countless benefit , I never intend to take advantage . and my child be , by the mother 's side , of a family old even than our - so far as that nonsense go - and be heir to wealth compare to which - if it only be rightly work - these Vaughan estate be nothing . all I ask you be to do a thing which I be sure you would do without ask - to assist they , if what I have leave they be spend before they prove their claim . here be a letter to Count Gaffori ; that excellent man be still alive ; and here be the certificate , and my own brief deposition , which I have beg a neighbouring magistrate to come to - day and attest ; here be my Lily 's Spalla , and perhaps other relic be in my son 's possession . lastly , here be two more letter , one to my old friend Peter Green , who have now much influence in that part of Corsica , the other to James McGregor , once my messmate at Lincoln 's Inn , now an acute and rise Counsel , and a lead authority upon municipal law . take all these , my darling , if you will so far oblige I ; for I fear my lovely daughter - be not   she lovely , Clara ? " 
 " the lovely girl in all the world ; and what be far more important , the sweetest , and the good . " 
 " yes , if you have search the kingdom , you could not have bring I such another love . but ah ! you should have see her mother ! however , I fear the sweet pet be a little careless and random , as her father use to be . at any rate , I prefer entrust this great budget to your brave and honest hand ; at least until my son come here to claim it . the deposition you shall have , when attest . " 
 " but , Uncle , surely you have well keep it all yourself . no fear of Mrs. Daldy now . " 
 " no , my darling ; but these thing must not be bury with I . " 
 there be something in his eye which make I start with terror . but he smile so sweetly that my terror flee . 
 " and now , my child , about yourself . though you have find I another daughter , I look upon you as the eld ; and I venture to speak to you , as a father would . be it as my Lily tell I ? be it true - God grant it may be - that you love my son , my Lily 's son , Henry Conrad ? why do not   you answer I , darling ? tell the truth like a real Vaughan . surely you be not ashamed of he . " and he lay his hand on my head . my tear fall fast ; and my heart be in a temp . 
 " yes , Uncle , " at last I answer , frighten for his suspense , and look he full in the face , " yes , Uncle , I do - I mean at least I do - love he very much at one time . " 
 " with all your heart , as we Vaughans love ; with all your heart , poor darling ? " 
 " yes , Uncle , " I sob , in bitter humiliation ; " none of my heart be leave I . " 
 " thank God ! what bl news for his mother ! my Harry be the happy fellow alive . " 
 " but , Uncle , he do not think so , he - he - do not   perceive his blessedness . " a flash of my old self - irony come even through my anguish . 
 " oh , I have hear all that . but surely you know the absurd mistake he make . " 
 " indeed , I can not guess it . be it my place to do that ? " 
 " of course it be ; when you be in the light , and he be all in the dark . whom do that kidnapper believe himself to have murder ? " 
 " you , Uncle , of course . " 
 " and whose child then do he suppose you to be ; if he hear of your existence , as he be sure to have do ? " 
 " merciful God , I see it all ! and how bitterly I have wrong he , my own noble Conrad ! " 
 my poor weak Uncle have to manage I , all by himself , in my terrible hysteric . frighten as he be , for he never before have to deal in that way with a nature resemble mine , he would not even ring for help , lest I should betray my secret to other ear than his own . when at last I come to myself , he kiss I tenderly , and say : 
 " my poor dear child , remember - when you may be glad to think of it - that whether I see my noble boy or not , I shall die now in perfect happiness . noble he must be , or Clara could not love he . it would have be the pet scheme of my heart , if I could have have a voice in it . and here it be do without I ! how often have I long and yearn that he could only see you , as you wait day and night by my pestilential bed , that he could only know the tale of your trouble and devotion . at my death , the generation so visit from heaven expire ; and you three darling start anew , with all thing in your favour . now mind that the good old Signor 's direction be comply with , and that Harry , if he live here , abandon the corsican property to his sister Lily . promise I this , my Clara . " 
 " of course I will , dear Uncle - I mean , so far as my influence go . and he will then be bind to do so under the deed - poll , if I understand you aright . but perhaps he have quite forget I now . " 
 " of course he think himself bind to avoid you . but I have write to set he right , and to bring he as soon as possible . and now about - about that horrible - " 
 " ah , yes . if I have the right , I would even let he go . my feeling have change from fierce hatred to utter contempt . and surely his vengeance be satisfied now . " 
 " no , Clara . it will flame more wildly than ever the moment he learn his mistake , and my final triumph over he . have he any idea where our Lily be ? " 
 " as yet , he can have none . if old Cora go to Albert Street last evening , she would learn nothing from Mrs. Shelfer , I take care of that , except that Lily have be there , and be go again . the old woman do not speak English enough to attempt to cross - examine . she love poor Lily , I know , but will be satisfied with the belief that the child have go to her brother 's . and as for that monster , even if he relent , he will be too proud to inquire . " 
 " what have my poor child do , that the brute turn she out , and strike she ? " 
 " nothing , I believe , beyond defend her brother Conrad , as she always do . I suppose I may call he ' Conrad , ' Uncle ? " 
 " yes , my dear , it be his true name , choose by his mother . where be you go so hastily ? " 
 " to London at once . for your sake , Uncle dear , I must not think of spar he . I must have he in custody to - night . I would have avoid it , if I could for a thousand reason ; but there be no alternative . " 
 " yes there be . in two day I shall be beyond his reach . do not   ask I what I mean . to - day be Thursday . promise only to let he go free till Saturday . " 
 " I will . but I must go to London . I can not rest quiet here . " 
 my Uncle 's face brighten beautifully . and he take my hand in his . 
 " I know what you mean , my darling . you intend to discover my Harry , for fear of any mishap . I will let you go , dear ; though the house seem empty without you , its truthful and graceful mistress . but you must not go alone . it be not right for a beautiful girl , however self - possess and dignified , especially one of your station , to rove about unattended . " 
 " only one man ever insult I , Uncle , I mean in a serious way , and he never do it again . " 
 " it do not matter . the example be bad , and all man be not gentleman . Mrs. Fletcher shall go with you , and our pretty Lily keep house . but I have an especial reason , and a most powerful one , for wish that you should be here . do not   go till to - morrow , my darling ; I be so well to - day , and I must see you once at your own table , with my daughter and I for your guest . " 
 " oh , Uncle , I hope so a thousand time . I will stop till the morning , if you have set your heart upon it . " 
 " I have indeed . you may go in the morning by the first train , and be back to - morrow night . will you promise ? " 
 though I could not understand his motive , and he be pleased to conceal it , I promise all he ask . then I tell he all the story of Conrad and the accident , how he save my mother 's life and mine , with the courage and skill of a true - bear mountaineer . my Uncle be move to tear , not only at the gallantry of his son , but also by the joy of discover that all the obligation lay not upon one side . I also weep at find that Lily have never hear of it . Conrad 's lofty nature scorn to narrate its own achievement . when , after that adventure , he discover who we be , he avoid we because he believe that his father have slay mine . it be not till a later date , when he become of age - as the Corsicans reckon manhood     - that lepardo Della Croce tell he all he know of his history , dwelt on the foul shame work to the Della Croce by his bigamist father , and try in vain to force on he the awful oath of Vendetta . the youth have too much english blood in his heart to accept the black inheritance . Thenceforth he could not bear the sight of the man who have kill , as they both suppose , his father , although , in his wrath for his mother 's wrong and his own , he would not resent the deed . what marvel then that he spurn I , and be madden with himself , at find that he , the illegitimate , be in love with I , his legitimate sister ? but now , we be only half - cousin , and nature have never mislead we . 
     i.e. the age of twenty . 
 all that evening , my Uncle be in the most glorious spirit , and I be not sure that Lily and I be very far behind he . he play we all sort of boyish trick , and we make reprisal with girlish one , till Lily 's joyous laughter rang halfway clown the corridor . I have dress she with especial care , and she do look such a love ! but it be all too sudden , and far too sweet to last . my Uncle indeed seem quite beside himself , more gladsome than nature allow we to be with impunity . then the vein dry all of a sudden , and the mind flow the opposite way . he make his beautiful daughter , who , though not much of a sophist , have a soul that thrill to music , he make she play the soft Corsican air , that seem to weep as they breathe , and which she have learn from old Cora . he know they all ; how well he know they , his face turn from the light betray . the depth of melodious sadness , the touch of some nervine chord , which know not its own existence , and start to be know and appreciate , as might an unconscious poet , and more than all the trembling spread of the feeler of the heart , these be the proof of nature 's presence in music or in poetry . 
 then he beg I to play some of the sweet and simple melody of Wales . these he declare , and I have already perceive it , these be bear of the self - same spirit , though not so highly intensify , as the corsican romance . 
 finally , he tell we many a move tale of his Lily ; tale a man be loth to tell to those with whom he expect to live . how she be love , and how she seem to love everybody , and pretty answer she make to those who praise her beauty , and more than word or kiss , the love thing she do , the elegance of self - denial , and the innocence of merit . 
 that night , that memorable night , we stay up more than two hour over his proper time for go to bed . he seem so sad to part , that I could not bear to hurry he . one thing he tell I which I be glad to hear . 
 " Clara , darling , I have take a liberty with your house . this afternoon , I write by the London post , for Annie Franks to come back again to - morrow , if she will , as an especial favour to I . " 
 I be rather surprised ; but answer he warmly , and in all truth : 
 " Dear Uncle , you know that I love she ; and I can not see too much of the few whom I really love . " 
 then , as I be to start at six o'clock in the morning , he wish I " good bye , " in a solemn manner , which seem to I quite uncalled for . he draw my young face to his own , so mark by sorrow and illness , look into my eye as if I be to remember something , then hold I in his trembling embrace , and kiss I long and fondly . 
 " God in heaven bless you , darling , for all you have do to I and mine . " 
 " mine , you should say , dear Uncle . I count they now my own . " 
 his daughter take he away , with her white arm throw around he . for now she sleep in the closet next to his room , where I have so long be quarter . 
 CHAPTER VII . 
 in the early morning , I be off for London , take Mrs. Fletcher with I , much against my will , because she seem to cumber I both in thought and action . between the door and the avenue , I look from the open carriage - I hate to be shut up in summer - at the dear old house . Lily have get up to breakfast with I , in spite of my prohibition ; and she be go with we as far as the lodge , to have a nice walk back . to my great surprise I see my poor Uncle , stand at his open window , wrap in a dressing - gown . he kiss his hand and wave I his last farewell . I leap on the seat to reply , and then scold he with my glove . half in play and half in sorrow , he mock my lively gesture , and the morning breeze lift his silver hair , as he waft I the last kiss . I tell Lily to scold he well , with my very good love , and she ask I in the most ladylike manner , if I see any green in her eye . the girl have pick up a great deal of slang among the fair collegian . Mrs. Fletcher look sadly shocked ; so I say , to reassure she : " you know , Mrs. Fletcher , we must make allowance for young lady who come from college . " 
 " to be sure , Miss Vaughan , to be sure we must , " she reply with her most sagacious air : and at Gloucester she whisper to the coachman , " John , the villain that steal Miss Lily send she to Oxford , in a young gentleman 's clothe , and she take a very high degree : but do not   say a word about it . " " not by any mean , ma'am , " answer John , with a grin . nevertheless , it find its way over the house , and the result be that all the girl come to Lily about their sweetheart . 
 I mention this trifling incident only to show how little I think that I then see the last of my Uncle . 
 at Paddington we meet Annie Franks take her ticket for Gloucester , and look most bright and blooming , with a grand pocket in her cloak , make to hold a three - volume novel . I have only time for a few word with she , in which I commend my Uncle to her especial attention , as she have ten time my cousin 's experience . then I go with she to the down - platform , and see she get into the carriage , and give she the last of my sandwich , while a cruel guard make she turn out her new pocket , insist that she must have a little dog conceal there . I laugh at the poor little dear , as crimson with mortification she show before all the gentleman the triple fluted bulk , and the guard read out , more in amazement than rudeness , " Sir Ingomar of the Red Hand ; or , the Knight of St. Valentine , and the Paynim Lady . " the gentleman be gentleman , and try very hard not to smile ; but the way the guard scratch his head be a great deal too much for they . " Dog 's ear , anyhow , " cry he , try to escape with a joke . I draw she out of the carriage , with tear in her soft gray eye , and put she into another , where Sir Ingomar be unknown , and might spur on at pleasure . then the smile return to her shy and innocent face , and she put her head to the window , and whisper gently to I : 
 " any strawberry leave , dear ? " 
 " I should think so , Annie . the good of they all , the British Queens , be just come in . and such a crop of grape ! " 
 Annie 's conception of perfect bliss be to sit upon a shady bank , " the breeze just fan her delicate cheek , " with a cabbage - leaf full of strawberry by she , and a cut - and - thrust novel upon her lap . off she go with a lovely smile , foresee all these delight . 
 from Paddington we drive straightway to the lodging of Conrad Vaughan . as we jolt along the new - road , which always have more hole in it than any other street in London , I lose my wit in a tumult of thick tempestuous thought . what would Conny say to see I , I the haughty Clara , come all impatiently even in quest of he ? would it not have be far well , far more like an english maiden , to wait , and wait , and wear the soul out , rather than to run the risk of mis - interpretation ? true , it be for his father 's sake , to save he from deadly peril , and to make his happiness complete ; but might not all have be do by messenger , as well as by I in person ? so at least might fancy those who do not know our enemy . bad of all , and cloudiest think , that fill the eye every time it come , - would he love I still ? would not the strong revulsion , that must have tear he in two , when he dash his hand on his forehead , and forget even man 's forbearance , would not , must not this have snap all the delicate root of love ? I could not tell . of man 's heart I know nothing ; but I feel that with I , a woman , such a horrible thing would create only longing to make amend . 
 " Mrs. Fletcher , how be my hair ? " 
 " Lovely , my pretty child " - she always call I so from habit when no one else be present - " you look your very good ; and I would like to see they that could - talk to I of Lilies indeed , when our Miss Clara - " 
 " no smut on my nose , Mrs. Fletcher , I hope ? I never feel sure , in London . you do not   know London , you see . " 
 " no , my pretty , as clean as a whistle , and as clear as the voice of a May - bird , every atom of you . there be no such complexion nowhere out of Gloshire or in it : and its all along of the brimstone and treacle I give you , when you be small . talk to I of lily - why I see three great butter spot , as big as the point of a needle , and I know by the make of her boot that her little toe turn over ; and what be more than that - " 
 " Mrs. Fletcher , I will not   hear a word of it . as to her little toe , I can most solemnly declare that you be wrong altogether ; for I have see her naked foot , and a lovelier one never be - " 
 " take yours out of the way , Miss. but - " 
 " but - here we be ; and you have make my cheek quite red ! I shall be ashamed to be see . " 
 however , it do not matter ; for there be no one there to see I . Conrad be go to Paris ; he have quit London quite suddenly , and there be a letter leave for his sister , which the girl forget to post , till she think it be too late . and he say very likely he should go on to Italy ; and they be not to keep the room , if they have a chance of let they , only to put away the thing he have leave , in the cupboard . so I take the letter , direct " Miss Isola Ross , " but I do not dare to open it , much as I long to do so . have enclose it in a new envelope , and post it in the near letter - box , with a heavy heart I re - enter the cab , and go on to Mrs. Shelfer 's . 
 Mrs. Shelfer be of course surprised to see I so soon again . nevertheless she be all kindness and hospitality , as usual . the residue of her little debt have be long ago release , and now I pay full rent , for I could easily afford it . in answer to my eager inquiry as to what have occur since Wednesday , the little woman say shortly : 
 " nothing at all , Miss , of any account , I thank you . only Charley throw double size , three time run , and win - " 
 " I do not   mean that , Mrs. Shelfer ; I mean , what have happen for I ? " 
 " nothing , Miss Vaughan ; no , nothing to concern a great lady like you : only such a queer lot come , and they seem to be friend of your . they be not   go from here more than half an hour ago . " 
 " tell I all about they . " 
 " they come and ring the bell , as modest as could be ; and when I go to the door , say they , ' if you please , where be Miss Clara , ma'am ? ' ' Miss Clara ! ' say I , ' a set of dressed up trollop like you , come and ask for Miss Clara ! she would Miss Clara you , pretty quick time , I doubt , if she be only here . ' ' we humbly hop no offence , ma'am , ' say the great big man , the big man as ever I see without pay , ' only we have come up from the country , ma'am . ' ' up from the country ! ' say I , ' need not   tell I that , my good giant ; any fool can see that . and if you take my advice , you will clap your hat on , and go down again , and thank God for it .
